This Order brings the provisions of the 1985 Act relating to unitary development plans into force in the Metropolitan Borough of Rotherham on 3rd July 1990.

items.title: The Agricultural Levies (Terms of Payment) Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Agricultural Levies (Terms of Payment) Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Tests) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Motor Vehicles (Tests) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Nitrate Sensitive Areas (Designation) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Nitrate Sensitive Areas (Designation) Order 1990. It changes the definition of “economic optimum” to express it in terms of the nitrogen content of inorganic nitrogen fertiliser (article 3(a)) and changes certain references to the restrictions on the application of organic nitrogen fertiliser and inorganic nitrogen fertiliser to restrictions on the application of nitrogen in such fertiliser (articles 5 and 6). Two further minor amendments have been made (articles 3(b) and 4).

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Refund of Tax) (Revocation) Order 1990
items.description: This Order revokes the Value Added Tax (Refundof Tax) Order 1973 which specified the Scottish Special Housing Association as a body entitled to a refundof value added tax on supplies to or importations by it if not made for the purpose of business.

items.title: The Housing Renovation etc. Grants (Reduction of Grant) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations provide for reduction of the amount of renovation grant and disabled facilities grant which may be paid by local housing authorities in respect of applications by owner-occupiers and tenants under Part VIII of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989.

items.title: The Water Supply (Water Quality) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations supplement Part VIA (Quality of Water) of the Water (Scotland) Act 1980 (“the 1980 Act”). They are primarily concerned with the quality of water supplied in Scotland for drinking, washing and cooking and with arrangements for the publication of information about water quality. The Regulations come into force on 1st May 1990, except regulations 13, 17, 18, 19 and 30 which come into force on 1st July 1990.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Civilian Fine Enforcement Officers) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules prescribe classes of authorities the civilian employees of which, by virtue of sections 125(2) and 136(2)(a) of the Magistrates' Courts Act 1980 (c. 43), as amended by section 65 of the Criminal Justice Act 1988 (c. 33), are empowered, when authorised in the prescribed manner, to execute, in the area in which their employing authority exercises its functions, certain categories of warrants in connection with the enforcement of fines and other sums adjudged to be paid by a summary conviction.

items.title: The Goods Vehicles (Operators' Licences) (Temporary Use in Great Britain) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amendthe Goods Vehicles (Operators' Licences) (Temporary Use in Great Britain) Regulations 1980 to implement Council Regulation (EEC) No. 4059/89 of 21 December 1989 laying down conditions under which non-resident carriers may operate national road haulage services within a Member State. They enable foreign goods vehicles (as defined in Regulation 3 of the 1980 Regulation) to be used for the carriage of goods between places in Great Britain under an authorisation granted pursuant to that Regulation. The Regulations also enable Northern Irelandgoods vehicles (as so defined) to be used for such carriage without an authorisation.

items.title: The Goods Vehicles (Community Cabotage Authorisations) (Fees) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations prescribe a fee of £5.00 payable on the issue of an authorisation granted pursuant to Council Regulation (EEC) No. 4059/89 of 21st December 1989 laying down conditions under which non-resident carriers may operate national road haulage services within a Member State.

items.title: The Spirit Drinks (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Scotland only, make provision for the administration, execution and enforcement of directly applicable Community provisions relating to the definition, description and presentation of spirit drinks set out in Council Regulation (EEC) No. 1576/89 (OJ No. L160, 12.6.1989, p.1) (the “Council Regulation”) and come into force on 5th July 1990.

items.title: The Companies (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Companies (Fees) Regulations 1988 by increasing some of the fees payable to the registrar of companies which were prescribed by those Regulations, as follows.

items.title: The Antarctic Treaty Act 1967 (Isle of Man) (Variation) Order 1990
items.description: This Order varies the Antarctic Treaty Act 1967 (Isle of Man) Order 1970 by removing the requirement for certain Orders in Council under the Antarctic Treaty Act 1967 to be approved by Tynwald before they have effect in the Isle of Man.

items.title: The Drug Trafficking Offences Act 1986 (Designated Countries and Territories) Order 1990
items.description: This Order provides that, subject to certain modifications, the Drug Trafficking Offences Act 1986 applies to an order made by a court in a designated country or territory for the purpose of recovering payments or other rewards received in connection with drug trafficking or their value, and to proceedings which have been or are to be instituted in a designated country or territory and may result in such an order being made there. It also provides, in Article 7 , that the value of any property recovered in a designated country or territory in response to a request by the government of the United Kingdom for assistance in the enforcement of an order is to be treated as reducing the amount payable in England and Wales under a confiscation order made by the Crown Court.

items.title: The Controlled Waters (Lochs and Ponds) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: Part II of the Control of Pollution Act 1974, which makes provision on water quality and control of discharges to water, applies to waters of various classes including waters of certain reservoirs. This Order defines as waters to which Part II applies other reservoirs except those which contain water that has been treated with a view to complying with regulation 23 of the Water Supply (Water Quality) (Scotland) Regulations 1990 (S.I. 1990/119).

items.title: The Trial of the Pyx (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Trial of the Pyx Order 1975 by inserting a definition of “coins ready for issue”. It also amends article 3(1) of that Order to provide that the sample of cupro-nickel coins to be provided for the Trial of the Pyx shall be one coin out of every 5,000 coins of a denomination of more than ten pence ready for issue,and one coin out of every 20,000 coins of a denomination of ten pence or less unless the total number of cupro-nickel coins subject to trial in any year is less than 250 million, in which case the sample is to be one coin out of every 5,000 ready for issue, irrespective of denomination.

items.title: The New Forest (Confirmation of Byelaws of the Verderers of the New Forest) Order 1990
items.description: By this Order the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food confirms byelaws made by the Verderers of the New Forest, and brings them into force on

items.title: The European Economic Interest Grouping (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the European Economic Interest Grouping (Fees) Regulations 1989 by increasing some of the fees payable to the registrar of companies which were prescribed by those Regulations, as follows.

items.title: The Medicines (Fees Relating to Medicinal Products for Animal Use) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which replace the Medicines (Fees Relating to Medicinal Products Animal Use) Regulations 1989, prescribe fees in connection with applications and inspections relating to licences and certificates granted under the Medicines Act 1968 in so far as they apply to medicinal products for animal use only.

items.title: The Company Auditors (Recognition Orders) (Application Fees) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the fees payable by applicants for recognition orders under Part II of the Companies Act 1989.

items.title: The Insurance Companies (Transfer of Long Term Business) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations provide that where there is a transfer of long term business from an insurance company authorised under the Insurance Companies Act 1982 to a friendly society authorised under Friendly Societies (Long Term Insurance Business) Regulations 1987, section 49 of the Insurance Companies Act 1982 shall apply withthe adaptations set out in regulation 2. In providing for these adaptations these Regulations give further effect to Article 25 of Council Directive 79/267/EEC dated 5 March 1979 (O. J. No. L63, 13.3.79, p.1) relating to the taking-up and pursuit of the business of direct life assurance.

items.title: The Education (Welsh Medium Teacher Training Incentive Supplement) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations which revoke and replace the Education (Welsh Medium Teacher Training Incentive Supplement) Regulations 1988 empower the Secretary of State to pay training supplements to persons undergoing training as teachers to facilitiate and encourage their training to teach subjects in Welsh as well as in English (regulation 4). This note briefly explains the Regulations and the main changes from the earlier ones.

items.title: The Surface Waters (Classification) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the system of classifying the quality of inland waters (as defined in section 30A(5)(b) of the Control of Pollution Act 1974) according to their suitability for abstraction by water authorities for supply (after treatment) as drinking water.

items.title: The Medicines (Medicated Animal Feeding Stuffs) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Medicines (Medicated Animal Feeding Stuffs) Regulations 1989 by—1defining the registrar who will keep the register of manufacturers (regulation 2(2));2providing that a person, in the course of a business carried on by him, may incorporate in animal feeding stuffs a veterinary drug which is already exempted from the licensing and certification requirements of the Medicines Act 1968 without the need to be registered (regulation 2(3)(a));3providing that incorporation of a medicinal product in animal feeding stuffs in accordance with a written veterinary direction applies only in respect of medicinal products to which a product licence relates and not in respect of medicinal products to which an animal test certificate relates (regulation 2(3)(a));4redrafting regulation 3 to clarify the provisions relating to the keeping of written records in respect of medicated animal feeding stuffs intended for export (regulation 2(3)(b));5making other minor drafting amendments (regulation 2(4) and (5) and (6));

items.title: The Stock Transfer (Gilt-edged Securities) (Exempt Transfer) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations specify further gilt-edged securities, namely securities which are on registers kept by the Bank of England and issued by the Government of Finland or the Government of Gibraltar, which may be transferred through the CGO Service established by the Central Gilts Office of the Bank. Other securities which may be so transferred are specified in the Stock Transfer (Gilt-Edged Securities) (Exempt Transfer) Regulations 1985 (S.I. 1985/1145, amended by S.I. 1990/1027), 1987 (S.I. 1987/1294), 1988 (S.I. 1988/232) and 1989 (S.I. 1989/880). Such transfers will be effective under section 1(2) of the Stock Transfer Act 1982 without the need for an instrument in writing.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates Texaco North Sea UK Company and Evansgrove Limited, as owners of the pipe-lines described in the Schedule, which lie above and between a number of offshore oilfields situated east of the Highland Region of Scotland.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates those persons listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule. The pipe-lines are all within the Ninian Field which lies to the North-East of the coast of Scotland.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 3) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates owners of the pipe-line lying between the North West Hutton and the South Cormorant oil fields situated north-east of the Shetland Islands. The companies listed in Part I of the Schedule are designated as owners of the apparatus and works and the riser which are part of the pipe-line and which are installed on or beneath the Cormorant A platform, and the companies listed in Part II of the Schedule are hereby designated as owners of the remainder of the pipe-line.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 4) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule. The pipe-lines lie between installations situated above the Tern, Eider and North Cormorant oilfields, north-east of the Shetland Islands.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 5) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule. The pipe-lines lie between installations situated above the Ness and Beryl Oilfields, east of the Orkney Islands.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 6) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as the owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule.The pipe-lines lie between installations situated above the Ivanhoe, Rob Roy and Claymore oilfields, east of the Highland Region of Scotland.

items.title: The British Railways Board (Central Wales Railway) Light Railway (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The British Railways Board (Central Wales Railway) Light Railway (Amendment) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Formal Investigations) (Amendment) Rules 1990
items.description: This version of the The Merchant Shipping (Formal Investigations) (Amendment) Rules 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Agricultural or Forestry Tractors and Tractor Components (Type Approval) (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Agricultural or Forestry Tractors and Tractor Components (Type Approval) (Fees) Regulations 1979 (“the 1979 Regulations”).

items.title: The Housing Renovation etc. Grants (Prescribed Forms and Particulars) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations prescribe forms of applications for grants under Part VIII of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989. They also prescribe particulars to be contained in applications. Form 1 is to be used for applications for renovation grants. Form 2 is to be used for applications for disabled facilities grants. Form 3 is to be used for applications of common parts grants. Form 4 is to be used for applications for HMO grants. (These are grants relating to houses in multiple occupation).

items.title: The Gipsy Encampments (District of Richmondshire) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates the District of Richmondshire as an area to which section 10 of the Caravan Sites Act 1968 applies.

items.title: The Education (Inner London Education Authority) (Property Transfer) Order 1990
items.description: This Order makes provision for the transfer of property, rights and liabilities of the Inner London Education Authority (“ILEA”) on its abolition by the Education Reform Act 1988 (“the Act”) on 1st April 1990 (“the abolition date”).

items.title: The Slaughter of Animals (Humane Conditions) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Regulations.)

items.title: The London Docklands Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (London Borough of Southwark) Order 1989
items.description: This Order vests in the London Docklands Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the council of the London borough of Southwark (or in another body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980), and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Slaughter of Animals (Humane Conditions) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations revoke and re-enact, with certain amendments, the provisions of the Slaughter of Animals (Prevention of Cruelty) Regulations 1958 (as amended).

items.title: The Slaughter of Poultry (Humane Conditions) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Slaughter of Poultry (Humane Conditions) Regulations 1984. The changes of substance made to the 1984 Regulations are the inclusion of—1a requirement that the occupier or person in charge of any premises at which birds are offered or exposed for sale prior to being slaughtered there shall ensure that, on arrival at the premises, the birds are placed in accommodation in which they are able to stand upright, turn round and stretch their wings and are provided with a sufficient supply of wholesome food and clean drinking water (regulation 2 (c));2a provision extending the requirements in the 1984 Regulations relating to the maximum periods during which birds may be suspended before being slaughtered or stunned so that they now apply to guinea-fowl, ducks, geese and quails kept in captivity as well as to turkeys and domestic fowls so kept (regulation 2(d));3a provision extending the prohibition in the 1984 Regulations on the immersion in a scalding tank or the plucking of birds before minimum periods have elapsed following the severance of one or more of the major blood vessels of the neck so that it now applies to guinea-fowl, ducks, geese and quails kept in captivity as well as to turkeys and domestic fowl so kept (regulation 2 (d)); and4a requirement that any person who slaughters birds by the Jewish or Muslim method shall ensure that each bird is slaughtered by the severance, by rapid, uninterrupted movements of a knife, of its carotid arteries and that the knife to be used for slaughtering the birds is undamaged and of sufficient size and sharpness so as to enable each bird to slaughtered in such a manner (regulation 2(e)).

items.title: The Local Government (Compensation for Premature Retirement) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, by extending the application of the Local Government (Compensation for Premature Retirement) (Scotland) Regulations 1979, allow for the payment of compensation to, or in respect of, persons aged 50 or over who are members of the Local Government Superannuation Scheme, and employed at Newbattle Abbey College. Compensation is payable to such persons in terms of the principal Regulations, as amended by these Regulations, where such persons are prematurely retired by their employing authority on redundancy or in the interests of the efficiency of their employing authority’s functions. The compensation is in the form of additions to accrued superannuation benefits.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations correct errors in the Merchant Shipping (Fees) Regulations 1990. In Part II of the Schedule, dealing with the feesfor tonnage measurement, one of the levels of tonnage at which a different fee becomes payable set out in the Table in paragraph 3is corrected,and in paragraph 4, ancillary fees, the phrase “not exceeding £42” is substituted for “of £37” in sub-paragraph (7).

items.title: The Classification, Packaging and Labelling of Dangerous Substances (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Classification, Packaging and Labelling of Dangerous Substances Regulations 1984 (“the principal Regulations”) to give effect with respect to Great Britain to the provisions of the Commission Directive 88/490/EEC (OJ No. L 259, 19.9.88, p.1) adapting to technical progress for the tenth time Council Directive 67/548/EEC (OJ No. L 196, 16.8.67, p.1) (OJ/SE 197, p.234) on the classification, packaging and labelling of dangerous substances in relation to substances dangerous for supply and of the Commission Directive 89/451/EEC (OJ No. L 216, 27.7.89, p.75) adapting to technical progress for the third time Council Directive 77/728/EEC (OJ No. L 303/23, 28.11.77, p.23) on the classification, packaging and labelling of paints etc.

items.title: Fees in the Department of the Registers of Scotland (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order increases certain of the fees payable for the registration or recording of deeds in the registers under the management and control of the Keeper of the Registers of Scotland and will result in an average increase of 10% overall.

items.title: The Surface Waters (Dangerous Substances) (Classification) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the system of classifying the quality of inland waters, coastal waters and relevant territorial waters (as defined in section 30A(1) of the Control of Pollution Act 1974) according to the presence in them of concentrations of the dangerous substances listed in the Schedules to the Regulations.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No.4) (Solicitors' Fees) 1990
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends Part I of Chapter III of the Table of Fees in rule 347 of the Rules of Court of Session by increasing the sum for outlays which may be allowed to solicitors electing to charge the inclusive fee for undefended actions other than consistorial actions from £100 to £120.

items.title: The Inner London Juvenile Courts (Selection of Chairmen) Order 1990
items.description: This Order places on a statutory footing for the first time the process for selecting chairmen of juvenile courts in the inner London area. The new process follows, with minor modifications, the non-statutory process which has been in use for the past five years.

items.title: The Petty Sessional Divisions (Bedfordshire) Order 1990
items.description: This Order gives effect to a draft order submitted by the magistrates' courts committee for the county of Bedfordshire and provides for the alteration of the name of the petty sessional division of North Bedfordshire to Bedford.

items.title: The London-Portsmouth Trunk Road A3 (Liphook-Ham Barn Section Slip Roads) (Part 3) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The London-Portsmouth Trunk Road A3 (Liphook-Ham Barn Section Slip Roads) (Part 3) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Income-related Benefits Schemes Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Family Credit (General) Regulations 1987, the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987, the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987 and the Housing Benefit (Community Charge Rebates) (Scotland) Regulations 1988 so that in calculating a claimant’s income and capital for determining his entitlement to those benefits any payment made to haemophiliacs under the Macfarlane (Special Payments) Trust are to be disregarded.

items.title: The Suppression of Terrorism Act 1978 (Designation of Countries) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates Finland and Greece for the purposes of the Suppresssion of Terrorism Act 1978 so that they become convention countries within the meaning of the Act.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Capital Finance) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: Section 57 of the Local Governmentand Housing Act 1989 (“the 1989 Act”) empowers the Secretary of State to specify by regulationscertain grants, contributionsand subsidies paid to local authorities. Such grants etc. then become “specified capital grants” for the purposes of that section. Section 57 provides that if a local authority receive a specified capital grant their relevant credit approvals shall be reduced by deducting therefrom an amount equal to the specified capital grant received.

items.title: The Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (Commencement No. 8 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (Commencement No. 8 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Petty Sessional Divisions (Berkshire) Order 1990
items.description: This Order gives effect to a draft Order submitted by the magistrates' courts committee for the County of Berkshire and provides for the transfer of certain areas between the petty sessional divisions of Maidenhead, Reading & Sonning, The Forest, West Berkshire and Windsor.

items.title: The Corn Returns (Scotland) (Variation) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Corn Returns (Scotland) Regulations 1976 by substituting a new form of return for the previous form set out in the Corn Returns (Scotland) (Amendment) Regulations 1981. The principal change is the introduction of new footnotes which specify the appropriate use of each row.

items.title: The School Boards (Financial Information) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the form in which financial statements are to be provided by an education authority to a school board for the purposes of section 17(4) of the School Boards (Scotland) Act 1988. The information required to be provided to a school board under that provision is an annual statement of the monies paid out by the education authority in the previous financial year in respect of the running costs of and capital expenditure on the school (referred to in the Regulations as “the outturn statement”) and an annual statement of the monies proposed to be paid out by the authority in respect of such costs and expenditure on the school in the financial year in which the statement is made (referred to in the Regulations as “the budget statement”). The Regulations also prescribe the dates by which such statements must be provided.

items.title: The Higher Education (Wales) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations made under sections 218(9)(b)and 232(6) of the Education Reform Act 1988, which apply only in relation to maintained institutions in Walesand to the institutions in Wales designated by Schedule 2, provide for the provision ofcourses, designated by or under Schedule 1 as courses of initial teacher training, to be subject to the approval of the Secretary of State (regulation 4). The Regulations enable the Secretary of State to direct that such a course shall cease to be provided (regulation 5) and to give directions with respect to the number and categories of students to be admitted to a course (regulation 6). TheRegulations succeed provisions contained in the Higher Education (Wales) Regulations 1989 (S.I. 1989/220) which applied to all full-time courses of higher education. The effect of these regulations is, therefore, to remove the requirement for approval in the caseof all courses of higher education other than courses of initial teacher training. This mirrors the position obtaining in England under the Education (Schools and Further and Higher Education) Regulations 1989 (S.I. 1989/351).

items.title: The Export of Goods (Control) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Export of Goods (Control) Order 1989 to take account of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 3722/89, Council Regulation (EEC) No. 3723/89, Commission Decision No. 3724/89/ECSC, Commission Regulation (EEC) No.3725/89, Commission Regulation (EEC) No. 3726/89and Commission Decision No. 3727/89/ECSC, which relate to the restriction and monitoring of exports of certain steel products to the United States of America.

items.title: The Education (School Teachers' Pay and Conditions) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Education (School Teachers' Pay and Conditions) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Housing (Right to Buy) (Designated Rural Areas and Designated Regions) (England) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates the Borough of West Devon (exception of the parishes of Tavistock and Okehampton and so much of the Dartmoor National Park as is within that borough) as a rural area for the purposes of section 157 of the Housing Act 1985 (“the Act”). The Order also designates the County of Devon as the designated region for the purposes of that section in relation to dwelling-houses in this rural area.

items.title: The Local Government Superannuation (Scotland) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Local Government Superannuation (Scotland) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1850) to correct an error in the provisions for calculation of death gratuity. The error was introduced by the Local Government Superannuation (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990 (S.I. 1990/422) which came into force on 1st April 1990.

items.title: The Local Government Finance (Miscellaneous Amendments and Repeal) Order 1990
items.description: This Order makes provision consequent upon the Local Government Finance Act 1988 (which establishes new systems of community charges and non-domestic rates).

items.title: The Social Security (Local Councillors) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Severe Disablement Allowance (Amendment) and Local Councillors Consequential) Regulations 1989. They provide for the calculation of a councillor’s basic allowance for the purposes of severe disablement allowance, sickness benefit, invalidity benefit or an invalidity pension under section 15 or section 16 of the Social Security Pensions Act 1975 (c. 60). They are made as a consequence of the amendment to Schedule 8 to the Social Security Act 1989 by Schedule 11 to the Local Government and Housing Act 1989. That Act introduced a basic allowance for councillors which is payable in addition to an attendance allowance and a special responsibility allowance.

items.title: The Standard Community Charge (Multipliers) Order 1990
items.description: This Order adds to the factors by reference to which charging authorities may specify classes of property for the purpose of determining their standard community charge multi)pliers. By virtue of the Order, charging authorities may have regard to the periods for which unoccupied properties have peviously been occupied, the period for which properties would have been unoccupied if all or some periods of occupation were treated as periods during which the properties were unoccupied, and the period which has elapsed since a grant of probate or of letters of administration was made.

items.title: The Income Tax (Interest Relief) (Qualifying Lenders) Order 1990
items.description: By article 2, the bodies named in article 3 are prescribed as qualifying lenders so that interest on qualifying loans made by them may be paid to them under deduction of tax under the mortgage interest relief at source scheme (MIRAS) (introduced by the Finance Act 1982 (c. 39)) from the dates specified in the Order.

items.title: The Electrical Equipment for Explosive Atmospheres (Certification) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directives No.76/117/EEC (OJ No. L24, 30.1.76, p. 45) concerning electrical equipment for use in potentially explosive atmospheres, 79/196/EEC (OJ No.L43, 20.2.79, p. 20) concerning electrical equipment for use in potentially explosive atmospheres employing certain types of protection, as adapted to technical progress by Commission Directives No.84/47/EEC (OJ No.L31, 2.2.84, p. 19) and 88/571/EEC (OJ No.L311, 17.11.88, p. 46) and Council Directive No.82/130/EEC (OJ No. L59, 2.3.82 p. 10) concerning electrical equipment for use in potentially explosive atmospheres in mines susceptible to firedamp, as adapted to technical progress by Commission Directive No.88/35/EEC (OJ No.L20, 26.1.88, p. 28).

items.title: The Revenue Support Grant (Scotland) Order 1989
items.description: This Order redetermines the amount of revenue support grant payable to each local authority in respect of the financial year 1989-90.

items.title: The Personal Injuries (Civilians) Amendment (No. 2) Scheme 1990
items.description: This Scheme extends the provisions relating to mobility supplement in Article 25A of the Personal Injuries (Civilians) Scheme 1983 (“the principal Scheme”), inserted by the Personal Injuries (Civilians) Amendment Scheme 1983 (S.I. 1983/1164). It enables a person who is receiving a pension under the principal Scheme because of disablement arising from being both blind and deaf, to qualify for mobility supplement where, by reason of the combined effects of being blind and deaf, he is unable without the assistance of another person to walk to any intended or required destination while out of doors.

items.title: The Police (Dispensation from Requirement to Investigate Complaints) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police (Anonymous, Repetitious Etc. Complaints) Regulations 1985 with effect from Ist July 1990 to enable the Police Complaints Authority to dispense with the requirements of Part IX of the Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 (which relates to the investigation of complaints about the conduct of police officers) in a wider range of circumstances. The additional circumstances are where a complaint is repetitious following the informal resolution of a previous similar complaint; where a complaint is vexatious; or where there has been unreasonable delay in making a complaint.

items.title: The Leicester-Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (A47) (Etling Green Junction Improvement Slip Road) Order 1990
The European Communities (Designation) ( No. 2) Order 1990 (revoked)
items.description: This is a further Order in the series of Orders designating the Ministers who, and departments which, may exercise powers to make regulations conferred by section 2(2) of the European Communities Act 1972 and specifying the matters in relation to which these powers may be exercised.

items.title: The Extradition (Suppression of Terrorism) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order applies Schedule 1 to the Extradition Act 1989 in the case of Finland and Greece, being States party to the European Convention on the Suppression of Terrorism (which was signed at Strasbourg on 27th January 1977), subject to and in accordance with the extradition treaties made with them as amended by the Convention.The Order comes into force on 1st August 1990.

items.title: The Parliamentary Constituencies (England) (Miscellaneous Changes) Order 1990
items.description: This Order gives effect without modification to the recommendations contained in the report of the Boundary Commission for England dated 28th February 1990.

items.title: The Naval, Military and Air Forces etc. (Disablement and Death) Service Pensions Amendment (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order extends the provisions relating to mobility supplement in article 26A of the Naval, Military and Air Forces etc. (Disablement and Death) Service Pensions Order 1983 (S.I. 1983/883) (“the principal Order”), inserted by the Naval, Military and Air Forces etc. (Disablement and Death) Service Pensions Amendment Order 1983 (S.I.1983/1116). It enables aperson who is receiving a pension under the principal Order because of disablement arising from being both blind and deaf, to qualify for mobility supplement where by reason of the combined effects of being blind and deaf he is unable without the assistance of another person to walk to an intended or required destination while out of doors.

items.title: The Cable (Prescribed Diffusion Service) Order 1990
items.description: Under the Cable and Broadcasting Act 1984 the Cable Authority are charged with a number of duties in relation to “prescribed diffusion services”. These include the duty, under section 4(9), to exercise their licensing powers in the manner they consider best calculated to promote the provision of such services, and the duty, under section 5, to select licensees to provide such services following advertisement and public consultation. By virtue of section 2(3) a “prescribed diffusion service” means a diffusion service of such description as the Secretary of State, after consultation with the Cable Authority, may by order prescribe.

items.title: The Revenue Support Grant (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order determines the amount of the revenue support grant payable to each local authority specified in the Schedule to the Order in respect of the financial year 1990-91.

items.title: The Nursing Homes Registration (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which replace the Nursing Homes Registration (Scotland) Regulations 1988, provide for the registration of persons in respect of nursing homes under the Nursing Homes Registration (Scotland) Act 1938 (“the 1938 Act”).

items.title: The Central Institutions (Recognition) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations recognise certain educational establishments for the provision of further education as central institutions and revoke the Central Institutions (Recognition) (Scotland) Regulations 1988.

items.title: The Police (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Police (Scotland) Regulations 1976, with effect from 1st April 1990 or, in the case of regulation 9, 1st July 1989 (retrospection is authorised by section 26(3) of the Police (Scotland) Act 1967).

items.title: The Employment Subsidies Act 1978 (Renewal) (Great Britain) Order 1990
items.description: This Order renews for Great Britain until 31st December 1991 the powers of section 1 of the Employment Subsidies Act 1978, which would otherwise not be exercisable after the end of June 1990, the section having been renewed until that date by the Employment Subsidies Act 1978 (Renewal) (Great Britain) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/2229). Section 1 of the Employment Subsidies Act 1978 authorises the Secretary of State to make payments to employers enabling them to retain in employment persons who might otherwise become unemployed, to take on new employees and generally to maintain or enlarge their labour force.

items.title: The Home-Grown Cereals Authority Levy (Variation) Scheme (Approval) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Home-Grown Cereals Authority Levy (Variation) Scheme (Approval) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Home-Grown Cereals Authority Oilseeds Levy Scheme (Approval) Order 1990
items.description: By this Order the Ministers approve a Scheme imposing a levy in respect of rapeseed, linseed, soyabean or sunflowerseed grown in the United Kingdom which are all home-grown cereals for the purposes of the Cereals Marketing Act 1965, by virtue of the Cereals Marketing Act (Application to Oilseeds) Order 1989 (S.I. 1989/1200) (which, inter alia, extended the definition of “home-grown cereals” in section 24 of that Act to include specified oilseeds). The Scheme, which is for the purpose of financing the Home-Grown Cereals Authority, applies throughout the United Kingdom and comes into force on 1st July 1990.

items.title: The Electricity (Restrictive Trade Practices Act 1976) (Exemptions) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order specifies agreements and descriptions of agreements relating to the generation, transmission or supply of electricity to which the Restrictive Trade Practices Act 1976 (“the 1976 Act”) is deemed not to apply and never to have applied. By virtue of section 100 (1) of the Electricity Act 1989 electricity is treated as goods for the purposes of the 1976 Act. This Order provides that the agreements specified in Part I of the Schedule to the Order, together with agreements meeting any of the descriptions specified in Part II of the Schedule and satisfying the relevant condition specified therein, will nevertheless fall outside the provisions of the 1976 Act.

items.title: The Dairy Produce Quotas (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Dairy Produce Quotas Regulations 1989 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The London North Circular Trunk Road (A406) and the London—Cambridge—Kings Lynn Trunk Road (A10) (Enfield) (Speed Limits) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The London North Circular Trunk Road (A406) and the London—Cambridge—Kings Lynn Trunk Road (A10) (Enfield) (Speed Limits) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Ungraded Eggs (Hygiene) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations prohibit the retail sale of cracked eggs by producers on their own farms, in local public markets or by door to door selling.

items.title: The National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force Part I (the National Health Service: England and Wales) and Part V (Miscellaneous and General) of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. The provisions are brought into force in stages on 5th, 25th and 26th July, 16th and 17th September and 1st October 1990 and 1st January and 1st April 1991.

items.title: The Family Health Services Authorities (Membership and Procedure) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision concerning the membership and procedure of Family Health Services Authorities established under the National Health Service Act 1977. They include provisions relating to membership — as to the number of members and conditions of membership (regulation 2), the tenure of office of members, the termination and suspension of tenure of office and eligibility for reappointment (regulations 3 to 5 and 8) and disqualification for appointment and cessation of disqualification (regulations 6 and 9). They include provisions relating to the constitution of authorities and to meetings and proceedings of those authorities — as to the vice-chairman (regulation 10), committees and joint committees (regulation 11) and the conduct of meetings and proceedings (regulations 12 and 13 and the Schedule), including provisions relating to restriction on members participating in meetings on account of pecuniary interest.

items.title: The Regional and District Health Authorities (Membership and Procedure) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provisions concerning the membership and procedure of Regional and District Health Authorities established under the National Health Service Act 1977. They include in Part II provisions relating to the numbers of members and conditions of membership (regulations 2 to 6 and Schedule 1), the tenure of office of members and termination of tenure of office and eligibility for reappointment (regulations 7 to 12) and for disqualification for appointment and cessation of disqualification (regulations 13 and 14). In Part III provisions are included relating to vice-chairman (regulations 15 and 16), the appointment of and exercise of functions by committees and sub-committees (regulations 17 and 18) and meetings and proceedings including disability for taking part in proceedings on account of pecuniary interest (regulations 19 and 20 and Schedule 2).

items.title: The Petroleum (Production) (Seaward Areas) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the provisions of the Petroleum (Production) (Seaward Areas) Regulations 1988 relating to the fee payable by an applicant for a production licence under the Petroleum (Production) Act 1934 in respect of an area offshore. In the case both of an invited and a non-invited application, the fee is increased to £3,700.

items.title: The Insurance Companies (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to the Insurance Companies Act 1982 (“the 1982 Act”), the Insurance Companies Regulations 1981 (“the 1981 Regulations”) and the Insurance Companies (Accounts and Statements) Regulations 1983 (“the 1983 Regulations”). The purpose of the amendments is to implement Council Directive 88/357/EEC (O.J. No. L172, 4.7.88, p. 1) relating to non-life insurance. Council Directive 73/239/EEC (O.J. No. L228, 16.8.73, p. 3) created a supervisory regime for direct non-life “establishment” business (ie general insurance business carried on by insurers through an establishment in a member State). Directive 88/357/EEC introduces changes to that regime and makes new provision for the regulation of insurers who provide direct non-life insurance to cover risks in a member State on a “services” basis, ie otherwise than through an establishment in that State.

items.title: The A40 Trunk Road (Western Avenue, Hillingdon) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1990
The Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (Commencement No. 8 and Transitional Provisions) (Amendment) Order 1990
The Ungraded Eggs (Hygiene) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations apply to the retail sale by the producer from his own production of ungraded eggs (which have not been packed in accordance with Articles 16 to 19 of Council Regulation (EEC) 2772/75 as amended) on his own farm, in a local public market and by door-to-door selling (regulation 3).

items.title: The Pilotage Act 1987 (Pilotage Commission: Transfer of Property, Rights and Liabilities) Order 1990
items.description: In accordance with section 26(2) of the Pilotage Act 1987 the Pilotage Commission submitted, on 14th March 1989, to the Secretary of State a scheme for the winding-up of the Commission and the disposal of the property, rights and liabilities of the Commission and the arrangements to be made as respects its staff. The Scheme is set out in Schedule 1 to the Order.

items.title: The Thames Water Utilities Limited (Local Statutory Provisions) (Consequential Repeals) Order 1990
The St Mary’s Music School (Aided Places) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the St Mary’s Music School (Aided Places) Regulations 1989 (the principal Regulations) to uprate the qualifying income levels for the remission of fees and charges and making of grants under the aided places scheme and in certain other respects.

items.title: The Education (Assisted Places) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Assisted Places) (Scotland) Regulations 1989 (the principal Regulations) to uprate the qualifying income levels for the remission of fees and charges and making of grants under the assisted places scheme and in certain other respects.

items.title: The Education Authority Bursaries (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education Authority Bursaries (Scotland) Regulations 1988. Regulation 2 is amended to specify those training programmes currently in operation. Regulation 5 is amended to enable a refugee (or the spouse or child of a refugee) to be eligible for a bursary if he has the appropriate status at the date of the education authority receiving his application. A further amendment to regulation 5 specifies that fees-only awards available to European Community students will not be available to persons who have been ordinarily resident in the British Islands throughout the 3 years preceding the relevant date in respect of the course in question.

items.title: The Students' Allowances (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Students' Allowances (Scotland) Regulations 1987. Regulation 2 is amended to specify those training programmes currently in operation. Regulation 5 is amended to enable a refugee (or the spouse or child of a refugee) to be eligible for an allowance if he has the appropriate status as at the date of his application being received by the Secretary of State. A further amendment to regulation 5 specifies that fees-only awards available to European Community students will not be available to persons who have been ordinarily resident in the British Islands throughout the 3 years preceding the relevant date in respect of the course in question. Regulation 7 is amended so as to introduce a condition that payment of an allowance is dependant on a written application being received by the Secretary of State by a date to be determined by him.

items.title: The War Pensions Committees Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision for War Pensions Committees.

items.title: The Corn Returns (Variation) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Corn Returns Regulations 1976 by substituting a new form of return for the previous form set out in theSchedule to the Corn Returns (Amendment) Regulations 1981. The principal change is in the footnotes to clarify the appropriate use of each row of the return.

items.title: The Fodder Plant Seeds (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations which further amend the Fodder Plant Seeds Regulations 1985 implement Article 2a of Commission Directive 86/109/EEC (OJ No. L93, 8.4.86, p. 21) as amended by Commission Directive 89/424/EEC (OJ No. L196, 12.7.89, p. 50) to the extent that Brown top, Birdsfoot trefoil, Trefoil, Black medick and Alsike clover are deleted from the list of seeds to which the “Commercial Seed” category applies (regulation 2(2) and (3)(b)).

items.title: The Seeds (National Lists of Varieties) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Seeds (National Lists of Varieties) (Amendment) Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Northumbria Regional Flood Defence Committee (Appointed Day) Order 1990
items.description: Section 137 of the Water Act 1989 contains provisions for the establishment, as from the1st September 1989 (“the transfer date”),of regional flood defence committees in place of regional land drainage committees. Subsection (4) of that section makes provisionfor the membership of a regional flood defence committee during a period beginning with the transfer date and ending with a date appointed by, in the case of a regional flood defence committee in England, the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheriesand Food.

items.title: The Southern Regional Flood Defence Committee (Appointed Day) Order 1990
items.description: Section 137 of the Water Act 1989 contains provisions for the establishment, as from 1st September 1989 (“the transfer date”), of regional flood defence committees in place of regional land drainage committees. Subsection (4) of that section makes provision for the membership of a regional flood defence committee during a period beginning withthe transfer date and ending witha date appointed by, in the case of a regional flood defence committee in England, the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food.

items.title: The South West Regional Flood Defence Committee (Appointed Day) Order 1990
items.description: Section 137 of the Water Act 1989 contains provisions for the establishment, as from the1st September 1989 (“the transfer date”),of regional flood defence committees in place of regional land drainage committees. Subsection (4) of that section makes provisionfor the membership of a regional flood defence committee during a period beginning with the transfer date and ending with a date appointed by, in the case of a regional flood defence committee in England, the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheriesand Food.

items.title: The Yorkshire Regional Flood Defence Committee (Appointed Day) Order 1990
items.description: Section 137 of the Water Act 1989 contains provisions for the establishment, as from the 1st September 1989 (“the transfer date”), of regional flood defence committees in place of regional land drainage committees. Subsection (4) of that section makes provision for the membership of a regional flood defence committee during a period beginning withthe transfer date and ending witha date appointed by, in the case of a regional flood defence committee in England, the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food.

items.title: The Building (Procedure) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations replace the scale of building control fees in the Building (Procedure) (Scotland) Regulations 1981. The average increase is approximately one-third. Demolition warrant fees are now included in paragraph 1(a) of the table of fees and are charged on a sliding scale rather than at a fixed rate, as was previously the case. In addition, a fixed fee is now payable in respect of all applications for extension of a period of a warrant, not only in respect of those applications relating to limited life buildings.

items.title: The Land Registration Act 1988 (Commencement) Order 1990
items.description: This order brings into force on 3rd December 1990 the Land Registration Act 1988 the main effect of which is to substitute a new section 112 for sections 112 to 112C of the Land Registration Act 1925 (c. 21). The new section provides for the opening of the land register to public inspection and for public inspection of certain other documents at the Land Registry.

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Conservation Measures) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order further amends the Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Conservation Measures) Order 1986 (S.I. 1986/2090) (“the principal Order”), which makes provision for the enforcement of certain of the enforceable Community restrictions and obligations concerning technical measures for the conservation of fishery resources which are contained in Council Regulation (EEC) No. 3094/86 (“the Council Regulation”).

items.title: The Land Registration (Matrimonial Homes) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules, which replace the Land Registration (Matrimonial Homes) Rules 1983, make provision for the protection by notice of a charge in respect of a spouse’s rights of occupation under the Matrimonial Homes Act 1983 in relation to registered land. The Rules also make provision and prescribe revised forms for applications to register and renew such notices and for renewal of cautions, entered before 14th February 1983, to protect a spouse’s rights of occupation. They also re-state the circumstances in which the Chief Land Registrar may be required to serve a notice warning off an existing caution protecting a spouse’s rights of occupation.

items.title: The Land Registration (Official Searches) Rules 1990
items.description: This version of the The Land Registration (Official Searches) Rules 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Land Registration (Open Register) Rules 1990
items.description: 1These Rules are made as part of the implementation of the Land Registration Act 1988 (c. 3) (which in particular provides for the opening of the register to the public) and prescribe:athe forms to be used for application for office copies of the register, title plan and documents referred to on the register and for certificates of inspection of the title plan and provide for the manner in which such applications may be made;bthe cases where as of right documents in the custody of the registrar relating to any land or charge but not referred to on the register may be inspected and copies or extracts taken and office copies obtained and cases where application for a search of the index of proprietors' names may be made;cthe form of certificate of official inspection of the title plan;2These Rules also:aamend the Land Registration Rules 1925 so as to revoke:–rule287 (authority to inspect the register)rule288 (inspection otherwise than under authority of the proprietor)rule289 (information as to entries on the register at a specified date; also regarding closed registers and cancelled entries)rule290 (discretion of registrar and as to inspection of the Minor Interests Index)rule296 (office copies of entries in register)form80 (authority to inspect register) andform108 (application for disclosure of landlord’s name and address);bsubstitute a new rule 5 of the Land Registration Rules 1990 to provide that during the currency of any notice given pursuant to that rule, and in addition to applications for an official certificate of the result of search of the Index Map, applications under rules 2, 3 and 7 of these Rules may be made by facsimile transmission.

items.title: The Common Agricultural Policy (Wine) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which apply throughout the United Kingdom and come into force on 6th August 1990, provide for the enforcement of EEC Regulations (as specified in Schedules 1 and 2) concerned with the production and marketing of wine and related products.

items.title: The Road Traffic Accidents (Payments for Treatment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order increases the amounts payable under sections 157(2) and 158(2) of the Road Traffic Act 1988. Section 157 provides for payments for hospital treatment of traffic casualties to be made by an owner or insurer of a vehicle the use of which gave rise to the injuries, and section 158 for payments, to be made by the person using such a vehicle, for emergency treatment and a medical practitioner’s travelling expenses.

items.title: The Companies (Fees) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Companies (Fees) Regulations 1988 by increasing some of the fees payable to the registrar of companies which were prescribed by those Regulations, as follows:

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Quota Measures) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, which revokes the Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Quota Measures) Order 1988, as amended, makes provision for the enforcement of certain of the enforceable Community restrictions and other obligations relating to sea fishing set out in the Regulation adopted by the Council of the European Communities on 19th December 1989 (“the Council Regulation”). The Council Regulation fixes total allowable catches and quotas for 1990.

items.title: The Community Health Councils (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Community Health Councils Regulations 1985 mainly in consequence of the enactment of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990.

items.title: The Enduring Powers of Attorney (Prescribed Form) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations prescribe a revised form of an enduring power of attorney, the explanatory information endorsed on it and the manner in which it is to be executed.

items.title: The Third Country Fishing (Enforcement) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, which replaces and revokes the Third Country Fishing (Enforcement) Order 1989, makes breaches of specified articles of the Community Regulations set out in column 1 of the Schedule to the Order offences for the purposes of United Kingdom law where they occur within British fishery limits.

items.title: The Health and Safety (Training for Employment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations give the protection for and duties of employees under the relevant statutory provisions as defined in the Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974 to those who are provided with “relevant training” as defined in regulation 2.

items.title: The Education (Individual Pupils' Achievements) (Information) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, made under section 22 of the Education Reform Act 1988 (“the Act”), require head teachers of schools maintained by local education authorities and of grant-maintained schools to provide information about individual pupils' educational achievements to parents of the pupils, including pupils over compulsory school age.

items.title: The Lands Tribunal (Amendment) Rules 1990
items.description: This version of the The Lands Tribunal (Amendment) Rules 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Food Safety Act 1990 (Commencement No. 1)Order 1990
items.description: Upon the passing of the Food Safety Act 1990 sections 13 and 51, and paragraphs 12 to 15 of Schedule 2 (and , so far as relating to those paragraphs, section 52) of that Act came into force.

items.title: The Home Purchase Assistance (Recognised Lending Institutions) Order 1990
items.description: This Order adds four additional bodies to the list in section 447(1) of the Housing Act 1985. (Other bodies have been added to this list by previous Orders.) Bodies which are so listed are lending institutions recognised for the purposes of section 445 (assistance for first-time buyers) of the Act and may receive advances from the Secretary of State under that section to enable them to provide assistance to first-time purchasers of house property (the “Homeloan” Scheme).

items.title: The Housing (Right to Buy) (Priority of Charges)Order 1990
items.description: Under the Housing Act 1985 (“the 1985 Act”) liability to repay discount following the exercise of the right to buy or the right to be granted a shared ownership lease is secured by a charge on the dwelling-house. Such a charge does not have priority over certain charges securing advances by bodies which are approved lending institutions for the purposes of section 156 of that Act.

items.title: The Mortgage Indemnities (Recognised Bodies)Order 1990
items.description: This Order specifies four additional bodies as recognised bodies for the purposes of sections 442 and 443 of the Housing Act 1985. (Other bodies have been specified in previous Orders.)

items.title: The Free Zone (Liverpool) Designation (Variation) Order 1990
items.description: This Order varies the Free Zone (Liverpool) Designation Order 1984 as amended by the Free Zone (Liverpool) Designation (Variation) Order 1988 by adding a further 22 acres to the 621 acres currently designated as a free zone. The further acreage comprises the North Langton and Brocklebank Docks in the Port of Liverpool.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Lead in Cattle) (England) Order 1990
items.description: This Order contains emergency prohibitions on various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the circumstances designated in article 2 of the Order, namely a high level of lead in cattle.

items.title: The Companies Act 1989 (Commencement No. 6 and Transitional and Saving Provisions) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force certain of the provisions of the Companies Act 1989.

items.title: The Companies (Fair Dealing by Directors) (Increase in Financial Limits) Order 1990
items.description: This Order increases certain of the financial limits specified in Part X of the Companies Act 1985 (enforcement of fair dealing by directors). The limits in question are the sums specified in sections 320(2), 335(1), 337(3), 340(7), and 344(1). In each case, the sum specified is doubled. Section 138 of the Companies Act 1989 (c. 40) provides for increases in the other financial limits (relating to loans etc to directors) specified in Part X of the Companies Act

items.title: The Companies (Unregistered Companies) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Companies (Unregistered Companies) Regulations 1985, which apply certain provisions of the Companies Act 1985 to unregistered companies. The amendments have the effect of applying to such companies the new provisions relating to company contracts and execution of documents inserted into the Companies Act 1985 by the Companies Act 1989. These provisions include the abolition of the requirement to have a corporate seal.

items.title: The Definition of Subsidiary (Consequential Amendments) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations effect a minor amendment to the Electricity Act 1989, consequential on the new definition of “subsidiary” inserted by section 144 of the Companies Act 1989 into the Companies Act 1985 (c. 6).

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) Regulations 1987.

items.title: The Coroners' Records (Fees for Copies) (Amendment) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules amend the Coroners' Records (Fees for Copies) Rules 1982. They increase the fee payable to a coroner or other person for furnishing a copy of an inquisition or other document in his custody relating to an inquest. The amounts increased by rule 2(2) relate to fees for copies other than photographic copies and the amount increased by rule 2(3) relates to the fee for a photographic copy.

items.title: The Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 (Commencement No. 5) Order 1990
items.description: The majority of the provisions of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 (the 1988 Act) were brought into force on 1st August 1989 by the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1989 (S.I. 1989/816 (C.21)), as amended by S.I. 1989/1303 (C. 45) (the Commencement No. 4 Order).

items.title: The Education (Student Loans) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st August 1990, are the first to be made under section 1 of and Schedule 2 to the Education (Student Loans) Act 1990 (“the Act”). They apply in relation to an academic year beginning on or after 1st August 1990 (regulation 1).

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (East Coast Cable Ltd) (Ipswich and Colchester) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to East Coast Cable Ltd on 7th June 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the Ipswich and Colchester area. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 19th July 1990. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, East Coast Cable Ltd will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its Ipswich and Colchester public telecommunication systems when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The A590 Greenodd to Barrow-in-Furness Trunk Road (Improvement at Melton Terrace) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A590 Greenodd to Barrow-in-Furness Trunk Road (Improvement at Melton Terrace) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Greenodd to Barrow-in-Furness Trunk Road (A590 Dalton-in-Furness Bypass) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Greenodd to Barrow-in-Furness Trunk Road (A590 Dalton-in-Furness Bypass) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Greenodd to Barrow-in-Furness Trunk Road (A590 Dalton-in-Furness Bypass) (Detrunking) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Greenodd to Barrow-in-Furness Trunk Road (A590 Dalton-in-Furness Bypass) (Detrunking) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Park Road (East of Oak Lea Road to North Road) (Trunking) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Park Road (East of Oak Lea Road to North Road) (Trunking) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The National Health Service (Appointment of Consultants) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (Appointment of Consultants) Regulations 1982 which prescribe procedures relating to the appointment of consultants.

items.title: The A59 Trunk Road Mellor Brook (Detrunking) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A59 Trunk Road Mellor Brook (Detrunking) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Samlesbury—Skipton Trunk Road (A59 Mellor Brook Bypass) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Samlesbury—Skipton Trunk Road (A59 Mellor Brook Bypass) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Coal Industry (Restructuring Grants) Order 1990
items.description: This Order concerns the making of grants by the Secretary of State to the British Coal Corporation under section 3 of the Coal Industry Act 1987 (grants for workforce redeployment and reduction, etc.).Article 3 specifies the financial year of the Corporation ending in March 1991 as a year in respect of which such grants may be made. Article 4 provides for the kinds of expenditure specified in the Schedule to the Order to rank as eligible expenditure of the Corporation and their wholly-owned subsidiaries in relation to the financial year. Article 5 sets a limit on the amount which may be advanced in relation to the financial year with respect to each head of expenditure specified in the Schedule. The limit is 90 per cent. of the relevant costs appearing in the consolidated profit and loss account or 90 per cent. of such larger amount as may be agreed by the Secretary of State and the Corporation with the approval of the Treasury.

items.title: The Social Security Revaluation of Earnings Factors Order 1990
items.description: This Order is made consequent upon a review under section 21 of the Social Security Pensions Act 1975 (“the Pensions Act”), which provides for the revaluation of earnings factors so that they maintain their value in relation to the general level of earnings obtaining in Great Britain.

items.title: The Companies Act 1989 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force certain of the provisions of the Companies Act 1989.

items.title: The Community Charges (Administration and Enforcement) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Community Charges (Administration and Enforcement) Regulations 1989 (“the 1989 Regulations”).

items.title: The Copyright (Material Open to Public Inspection) (Marking of Copies of Plans and Drawings) Order 1990
items.description: Section 47(2) of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 provides exceptions to copyright in the case of material which is open to public inspection pursuant to a statutory requirement. This Order provides that, in the case of copies of plans and drawings, the exceptions apply only to copies which are marked in the manner specified in article 2 of the Order.

items.title: The Opencast Coal (Rate of Interest on Compensation) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: Section 35 of the Opencast Coal Act 1958 provides that interest shall be payable in addition to compensation in certain circumstances. This Order provides that the rate of interest shall be 4 per cent, per annum less than the base rate quoted from time to time by the Committee of London Clearing Bankers, and revokes the Opencast Coal (Rate of Interest on Compensation) Order 1990.

items.title: Children (Admissibility of Hearsay Evidence) Order 1990
items.description: This Order exercises the power taken by the Lord Chancellor in the Children Act 1989 to abrogate the hearsay rule in respect of evidence given in civil proceedings in connection with the upbringing, maintenance or welfare of a child.

items.title: Food Hygiene (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Food Hygiene (Docks, Carriers etc.) Regulations 1960 and the Food Hygiene (General) Regulations 1970 by modifying the duty imposed by those Regulations to prevent infestation of areas where food is kept, by pests (regulations 2(b) and 4(c)).

items.title: The Education (Reorganisation in Inner London) (Redundancy Payments) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Redundancy Payments (Local Government) (Modification) Order 1983 as previously amended by the Redundancy Payments (Local Government) (Modification) (Amendment) Orders 1985, 1988, 1989 and 1990. Those Orders modified certain redundancy payments provisions of the Employment Protection (Consolidation) Act 1978 (c. 44) in their application to persons employed in relevant local government service so that their employment in the service is to be treated as if it were continuous for the purposes of those provisions. This Order adds the City Literary Institute to the list of employers to whose employees the 1983 Order as amended applies.

items.title: The Education (Reorganisation in Inner London) (Compensation) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Reorganisation in Inner London) (Compensation) Regulations 1989 (“the 1989 Regulations”) which make provision for the payment of compensation in cases falling within section 173(1)and (2) of the Education Reform Act 1988 (c. 40) (certain persons suffering loss of employment with the Inner London Education Authority or the London Residuary Body, or loss or diminution of emoluments, attributable to provision made by or under Part III of that Act)

items.title: The Building Societies (Designation of Qualifying Bodies) Order 1990
items.description: The Order consolidates the Orders revoked by article 5 and makes provision as to the bodies corporate which a building society may invest in or support under section 18 of the Building Societies Act 1986.

items.title: The Charge Limitation (England) (Maximum Amount) Order 1990
items.description: This Order states the amount which the amount calculated by the Council of the London Borough of Hillingdon in relation to the chargeable financial year beginning with 1st April 1990 under section 95 of the Local Government Finance Act 1988 is not to exceed.

items.title: The Charge Limitation (England) (Maximum Amount) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order states the amount which the amount calculated by each of the charging authorities listed in the Schedule to the Order in relation to the chargeable financial year beginning with 1st April 1990 under section 95(4) of the Local Government Finance Act 1988 is not to exceed.

items.title: The Charge Limitation (England) (Maximum Amount) (No. 3) Order 1990
items.description: This Order states the amount which the amount calculated by each of the charging authorities listed in Schedule 1 to the Order in relation to the chargeable financial year beginning with 1st April 1990 under section 95(4) of the Local Government Finance Act 1988 is not to exceed. It also states the amount which the aggregate amount of precepts issued by each of the precepting authorities listed in Schedule 2 for that year is not to exceed.

items.title: Church of England (Legal Aid) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules make it possible to grant ecclesiastical legal aid under the Church of England (Legal Aid and Miscellaneous Provisions) Measure 1988 to a priest or deacon in respect of any appeal by him to an archbishop under section 50 of the Ecclesiastical Jurisdiction Measure 1963 (1963 No. 1) where the diocesan bishop intends to depose him from Holy Orders. The Rules also provide that in such cases the Legal Aid Commission will not be bound by the normal rule which requires a person applying for ecclesiastical legal aid to show that he has reasonable grounds for taking, defending or being a party to the proceedings in respect of which legal aid is sought.

items.title: Ecclesiastical Judges and legal Officers (Fees) Order 1990
items.description: This Order increases the fees fixed by the Ecclesiastical Judges and Legal Officers (Fees) Order 1989 (other than the fees for certain items in connection with the register of patrons under the Patronage (Benefices) Measure 1986 (where the amount payable is, in effect, a fair and reasonable sum rather than a fixed fee) and the fees for acting as presiding officer at elections to the General Synod). It also substitutes a new table of fees payable on taxation of costs in discipline and faculty appeal cases for that contained in the Legal Officers Fees Order 1975.

items.title: The Driving Licences (Community Driving Licence) Regulations 1990
