items.description: These Regulations amend the Road Traffic Act 1988, the Road Traffic Offenders Act 1988, the Road Traffic (Driver Licensing and Information Systems) Act 1989 (c. 22), the Criminal Justice Act 1972 and the Powers of Criminal Courts Act 1973 so as to give further effect to Article 2 of Council Directive (EEC) No. 80/1263 of 4th December 1980 on the introduction of a Community driving licence.

items.title: Legal Officers (Annual Fees) Order 1990
items.description: This Order increases the annual fees for diocesan and provincial registrars fixed by the Legal Officers (Annual Fees) Order 1989.

items.title: Parochial Fees Order 1990
items.description: Article 1 and the Schedule establish a new table of fees payable for certain matters in connection with baptisms, marriages and burials, for the erection of monuments in churchyards and for other miscellaneous matters. The fees are in substitution for fees prescribed by the Parochial Fees Order 1989.

items.title: The Northern Ireland Act 1974 (Interim Period Extension) Order 1990
items.description: This Order extends until 16th July 1991 the period specified in section 1(4) of the Northern Ireland Act 1974 (as extended by the Northern Ireland Act 1974 (Interim Period Extension) Order 1989 (S.I. 1989/1096)) for the operation of the temporary provisions for the government of Northern Ireland contained in Schedule 1 to that Act.

items.title: The Leicester—Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (A47)(Narborough Bypass) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Leicester—Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (A47)(Narborough Bypass) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Welfare of Livestock Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Welfare of Livestock Regulations 1990 Approved by both Houses of Parliament is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Social Security Act 1990 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Social Security Act 1990 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Local Government (Politically Restricted Posts) (No. 2) Regulations 1990
items.description: Local authorities are required by section 2(2) of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 to prepare and maintain a list of posts in respect of which the annual rate of remuneration is or exceeds £19,500 or such higher amount as may be specified in or determined under regulations made by the Secretary of State.

items.title: The Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 (Application to Armed Forces) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 (Application to Armed Forces) Order 1985 by substituting a reference to a member of the Royal Naval Regulating Branch for the reference to a member of the Royal Naval Special Investigation Branch in the definition of “service policeman”.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Collection and Enforcement) (Miscellaneous Provisions) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which are made under Part III of the Local Government Finance Act 1988, deal with miscellaneous matters concerning the collection and enforcement of non-domestic rates.

items.title: The Glasgow to Carlisle Motorway (M74) (River Sark to Guards Mill Section) Scheme 1990
items.description: This version of the The Glasgow to Carlisle Motorway (M74) (River Sark to Guards Mill Section) Scheme 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Inshore Fishing (Birch Point, Firth of Clyde) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1990
items.description: This Order prohibits all fishing for sea fish in waters within a radius of 2 nautical miles of a point in the Firth of Clyde, off Birch Point, Isle of Arran, described in article 2(2) of the Order. The prohibition applies from 23.59 hours on Friday 20th July 1990 until 00.01 hours on Monday 6th August 1990.

items.title: The Portsmouth (Camber Dock and Flathouse Wharf) Harbour Revision Order 1990
items.description: This Order empowers Portsmouth City Council as the harbour authority for the Camber Dock and Flathouse Wharf undertakings, to provide and charge for parking facilities, to remove vehicles and trailers causing an obstruction or left without permission, to grant leases for harbour purposes and to appropriate any part of the undertakings for exclusive or preferential use. The Order also incorporates and applies certain provisions of the Harbours Docks and Piers Clauses Act 1847 and disapplies other provisions of that Act.

items.title: The Design Right (Proceedings before Comptroller) (Amendment) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules amend rule 6 of the Design Right (Proceedings before Comptroller) Rules 1989 (which authorises the Comptroller to refuse to recognise certain persons as agents in respect of any proceedings before him) to take account of the coming into force of sections 275 (register of patent agents) and 276 (persons entitled to describe themselves as patent agents) of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988.

items.title: The Patent Agents (Non-recognition of Certain Agents by Comptroller) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules authorise, in accordance with section 281 of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 the Comptroller-General of Patents, Designs and Trade Marks to refuse to recognise certain persons as agents in respect of any business under the Patents Act 1949, the Registered Designs Act 1949 or the Patents Act 1977.

items.title: The Patents (Amendment) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules revoke paragraphs (3)and (4) of Rule 90 of the Patents Rules 1982, which authorised the comptroller to refuse to recognise certain agents in respect of any business under that Act. The revocation is consequential on the coming into force of section 281 of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 (c. 48) and the making of rules thereunder to authorise the Comptroller-General of Patents, Designs and Trade Marks to refuse to deal with certain agents — see S.I.1990/1454 (The Patents Agents (Non-recognition of Certain Agents by Comptroller) Rules 1990).

items.title: The Registered Designs (Amendment) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules revoke rule 11 of the Registered Designs Rules 1989 (which authorised the Registrar to refuse to recognise certain persons as agents in respect of any business under the Registered Designs Act 1949) consequent on the making of similar rules under section 281 of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 (c. 48) (see S.I. 1990/1454)and the repeal by that Act of section 32 of the Registered Designs Act 1949.

items.title: The Register of Patent Agents Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules revoke and replace with modifications the Register of Patent Agents Rules 1978 as amended. They regulate the registration of persons who act as agents for others for the purpose of applying for or obtaining patents in the United Kingdom or elsewhere. Those on the register kept under the Register of Patent Agents Rules 1978 (the register to be kept under these Rules) on the coming into force of these Rules are treated as registered patent agents for the purposes of these Rules and section 276 of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 (persons entitled to describe themselves as patent agents).

items.title: The Register of Trade Mark Agents Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules regulate the registration of persons who act as agents for others for the purpose of applying for or obtaining the registration of trade marks.

items.title: The Trade Marks and Service Marks (Amendment) Rules 1990
items.description: Rule 14 of the Trade Marks and Service Marks Rules 1986 authorises the Registrar to refuse to recognise certain persons as agents in respect of business under the Trade Marks Act 1938. These Rules amend that rule to provide for the individuals or persons who may be refused recognition consequent on the coming into force of section 282 (register of trade mark agents) of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988and the making of rules thereunder — see the Register of Trade Mark Agents Rules 1990 (S.I.1990/1458).

items.title: The Community Charges (Co-owners) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the administration and collection of standard and collective community charges to which co-owners are jointly subject by virtue of regulation 59 of the Community Charges (Administration and Enforcement) Regulations 1989 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Supreme Court Fees (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Supreme Court Fees (Amendment) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The British Gas Corporation (Dissolution) Order 1990
items.description: This Order dissolves the British Gas Corporation. The Corporation’s property, rights and liabilities were transferred to British Gas p.l.c. on 24th August 1986 under section 49 of the Gas Act 1986. Section 57 of that Act provided for the Corporation to continue in existence after the transfer dat in order to carry out certain duties specified in Schedule 8 to the Act (in particular a duty to secure that the vesting of any foreign property, rights and liabilities in British Gas p.l.c. was effective under the relevant foreign law); those duties have been fulfilled.

items.title: The Agricultural Holdings (Form of Award in Arbitration Proceedings) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, which applies to England and Wales only, prescribes the form in which awards are to be made in proceedings on arbitrations under the Agricultural Holdings Act 1986 (article 3 and Schedule) and revokes the Agricultural Holdings (England and Wales) Rules 1948, as varied (“the 1948 Rules”) (article 4). The new form of award makes specific reference for the first time to rent cases, questions arising out of a notice to quit and the giving of reasons for the award, where appropriate. Arbitrators are required to omit or otherwise modify the items prescribed in the form only as far as circumstances require.

items.title: The Department of Trade and Industry (Fees) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Department of Trade and Industry (Fees) Order 1988 to enable the Secretary of State to take into account, in the determination of the amount of the fees fixed by him, certain other functions of the Secretary of State and of the Comptroller-General of Patents, Designs and Trade Marks (specified in article 2 of the Order) in relation to trade marks, registered designs and patents.

items.title: The Assured Tenancies and Agricultural Occupancies (Rent Information) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: The Assured Tenancies and Agricultural Occupancies (Rent Information) Order 1988, which this Order amends, specifies the information on rents of assured tenancies and assured agricultural occupancies which is to be made publicly available by the President of each rent assessment panel, the manner in which it is to be made available and the fee for a certified copy of the information. Under this Order further information is specified where a rent assessment committee is precluded by section 22(3) of the Housing Act 1988 from making a determination in relation to an assured shorthold tenancy. The Order also clarifies article 2 of the earlier Order (cases in which that Order applies).

items.title: The Legal Advice and Assistance (Scope) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Legal Advice and Assistance (Scope) Regulations 1989 by extending the scope of assistance by way of representation (ABWOR) to proceedings under the Matrimonial Proceedings (Magistrates' Courts) Act 1960 (which, notwithstanding its repeal by the Domestic Proceedings and Magistrates' Courts Act 1978, is preserved in relation to existing orders) and section 10A of the Fire Precautions Act 1971.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Competition and Specified Periods) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision under Part I of the Local Government Act 1988 (“the Act”) in respect of the activity of managing sports and leisure facilities by local authorities and specified authorities (defined in regulation 2) in Scotland. Managing sports and leisure facilities is one of the “defined activities” to which Part I of the Act applies, having been added to the list of defined activities in section 2(2) of the Act by the Local Government Act 1988 (Competition in Sports and Leisure Facilities) Order 1989 (S.I. 1989/2488).

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemptions) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1990
items.description: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemptions) (Scotland) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/1415) (“the 1988 Order”) provides that an activity listed in section 2(2) of the Local Government Act 1988 shall not be treated as a defined activity for the purpose of that Act so long as specified conditions are fulfilled.

items.title: The Abolition of Domestic Rates (Domestic and Part Residential Subjects) (No.2) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: Under Part I of the Abolition of Domestic Rates Etc. (Scotland) Act 1987 (c. 47) (“the Act”), rates are not leviable in respect of domestic subjects. Domestic subjects are defined in section 2(3) of the Act, as amended by the Local Government Finance Act 1988 (c. 41).

items.title: The Social Security (Unemployment, Sickness and Invalidity Benefit) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Social Security (Unemployment, Sickness and Invalidity Benefit) Amendment Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Electricity (Restrictive Trade Practices Act 1976) (Exemptions) (No. 3) Order 1990
items.description: This Order specifies agreements and descriptions of agreements relating to the generation, transmission or supply of electricity to which the Restrictive Trade Practices Act 1976 (“the 1976 Act”) is deemed not to apply and never to have applied. By virtue of section 100 (1) of the Electricity Act 1989 electricity is treated as goods for the purposes of the 1976 Act. This Order provides that the agreements specified in Part I of the Schedule to the Order, together with agreements meeting any of the descriptions specified in Part II of the Schedule and satisfying the relevant condition specified therein, will nevertheless fall outside the provisions of the 1976 Act.

items.title: The Financial Services Act 1986 (Electricity Industry Exemptions) Order 1990
items.description: Article 2 of the Order exempts the persons mentioned in that article from the requirement to obtain authorisation under the Financial Services Act 1986 in order to carry on the business of engaging in the relevant activities mentioned in the article.

items.title: The Financial Services Act 1986 (Restriction of Scope of Act and Meaning of Collective Investment Scheme)(No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order excludes the employee share schemes described in article 2 of the Order from the scope of the provisions of the Financial Services Act 1986 relating to collective investment schemes.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (England) Amendment No. 3 Order 1990
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (England) Order 1987 contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent the human consumption of food which has been or which may have been rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the USSR.

items.title: The County Court (Amendment No. 2) Rules 1990
items.description: The Lord Chancellor has power under section 287(1) of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 to designate a county court as a patents county court, with jurisdiction to hear and determine proceedings relating to patents or designs (and matters ancillary thereto). This Order amends the County Court Rules 1981 by adding a new Order 48A, which will establish a procedure for this new county court jurisdiction.

items.title: Patents County Court (Designation and Jurisdiction) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates the Edmonton County Court as a patents county court and confers on it jurisdiction to hear and determine proceedings relating to patents or designs (and matters ancillary thereto). The Edmonton County Court will be the first patents county court in England Wales and the commencement date of this Order coincides with the commencement of Part VI of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988, which makes provision for the first time for designated county courts to exercise jurisdiction in proceedings relating to patents on designs.

items.title: The Public Record Office (Fees) Regulations 1990
items.description: The Regulations replace the Public Record Office (Fees) Regulations 1988 and prescribe the full range of fees to be charged for authentication of copies of records and for other services provided by the Public Record Office.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Competition) (Wales) Regulations 1990
items.description: Section 6(3) of the Local Government Act 1988 enables the Secretary of State to require that an authority mentioned in section 1(1) of the Act (“a defined authority”) may carry out functional work falling within an activity listed in section 2(2) and described in Schedule 1 to the Act (“a defined activity”), only after subjecting the work to competition. The Local Government Act 1988 (Competition in Sports and Leisure Facilities) Order 1989 (S.I. 1989/2488) added the managing of sports and leisure facilities to the activities listed in section 2(2) and described in Schedule 1 to the Act. These Regulations specify the dates from which and the circumstances in which those bodies who are defined authorities (“referred to in the Order as specified authorities”) in Wales may not conduct the management of sports and leisure facilities without first subjecting them to competition.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemptions) (Wales) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemptions) (Wales) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/1469) provides that an activity listed in section 2(2) of the Local Government Act 1988 shall not be treated as a defined activity for the purpose of that Act so long as specified conditions are fulfilled.

items.title: The Pyramid Selling Schemes (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Pyramid Selling Schemes Regulations 1989 (“the Principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Highways (Road Humps) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Highways (Road Humps) Regulations 1990 (a) so that where there is a series of humps the application of the requirements specified in regulation 4(4) of those regulations is restricted to the first in the series of humps, and (b) to obviate a minor drafting inconsistency.

items.title: The Army, Air Force and Naval Discipline Acts (Continuation) Order 1990
items.description: This Order enables the provisions of the Army Act 1955, the Air Force Act 1955and the Naval Discipline Act 1957 to continue in force for a period of twelve months beyond 31st August 1990.

items.title: The British Nationality (Namibia) Order 1990
items.description: This Order adds Namibia to Schedule 3 to the British Nationality Act 1981 with the result that citizens of Namibia shall have the status of Commonwealth citizens.

items.title: The Child Abduction and Custody (Parties to Conventions) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Child Abduction and Custody (Parties to Conventions) Order 1986. It adds The Kingdom of the Netherlands to the list of the Contracting States to the Convention on the Civil Aspects of International Child Abduction signed at the Hague on 25th October 1980 (Cm.33) and to the European Convention on Recognition and Enforcement of Decisions Concerning Custody of Children and on the Restoration of Custody of Children, signed at Luxembourg on 20th May 1980 (Cm. 191).

items.title: The Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 (Isle of Man) Order 1990
items.description: This Order extends to the Isle of Man, subject to exceptions and modifications, certain of the provisions of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 relating to registered designs, patents and trademarks which have not already been extended there by the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 (Isle of Man) (No.2) Order 1989 (S.I.1989/1292); those provisions are section 272 and Schedule 3 (so far as not already extended), section 273 and Schedule 4 and Part V of that Act, together with associated amendments and repeals in the Registered Designs Act 1949 and the Patents Act 1977 made by Schedules 5 and 8 to that Act.

items.title: The European Convention on Extradition Order 1990
items.description: This Order gives effect to the European Convention on Extradition. It provides that the Extradition Act 1989, so far as it relates to extradition procedures under Part III of that Act shall apply as between the United Kingdom and the States parties to the European Convention on Extradition which are listed in Part I of Schedule 2 to the Order. It will come into force when the Convention enters into force for the United Kingdom, that is to say, 90 days after the deposit of the instrument of ratification on behalf of the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Copyright (Status of Former Dependent Territories) Order 1990
items.description: This Order declares the status under Part I of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 of certain former dependent territories of the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Films Co-Production Agreements (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: The Films Co-Production Agreements Order 1985 (“the principal Order”) provides that films made in accordance with the agreements between the Government of the United Kingdom and the Governments of the countries listed in the Schedule, as amended by S.I. 1985/2001, are to be treated as British films for the purposes of Schedule 1 to the Films Act 1985 (Certification for the purposes of section 72 of the Finance Act 1982 (c. 39) in case of British films.) The present Order extends the list of agreements scheduled to the principal Order to include an agreement with Australia dated 12th June 1990 (Cmnd. 1143).

items.title: The Air Navigation (Noise Certification) Order 1990
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the Air Navigation (Noise Certification) Order 1987. In addition to some minor and drafting amendments the following change is made:—

items.title: The Planning (Listed Buildings and Conservation Areas) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make procedural provision for applications for listed building consent, for conservation area consent, for the variation or discharge of conditions attached to listed building consents or conservation area consents, and for appeals in respect of these matters.

items.title: The National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 (Commencement No.2) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 24th July 1990 certain provisions of Part II (the National Health Service: Scotland) and Part V (Miscellaneous and General) of, and of Schedules 6, 9 and 10 to, the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. The provisions brought into force relate to local health councils and to National Health Service trusts in Scotland.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Wales)(No. 5) Amendment No. 3 Order 1990
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Wales) (No. 5) Order 1987, as amended by S.I. 1988/9, 9511329, 1680, 1989/2, 660, 1279, 1770, 1990/34 and 1161 contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent the human consumption of food which has been or which may have been rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the USSR.

items.title: The Education (Special Educational Needs) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Special Educational Needs) Regulations 1983 (“the principal Regulations”). In addition to minor and drafting amendments, they make the following changes of substance. They replace the terms “deaf” and “blind”, used in regulation 5 of the principal Regulations, with the terms “hearing impaired” and “visually impaired”, and make provision regarding the seeking of educational advice in respect of a child who is both hearing impaired and visually impaired (regulation 2(4) and (6)). They amend the definition of “qualified teacher” in regulation 5(4) of the principal Regulations in order to take account of the power in section 218 of the Education Reform Act 1988 to make regulations relating to the employment of teachers (regulation 2(5)). They amend regulation 10 of, and the Schedule to, the principal Regulations in order to take account of section 18 of that Act, so that a statement of a child’s special educational needs is to specify any modifications to, and any exclusions from the application of, the provisions of the National Curriculum, and the provision substituted for any such exclusions in order to maintain a balanced and broadly based curriculum (regulation 2(7) and (13)). They also amend regulation 10 of the principal Regulations so as to ensure that such a statement specifies any non-educational provision proposed to be made by the local education authority as well as any such provision to be made available by certain other specified bodies (regulation 2(9)). They permit a child’s statement of special educational needs to be disclosed to a local authority in their performance of certain duties, relating to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children, imposed on them by the Children Act 1989 (regulation 2(12)).

items.title: The Authorities for London Post-Graduate Teaching Hospitals (Constitution) Order 1990
items.description: This Order continues in being special health authorities for London post-graduate teaching hospitals originally established under Orders which are revoked by article 12 (article 3). It provides a new constitution for those authorities with officer and non-officer members (articles 5 to 8). Article 9 permits remuneration to be paid to non-officer members. Articles 10 and 11 make provisions relating to publicity for meetings and membership of nominees of those authorities of joint consultative committees within the meaning of section 22 of the National Health Service Act 1977.

items.title: The Authorities for London Post-Graduate Teaching Hospitals Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision in regulations 3, 6 and 7 relating to the appointment of members of the special health authorities for London post-graduate teaching hospitals continued in being by the Authorities for London Post-Graduate Teaching Hospitals (Constitution) Order 1990. They also make a number of specific provisions relating to the membership of those authorities — as to tenure of office (regulation 3), termination of tenure of office (regulation 4), joint membership (regulation 5)and eligibility for re-appointment (regulation 6)and a committee (regulation 7).

items.title: The Aerodromes (Designation) (Detention and Sale of Aircraft) Order 1990
items.description: Section 88 of the Civil Aviation Act 1982 empowers the person owning or managing an aerodrome to which the section applies to detain and sell aircraft where default is made in the payment of airport charges. Section 88(10), as amended by the Airports Act 1986, provides that the section applies to any aerodrome owned or managed by any government department or a local authority, other than a district council in Scotland, and to any other aerodrome designated for the purposes of the section by an order made by the Secretary of State. This Order designates the airports listed in the Schedule as aerodromes to which section 88 applies.

items.title: The Banking Act 1987 (Exempt Transactions) (Amendment No. 3) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Banking Act 1987 (Exempt Transactions) Regulations 1988 by inserting a new regulation 10A. This prescribes the acceptance by Students Loans Company Limited of a deposit made by a government department in furtherance of arrangements made by the Secretary of State under the Education (Student Loans) Act 1990, or by the Department of Education for Northern Ireland under the Education (Student Loans) (Northern Ireland) Order 1990, as a transaction to which the prohibition on deposit-taking imposed by section 3 of the Banking Act 1987 does not apply.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Transitional Provisions and Savings) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make transitional provisions and savings in connection with the coming into force of section 11(3) to (6) of the Social Security Act 1990.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in Technology) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in Technology) Order 1990 by substituting a new article 4 thereof.

items.title: The Assured Tenancies and Agricultural Occupancies (Forms) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the forms, prescribed by the Assured Tenancies and Agricultural Occupancies (Forms) Regulations 1988, for giving notice of an intention to grant an assured shorthold tenancy and for applying to a rent assessment committee under Part I of the Housing Act 1988. There are drafting amendments and amendments related to the abolition of domestic rating.

items.title: The Rainhill Stoops to Queensway Trunk Road (A568 Widnes Eastern Bypass and Slip Roads) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Rainhill Stoops to Queensway Trunk Road (A568 Widnes Eastern Bypass and Slip Roads) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Education (Access Funds) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st September 1990, empower the Secretary of State to pay grants (to be known as “access funds”) to the managers of certain educational institutions for the financial assistance of students at those institutions (regulations 1 and 2). Payments may be made at such times and in such instalments as the Secretary of State considers appropriate (regulation 3). Payment may be dependent on the fulfilment of conditions imposed by the Secretary of State (regulation 4). Institutions receiving access funds must keep records and accounts and furnish information, returns and accounts to the Secretary of State as he may require, repay unexpended funds as required, only assist those classes or categories of students which the Secretary of State determines and comply with such other requirements as he may determine (regulation 5).

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) Amendment (No.3) Order 1990
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) Order 1989 contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape in April 1986 of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the Ukraine, USSR.

items.title: The Life Assurance (Apportionment of Receipts of Participating Funds) (Applicable Percentage) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 17th August 1990, provides the method of determining “the applicable percentage” for the purposes of section 432E(3) of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Assessment Arrangements for English, Mathematics and Science) Order 1990
items.description: Section 4(1) and (2) of the Education Reform Act 1988 places a duty on the Secretary of State to establish the National Curriculum by specifying by order appropriate attainment targets, programmes of study and assessment arrangements for each of the foundation subjects. Attainment targets and programmes of study for pupils in English (in the first key stage), mathematics and science were specified by three Orders (S.I.s 1989/907, 308 and 309 respectively).

items.title: The Combined Probation Areas (Bedfordshire)Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Combined Probation Areas Order 1986 to take account of there-naming of the petty sessional division of North Bedfordshire as Bedford effected by the Petty Sessional Divisions (Bedfordshire) Order 1990 (S.I. 1990/1266) which comes into force on 1st August 1990.

items.title: The Education (Assisted Places) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Assisted Places) Regulations 1989 in respect of a school year beginning on or after 21st August 1990.

items.title: The Education (Assisted Places) (Incidental Expenses) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Assisted Places) (Incidental Expenses) Regulations 1989. They come into force on 21st August 1990 and apply to a school year beginning on or after that date.

items.title: The Education (Grants) (Music and Ballet Schools) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
The Social Security Benefits (Student Loans and Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Community Charge Benefits (General) Regulations 1989, the Family Credit (General) Regulations 1987, the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987, the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 and the Social Security (Unemployment, Sickness and Invalidity Benefit) Regulations 1983 with respect to students.

items.title: The Weights and Measures (Various Foods) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Weights and Measures (Intoxicating Liquor) Order (S.I. 1988/2039)and the Weights and Measures (Miscellaneous Foods) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/2040).

items.title: The School Pupil Records (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations require education authorities to give “appropriate persons” access to personal information in records held by them relating to school pupils, past and present, and requires them on request by such persons to correct any such information which is inaccurate. They provide also for certain exemptions from these requirements.

items.title: The Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (Commencement No. 9 and Saving) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (Commencement No. 9 and Saving) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Local Government (Committees and Political Groups) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision for securing the political balance on certain relevant authorities, committees and bodies and for determining the voting rights of members of certain committees.

items.title: The Justices of the Peace (Size and Chairmanship of Bench) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules consolidate, with amendments, the Justices of the Peace (Size and Chairmanship of Bench) Rules 1986 and the Justices of the Peace (Size and Chairmanship of Bench) (Amendment) Rules 1987.

items.title: The Education (Further and Higher Education Institutions Access Funds) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st September 1990, empower the Secretary of State to pay access funds to the governing bodies of certain institutions of further or higher education for the financial assistance of students at those institutions. (Regulations 1, 2 and 3 and the Schedule.) Payments may be made at such times and in such instalments as the Secretary of State considers appropriate. (Regulation 3(2).) Payment may be dependent on the fulfilment of conditions imposed by the Secretary of State. (Regulation 4.) Institutions receiving access funds must comply with requirements specified by the Secretary of State; in particular they must keep records and accounts and furnish information, returns and accounts to the Secretary of State as he may require, repay unexpended funds as required and only assist classes or categories of students specified by the Secretary of State. (Regulation 5.)

items.title: The Sheep Scab (National Dip) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Sheep Scab (National Dip) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Social Security (Recoupment) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: This instrument is made before the end of the period of 6 months beginning with the coming into force of the enactments under which the Regulations are made. The Regulations contained in this instrument are accordingly exempt by section 61(5) of the Social Security Act 1986 (c. 50), as amended by the Social Security Act 1989 (c. 24)Schedule 8, paragraph 12(3), from reference to the Social Security Advisory Committee and have not been so referred.

items.title: The Seed Potatoes (Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Scotland only and which revoke and supersede the Seed Potatoes (Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 1988, prescribe the fees payable in respect of certain matters arising under the Seed Potatoes Regulations 1984.

items.title: The Community Charges and Non-Domestic Rating (Demand Notices) (England) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations provide for the form and contents of community charge demand notices, and for the contents of rate demand notices, which are issued by charging authorities in England (other than the Common Council of the City of London), and for the information to be supplied when such notices are served by them.

items.title: The Further Education Student Records (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations require education authorities to give “appropriate persons” access to personal information in records held by them relating to further education students, past and present, and requires them on request by such persons to correct any such information which is inaccurate. They provide also for certain exemptions from these requirements.

items.title: The Education (Teachers) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These regulations make further amendments to the Education (Teachers) Regulations 1989, as amended by the Education (Teachers) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1989.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Control of Advertisements) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Town and Country Planning (Control of Advertisements) Regulations 1989 in consequence of the Town and Country Planning Act 1990. That Act, together with other enactments, consolidates and replaces the Town and Country Planning Act 1971 (c. 78) and certain related legislation, with effect from 24th August 1990.

items.title: The EEC Merger Control(Consequential Provisions) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision consequential upon Council Regulation (EEC)No. 4064/89 on the control of concentrations between undertakings (“the Merger Control Regulation”).

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Competition) (England) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision under Part I of the Local Government Act 1988 (“the Act”) in respect of the activity of managing sports and leisure facilities by specified authorities (defined in regulation 1(2)) in England.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemptions) (England) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemptions) (England) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/1372) provides that an activity listed in section 2(2) of the Local Government Act 1988 shall not be treated as a defined activity for the purpose of that Act so long as specified conditions are fulfilled.

items.title: The Central Rating Lists (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1990
items.description: Under the Central Rating Lists Regulations 1989 certain persons are designated, and descriptions of hereditaments are prescribed in relation to those persons, with a view to securing the central rating of those hereditaments, en bloc.

items.title: The London Docklands Development Corporation (Planning Functions) Order 1990
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the London Docklands Development Corporation (Planning Functions) Order 1981.

items.title: The Merseyside Development Corporation (Planning Functions) Order 1990
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the Merseyside Development Corporation (Planning Functions) Order 1981.

items.title: The A20 Trunk Road (Sidcup Road, Greenwich) (Prohibition of Use of Gap in Central Reservation) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A20 Trunk Road (Sidcup Road, Greenwich) (Prohibition of Use of Gap in Central Reservation) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Local Elections (Parishes and Communities) (Amendment) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules amend the Local Elections (Parishes and Communities) Rules 1986. Rules 2(4), 3(4) and (5) and 4(5) and (6) modify the declarations of identity for use in connection with postal voting (and also such use at various combined elections). Rule 2(5) amends the official poll card in an attempt to clarify the use to which it may be put. Rules 2(2), 3(2) and (3) and 4(2) to (4) correct errors or omissions in the 1986 Rules.

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1988 (Confiscation Orders) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends Part I of Schedule 4 to the Criminal Justice Act 1988 to add certain offences to the offences listed there in respect of which magistrates' courts may make confiscation orders under section 71 of that Act. The added offences are offences under paragraph 10 of Schedule 12 to the London Government Act 1963, section 4 of the Private Places of Entertainment (Licensing) Act 1967 and paragraph 12 of Schedule 1 to the Local Government (Miscellaneous Provisions) Act 1982, all of which relate to the licensing of music and dancing entertainments. In each case the offences are those concerning the use of an unlicensed place for an entertainment or the use of a licensed place for an entertainment in contravention of any term or condition of the licence which limits the number of people who may be present at the entertainment. These are the offences for which the penalties were increased by the Entertainments (Increased Penalties) Act 1990.

items.title: The Police Cadets (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Police Cadets (Scotland) Regulations 1968.

items.title: The Police (Amendment No. 3) Regulations 1990
The Police Cadets (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Police Cadets Regulations 1979. Regulation 3(a) and (b) increase the pay of cadets attached to the Metropolitan Police force from 1st July 1989 and again from 1st September 1989 and of cadets elsewhere from 1st September 1989 (retrospective effect is authorised by section 33(4), as applied by section 35(2), of the Police Act 1964). Regulation 3(c) increases charges payable by cadets for board and lodging provided by police authorities.

items.title: The Police Federation (Amendment) Regulations 1990 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police Federation Regulations 1969. Regulations 3 and 7 amend the provision concerning branch board members who hold certain offices. New regulation 15A (inserted by regulation 4 of these Regulations) authorises the Police Federation for England and Wales to make rules relating to the matters specified in paragraph (1) thereof. Regulations 5 and 6 of these Regulations make fresh provision in relation to the raising of funds by the Federation and the use of such funds respectively, so as to give the Federation greater control over those matters. Regulation 4 of these Regulations also revises the provision concerning the defrayment from Federation funds of the emoluments of officers of the Federation.

items.title: The Prevention of Terrorism (Temporary Provisions)(Designated Ports) Order 1990
items.description: This Order removes Southampton from the list of seaports designated, for the purposes of paragraph 8 of Schedule 5 to the Prevention of Terrorism (Temporary Provisions) Act 1989, in Part I of Schedule 6 to that Act.

items.title: The Local Elections (Principal Areas) (Amendment) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules amend the Local Elections (Principal Areas) Rules 1986 which after 1st April 1990 will apply in respect of the election of county, district or London borough councillors in England and Wales. Rules 2, 3, 4(3), (5) to (7), (8)(a), (9), (10)(a) and (12), 5(2) to (5), (6)(a), (7), (8), (9)(a) and (c), (10)~ to (15) and (18) and 6(2), (3), (5)(b), (6) and (10) of these Rules are consequential on the repeal of the provisions in primary legislation relating to elections to the Inner London Education Authority (see Part I of Schedule 13 to the Education Reform Act 1988 (c. 40).

items.title: The Limited Partnerships (Unrestricted Size) No. 2 Regulations 1990
items.description: The Limited Partnerships Act 1907 provides that a limited partnership shall not consist of more than twenty persons. These Regulations exempt from that prohibition partnerships formed for the purpose of carrying on business as insurance brokers where each partner is either a registered insurance broker or an enrolled body corporate within the meaning of the Insurance Brokers (Registration) Act 1977.

items.title: The Partnerships (Unrestricted Size) No. 6 Regulations 1990
items.description: Section 716(1) of the Companies Act 1985 prohibits the formation of partnerships consisting of more than twenty persons. These Regulations exempt from that prohibition partnerships formed for the purpose of carrying on business as insurance brokers where each partner is either a registered insurance broker or an enrolled body corporate within the meaning of the Insurance Brokers (Registration)Act 1977.

items.title: The Industrial Training Levy (Road Transport) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st August 1991, gives effect to proposals of the Road Transport Industry Training Board which were submitted to the Secretary of State. The proposals are for the imposition of a levy on employers in the road transport industry for the purpose of raising money towards meeting the expenses of the Board.

items.title: The Redundancy Payments (Merchant Seamen Exclusion)Order 1973 (Revocation) Order 1990
items.description: This Order revokes the Redundancy Payments (Merchant Seamen Exclusion) Order 1973 (“the 1973 Order”) which excluded certain employments as a merchant seaman from the redundancy payments provisions of the Employment Protection (Consolidation) Act 1978. The effect of the revocation is to apply those provisions to merchant seamen to whom the 1973 Order applied.

items.title: The Milk and Dairies and Milk (Special Designation) (Charges) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to England and Wales, revoke and re-enact with modifications the Milk and Dairies and Milk (Special Designation) (Charges) Regulations 1987 as amended. The Regulations impose revised charges for visits to dairy farms by persons authorised by the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food and the Secretary of State for Wales for the purpose of ascertaining whether provisions of the Milk and Dairies (General) Regulations 1959 and the Milk (Special Designation) Regulations 1989 are being complied with. There is provision for charging at a lower rate in specified circumstances where the number of cows kept for milking purposes is less than 20. The charges are set out in the Table below:–MatterCharge under 1987 RegulationsCharge under these RegulationsA1Dairy farm visit other than a dairy farm visit for the sole purpose of taking samples of milk or of water or both:ahigher rate of charge;£78.00£87.00lower rate of charge. £60.00£67.00A2First further dairy farm visit arising out of the finding of a dairy farm visit in A1:ahigher rate of charge;–£87.00lower rate of charge. –£67.00A3Subsequent further dairy farm visit as in A2:ahigher rate of charge;£78.00£87.00lower rate of charge. £60.00£67.00B1Dairy farm visit for the sole purpose of taking samples of milk or of water or both.£26.00£35.00B2First follow-up visit.––B3Each subsequent follow-up visit.–£23.00

items.title: The Special Road (Glan Conwy to Conwy Morfa) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations provide for the regulation of traffic using the lengths of the special road described in Schedules 1 and 2, from Glan Conwy to Conwy Morfa in the County of Gwynedd. Use of the adjoining lengths of the special road from Llanddulas to Colwyn Bay and Colwyn Bay to Glan Conwy is regulated by the Special Road (Llanddulas to Colwyn Bay) Regulations 1984 (S.I. 1984/1719) and the Special Road (Colwyn Bay to Glan Conwy) Regulations 1985 (S.I. 1985/824) respectively. Except as provided in these Regulations, the road can be used only by traffic of Classes I, II and IV, as defined in Schedule 4 to the Highways Act 1980. The special road is not a motorway and therefore the provisions of the Motorways Traffic (England and Wales) Regulations 1982 (S.I. 1982/1163) do not apply to it.

items.title: The General Medical Council Preliminary Proceedings Committee and Professional Conduct Committee (Procedure) (Amendment) Rules Order of Council 1990
items.description: The Rules approved by this Order amend the General Medical Council Preliminary Proceedings Committee and Professional Conduct Committee (Procedure) Rules 1988 (annexed to S.I. 1988/2255) by providing for the nomination of a lay member of the Council to assist in the initial consideration of complaints under those Rules.

items.title: The Export of Goods (Control) (Amendment No. 4) Order 1990
items.description: This Order further amends the Export of Goods (Control) Order 1989 (“the Order”).

items.title: The Diseases of Animals (Approved Disinfectants)(Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order further amends the Diseases of Animals (Approved Disinfectants) Order 1978 by adding to the list of approved disinfectants in that Order those newly approved disinfectants which are mentioned in Schedule 1 to this Order (article 3(1))and by deleting from that list the disinfectant mentioned in Schedule 2 (article 3(2)). The disinfectant deleted may, however, continue to be used as approved disinfectant until 31st December 1990 (article 3(3)and (4)).

items.title: The Avon, Somerset and Wiltshire (County Boundaries) Order 1990
items.description: This Order transfers areas between the counties of Avon, Somerset and Wiltshire. Consequential changes to districts, parishes and local government electoral areas are made.

items.title: The Meat and Livestock Commission Levy (Variation) Scheme (Confirmation) Order 1990
items.description: By this Order the Ministers confirm the Meat and Livestock Commission Levy (Variation) Scheme 1990 which replaces the variation Scheme confirmed by the Meat and Livestock Commission Levy (Variation) Scheme (Confirmation) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/838) and further varies the Meat and Livestock Commission Levy Scheme 1987 confirmed by the Meat and Livestock Commission Levy Scheme (Confirmation) Order 1987 (S.I. 1987/1303) (“the Scheme”). Under the Scheme charges are imposed on slaughterers and exporters (including producers who export livestock) to ensure effective collection. However, the Commission is authorised to determine that slaughterers may recover from producers from whom they have purchased animals one half of the charges payable in respect of General Expenses and all charges payable in respect of Species Promotion Expenses. Producers thereby contribute to the financing of the Commission via the recovery by slaughterers of levy paid by them.

items.title: The Control of Gold, Securities, Payments and Credits (Kuwait) Directions 1990
items.description: These Directions prohibit (except with Treasury permission) any recipient in the United Kingdom, the Channel Islands or the Isle of Man of an order from the government of or any person resident in Kuwait from carrying out that order insofar as the order (a) requires the recipient to make any payment or to part with any gold or securities or (b) requires any change to be made in the persons to whose credit any sum is to stand or to whose order any gold or securities is to be held.

items.title: The Plant Breeders' Rights (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Plant Breeders' Rights Regulations 1978.

items.title: The Plant Breeders' Rights (Festulolium) Scheme 1990
items.description: This Scheme, made under the Plant Varieties and Seeds Act 1964 prescribes all varieties of Festulolium as varieties in respect of which grants of plant breeders' rights may be made and also prescribes the period of twenty-five years as that during which the rights may be exercised (paragraphs 2 and 3).

items.title: The Plant Breeders' Rights (Miscellaneous Ornamental Plants) Scheme 1990
items.description: This Scheme, made under the Plant Varieties and Seeds Act 1964, prescribes the varieties of miscellaneous ornamental plants in respect of which grants of plant breeders' rights may be made and also prescribes the period of 20 years as that during which the rights may be exercised (paragraphs 2and 3and Schedule).

items.title: The Plant Breeders' Rights (Oil and Fibre Plants) (Variation) Scheme 1990
items.description: This Scheme varies the Plant Breeders' Rights (Oil and Fibre Plants) Scheme 1980 (as varied) by adding borage (Borago Officinalis L.), turnip rape (Brassica rapa L. var silvestris (Lam.) Briggs), coriander (Coriandrum sativum(L) ) and sunflower (Helianthus annus L.) to the species of which plant varieties may be the subject of plant breeders' rights under Part I of the Plant Varieties and Seeds Act 1964. The period prescribed as that during which rights in each of those species may be exercised is 20 years. During that period infringements of the rights in the registered name of a variety of borage, coriander or sunflower may be the subject of legal proceedings when committed in connection with any such variety.

items.title: The Plant Breeders' Rights (Trees, Shrubs and Woody Climbers) (Variation) Scheme 1990
items.description: This Scheme varies the Plant Breeders' Rights (Trees, Shrubs and Woody Climbers) Scheme 1969 by extending (in paragraph 2) the schedule of genera and species of which plant varieties may be the subject of plant breeders' rights under Part I of the Plant Varieties and Seeds Act 1964 to cover all species of the genus Cornus L., thirty years being prescribed as the period during which the rights may be exercised. During that period infringements of the rights in the registered name of a variety of Cornus L. may be the subject of legal proceedings when committed in connection with any variety of Cornus.

items.title: The Dartford—Thurrock Crossing Tolls Order 1990
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st September 1990, fixes the amounts of the tolls to be paid to use the Dartford—Thurrock Crossing, presently consisting of the Dartford Tunnels.

items.title: The Dartford—Thurrock Crossing (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Dartford—Thurrock Crossing Regulations 1989 in three ways. First, by increasing the charges to be made in respect of vehicles above a specified width or length which use the tunnels. (These charges are in addition to any tolls which may be levied.) Secondly, by increasing some (not all) of the minimum and additional charges which are used to calculate the fee payable under regulation 12 of the 1989 Regulations in respect of attendance at or removal of a vehicle which has broken down in the tunnels. Thirdly, by defining a “large break-down vehicle”, a term used in Regulation 12(1) of the 1989 Regulations.

items.title: The Education (Bursaries for Teacher Training) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Bursaries for Teacher Training) Regulations 1988. They empower the Secretary of State to pay training bursaries in respect of courses of initial teacher training which begin on or after 1st September 1990and of which the principal subject matter is either Welsh or a specified modern foreign language. A drafting amendment has been made to regulation 5 (c).

items.title: The Household Appliances (Noise Emission) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive 86/594/EEC on airborne noise emitted by household appliances.

items.title: The Control of Gold, Securities, Payments and Credits (Republic of Iraq) Directions 1990
items.description: These Directions prohibit (except with Treasury permission) any recipient in the United Kingdom, the Channel Islands or the Isle of Man of an order from the government of or any person resident in the Republic of Iraq from carrying out that order insofar as the order (a) requires the recipient to make any payment or to part with any gold or securities or (b) requires any change to be made in the persons to whose credit any sum is to stand or to whose order any gold or securities is to be held.

items.title: The Portsmouth Water (Constitution and Regulation) Order 1990
items.description: Section 101(2) of the Water Act 1989 provides for the replacement of provisions relating to the constitution and regulation of a statutory water company contained in its local Acts and Orders by provisions contained in a memorandum and articles which have been approved by special resolution of the members of the company. The replacement provisions have effect only if they are approved by the Secretary of State and come into force on such date as he determines.

items.title: The River Deveron Salmon Fishery District (Baits and Lures) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations specify natural prawns and shrimps and any part of them for the purposes of the definition of “rod and line” in section 24(1) of the Salmon and Freshwater Fisheries (Protection) (Scotland) Act 1951. They apply throughout the River Deveron salmon fishery district for the whole of each year. The effect is to prohibit the use of natural prawns and shrimps (or any part of them) as bait when fishing by rod and line for salmon and sea trout.

items.title: The A45 Trunk Road (Coventry Road and Daventry Road, Dunchurch) (De-Trunking) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A45 Trunk Road (Coventry Road and Daventry Road, Dunchurch) (De-Trunking) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Standard Community Charge and Non-Domestic Rating (Definition of Domestic Property) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the definition of domestic property applicable for the purposes of section 3 (standard community charge) and Part III (non-domestic rating) of the Local Government Finance Act 1988.

items.title: The M45 Motorway (Thurlaston to Dunchurch Section) Connecting Roads Scheme 1990
items.description: This version of the The M45 Motorway (Thurlaston to Dunchurch Section) Connecting Roads Scheme 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Nurses, Midwives and Health Visitors (Midwives Training) Amendment Rules Approval Order 1990
items.description: The Rules approved by this Order, and made by the United Kingdom Central Council for Nursing, Midwifery and Health Visiting, further amend the Rules approved by the Nurses, Midwives and Health Visitors Rules Approval Order 1983 (“the principal Rules”) as they relate to the training of student midwives for entry to Part 10 of the register of nurses, midwives and health visitors.

items.title: The Caribbean Territories (Control of Gold, Securities, Payments and Credits: Kuwait and Republic of Iraq)Order 1990
items.description: This Order prohibits persons in the Caribbean territories mentioned in section 2 from giving effect to any directions to make payments or part with any gold or securities or to make any change in the persons to whose credit any sum is to stand or to whose order any gold or security is to be held if the directions are given by or on behalf of the Governments of Kuwait or the Republic of Iraq or by or on behalf of any persons resident in those countries unless the transaction is permitted by the Governor.

items.title: The Potato Marketing Scheme (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order reduces the size of the Potato Marketing Board, alters the boundaries of its electoral districts and lengthens the terms of office of elected Board Members. The Joint Consultative Committee is reconstituted under an Independent Chairman so as to increase the involvement of consumer and other non-producer interests in market management decisions and other matters referred to it. Certain Committees and obsolete powers of the Board are abolished. Provision is made for the Board to charge VAT on producers' contributions. The deposit paid by candidates standing for election to the Board is increased and the qualification required of candidates and members of the Board is amended to reflect changes in the statutory definition of insolvency. Other minor and consequential amendments to the Potato Marketing Scheme 1955 are made.

items.title: The Education (Mandatory Awards) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st September 1990, supersede the Education (Mandatory Awards) Regulations 1989 which are revoked (regulation 6(1)).

items.title: The Trade Effluents (Prescribed Processes and Substances) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Trade Effluents (Prescribed Processes and Substances) Regulations 1989 (the “1989 Regulations”). The main changes are as follows.

items.title: The Gas (Alternative Method of Charge) Regulations 1990
items.description: The Regulations provide that, as an alternative to the standard method of charge provided for by section 12 of the Gas Act 1986 (whereby the number of therms supplied to a customer is calculated on the basis of the declared calorific value of the gas), the number of therms supplied by a public gas supplier may be calculated on the basis of actual calorific values of gas determined by the supplier in accordance with the Regulations. It is provided that the places and the times at which, and the manner in which, determinations of actual calorific values are to be made are to be those specified in directions made by the Secretary of State.

items.title: The Gas (Testing of Apparatus and Equipment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations apply to public gas suppliers, in so far as the number of therms supplied by them is calculated on the basis of actual calorific values of the gas (“relevant suppliers”), who have been directed by the Secretary of State pursuant to the Gas (Alternative Method of Charge) Regulations 1990 to provide and maintain premises, apparatus or equipment for the purpose of making determinations of actual calorific values of gas supplied by them.

items.title: The Local Government (Assistants for Political Groups) (Remuneration) Order 1990
items.description: One of the requirements relating to the terms on which a person is appointed to or holds an appointment in pursuance of section 9 (assistants for political groups) of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 is that the annual rate of remuneration for the post is less than £13,500 or such higher amount as the Secretary of State may specify.

items.title: The College Councils (Scotland) (No.2) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision in respect of the establishment of new college

items.title: The National Health Service (General Dental Services) (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Dental Services) Regulations 1973 (“the principal Regulations”) which regulate the terms on which general dental services are provided under the National Health Service Act 1977, and make consequential and associated amendments to the Regulations concerning charges for dental treatment and appliances and service committee and other disciplinary procedures. The principal changes made by these Regulations are as follows.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum)(Assessment Arrangements in English, Welsh, Mathematics and Science) (Wales) Order 1990
items.description: Section 4(2) of the Education Reform Act 1988 places a duty on the Secretary of State to establish the National Curriculum by specifying by order appropriate attainment targets, programmes of study and assessment arrangements for each of the foundation subjects. Attainment targets and programmes of study for pupils in English, Welsh, mathematics and science were specified by four Orders (S.I.s 1989/907, 1990/1082, 1989/308 and 1989/309). The provisions of this Order only relate to the assessment of Welsh and not Welsh Second Language (article 2(1)). S.I. 1990/1082 sets out different attainment targets and programmes of study for Welsh and for Welsh Second Language.

items.title: The Electricity Supply Pension Scheme (Eligible Persons) Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Electricity Supply Pension Scheme (Eligible Persons) Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Export of Goods (Control) (Iraq and Kuwait Sanctions) Order 1990
items.description: This Order prohibits the export, without a licence under the Order from the Secretary of State, of all goods to Iraq and Kuwait, or to any other destination where the goods are to be delivered to a person for the purposes of a business carried on in or operated from Iraq or Kuwait.

items.title: The Sea Fish Industry Authority (Levy Powers) Order 1990
items.description: Section 4 of the Fisheries Act 1981 enables the Sea Fish Industry Authority (“the Authority”) by regulations confirmed by order to impose a levy on persons engaged in the sea fish industry. This Order amends the maximum rate at which the levy may be imposed in respect of the weight of sea fish or sea fish products landed in the United Kingdom or trans-shipped within British fishery limits. The rate is increased from 0.8p per kilogram to 2p per kilogram in the case of sea fish. In relation to any sea fish product the rate must be such that its yield will not in the opinion of the Authority exceed the yield from a levy at the rate of 2p per kilogram (previously 0.8p per kilogram) on the sea fish required on average (whether alone or together with any other substance or article) to produce a kilogram of that product.

items.title: The Anglian Regional Flood Defence Committee (Appointed Day) Order 1990
items.description: Section 137 of the Water Act 1989 contains provisions for the establishment, as from the1st September 1989 (“the transfer date”),of regional flood defence committees in place of regional land drainage committees. Subsection (4) of that section makes provision for the membership of a regional flood defence committee during a period beginning with the transfer date and ending with a date appointed by, in the case of a regional flood defence committee in England, the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food.

items.title: The Anglian Regional Flood Defence Committee Order 1990
items.description: Section 138(2) of the Water Act 1989 (hereinafter called “the Act”) provides that the total number of members of a regional flood defence committee of the National Rivers Authority shall not be less than eleven or more than seventeen unless a determination that the total number of such members should be increased is submitted by the National Rivers Authority to the appropriate Minister (in the case of the Anglian Regional Flood Defence Committee, the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food) under section 138(3) of the Act, and is confirmed by the Minister. Where the total number of members is so increased, the Minister is required by section 138(5) of the Act by Order to specify the number of members to be appointed to the committee by each of the constituent councils for the committee. The total number to be so appointed is required by section 138(6) of the Act to exceed by one the number of the other members of the committee (appointed by the Minister and the National Rivers Authority).

items.title: The North West Regional Flood Defence Committee (Appointed Day) Order 1990
items.description: Section 137 of the Water Act 1989 contains provisions for the establishment, as from the 1st September 1989 (“the transfer date”), of regional flood defence committees in place of regional land drainage committees. Subsection (4) of that section makes provision for the membership of a regional flood defence committee during a period beginning with the transfer date and ending with a date appointed by, in the case of a regional flood defence committee in England, the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food.

items.title: The Severn-Trent Regional Flood Defence Committee (Appointed Day) Order 1990
items.description: Section 137 of the Water Act 1989 contains provisions for the establishment, as from the1st September 1989 (“the transfer date”),of regional flood defence committees in place of regional land drainage committees. Subsection (4) of that section makes provision for the membership of a regional flood defence committee during a period beginning with the transfer date and ending with a date appointed by, in the case of a regional flood defence committee for an area, the greater part of which is in England, the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food.

items.title: The Wessex Regional Flood Defence Committee (Appointed Day) Order 1990
items.description: Section 137 of the Water Act 1989 contains provisions for the establishment, as from the 1st September 1989 (“the transfer date”), of regional flood defence committees in place of regional land drainage committees. Subsection (4) of that section makes provision for the membership of a regional flood defence committee during a period beginning with the transfer date and ending with a date appointed by, in the case of a regional flood defence committee in England, the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food.

items.title: The Wessex Regional Flood Defence Committee Order 1990
items.description: By virtue of section 138(5) of the Water Act 1989 (“the Act”), the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food (in relation to regional flood defence committees in England) must specify the number of members to be appointed to a regional flood defence committee by each of the constituent councils for the committee where (inter alia) the total number of members of the Committee is varied.

items.title: The Cambridge, East Surrey, East Worcestershire and Yorkshire (Pipelaying and Other Works)(Codes of Practice) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Cambridge, East Surrey, East Worcestershire and Yorkshire (Pipelaying and Other Works)(Codes of Practice) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A12 Trunk Road (Eastern Avenue, Redbridge) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A12 Trunk Road (Eastern Avenue, Redbridge) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Iraq and Kuwait (United Nations Sanctions) Order 1990
items.description: This Order imposes restrictions pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No. 661 of 6th August 1990, on the exportation of goods from Iraq and Kuwait and on supply of goods to Iraq and Kuwait as well as certain related activities and dealings, including the carriage of such goods in British ships or aircraft. The Order also makes provision for the investigation of ships and aircraft that are suspected of contravening the Order.

items.title: The Iraq and Kuwait (United Nations Sanctions) (Dependent Territories) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, applies to each of the territories specified in Schedule 1. It prohibits the importation into the territory of certain goods exported from Iraq or Kuwait and the exportation from the territory of certain goods intended for Iraq or Kuwait. It also prohibits certain activities and dealings relating to the exportation from Iraq or Kuwait of the former goods and the supply to Iraq or Kuwait of the latter goods, including carriage of these goods in British ships or aircraft. It also makes provision for the investigation of ships and aircraft that are suspected of contravening this Order. It confers certain powers relating to the obtaining of evidence and information for the purposes of the Order.

items.title: The Education (Inner London Education Authority) (Transitional and Supplementary Provisions) (No. 3) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Education (Areas to which Pupils and Students Belong) Regulations 1989 in consequence of the abolition of the Inner London Education Authority (“ILEA”).

items.title: The Secretary of State’s Traffic Orders (Procedure) (England and Wales) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations lay down the procedure to be followed by the Secretary of State for Transport and the Secretary of State for Wales in connection with the making by them of the main types of traffic and parking place orders under the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984. The Regulations restate with modifications the procedures formerly set out in the Secretary of State’s Traffic Orders (Procedure) (England and Wales) Regulations 1986 (S.I. 1986/180). Apart from some changes of substance, referred to below, the drafting has been modified to reflect the provisions of the Local Authorities' Traffic Orders (Procedure) (England and Wales) Regulations 1989 (S.I. 1989/1120).

items.title: The Income-Related Benefits Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987 and the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 by providing for deaf students entitled to certain educational awards because of their disability, to be eligible for housing benefit and income support; and by providing for the disregard on student loans to be subject to the overall £10 limit on income disregards (regulations 4 and 5). The Regulations also amend the Family Credit (General) Regulations 1987 and the Community Charge Benefits (General) Regulations 1989 in this latter respect (regulations 2 and 3).

items.title: The Mid Kent Water (Constitution and Regulation) Order 1990
items.description: Section 101(2) of the Water Act 1989 provides for the replacement of provisions relating to the constitution and regulation of a statutory water company contained in its local Acts and Orders by provisions contained in a memorandum and articles which have been approved by special resolution of the members of the company. The replacement provisions have effect only if they are approved by the Secretary of State and come into force on such date as he determines.

items.title: The Portsmouth Flathouse Quay Harbour Revision Order 1990
items.description: This Order empowers Portsmouth City Council as the harbour authority for the Flathouse Quay to construct works extending the Quay and confers on the Harbour Master powers to control vessels using the Quay. The Order also authorises the Council, to dredge the channel giving access to the works and permits the Council to lease the works once completed. The Applicant for the Order is Portsmouth City Council.

items.title: The Merger (Fees) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations provide for fees to be payable in connection with the exercise by the Secretary of State, the Director General of Fair Trading and the Monopolies and Mergers Commission of their functions under Part V of the Fair Trading Act 1973 (“the Act”) relating to the supervision of actual and prospective mergers.

items.title: The National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) Amendment No. 2 Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) Amendment No. 2 Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) (Scotland) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1990
