items.description: This version of the The National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) (Scotland) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Heavy Metal Poisoning) (England) Order 1990
items.description: This Order prohibits the movement of animals and anything derived from them from a designated area (articles 3 and 4 and the Schedule). They are likely to create a hazard to human health because they may be contaminated (article 2).

items.title: The Accounting Standards (Prescribed Body) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the Accounting Standards Board Limited for the purposes of section 256(1) of the Companies Act 1985. Statements of standard accounting practice issued by a body so prescribed are “accounting standards” for the purposes of the provisions of Part VII of that Act. (Accounts prepared under Part VII are required to disclose whether or not they comply with accounting standards.)

items.title: The Import and Export (Plant Health Fees) (Forestry) (Great Britain) Order 1990
items.description: This Order prescribes fees in respect of the services listed in Schedules 1, 2 and 3 to the Order (articles 3 to 7). These services are performed in connection with applications received for the issue of phytosanitary certificates or licences in accordance with the provisions of the Plant Health (Forestry) (Great Britain) Order 1989. Certificates are required to accompany exports of certain wood, wood products, seeds, forest tree plants, soil and used forestry machinery. Licences are issued to enable the importation of articles which would otherwise be prohibited.

items.title: The Professions Supplementary to Medicine (Registration Rules) (Amendment) Order of Council 1990
items.description: The Rules approved by this Order increase with effect from 1st October 1990 the registration fees, retention fees and restoration fees payable to the Boards established under the Professions Supplementary to Medicine Act 1960.

items.title: The Bristol, Rickmansworth, Sunderland and South Shields and Sutton (Pipelaying and Other Works) (Codes of Practice) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Bristol, Rickmansworth, Sunderland and South Shields and Sutton (Pipelaying and Other Works) (Codes of Practice) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Movements of Capital (Required Information) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 3rd September 1990, specify the information which is required to be given to the Commissioners of Inland Revenue (“the Board”) by section 765A(2)(a) of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (“the 1988 Act”) within six months of the carrying out of a relevant transaction. A relevant transaction is a movement of capital to which Article 1 of the Directive of the Council of the European Communities of 24th June 1988 for the implementation of Article 67 of the EEC treaty (No.88/361/EEC) applies and which would be unlawful under section 765(1) of the 1988 Act if that Article did not apply to it. The movements of capital in question are those taking place on and after 1st July 1990 between persons resident in member States of the European Community which, if that Article did not apply to them, would require the consent of the Treasury given specially under that section.

items.title: The Telecommunication Apparatus (Approval Fees) (British Approvals Board for Telecommunications) Order 1990
items.description: This Order provides for the charging of fees in respect of approval of the apparatus specified in the Schedule to the Order for connection to the telecommunication systems specified in the approval. Licences granted under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 may require apparatus which is connected, whether directly or indirectly, to public telecommunication systems to be approved for such connection under section 22 of the Act.

items.title: The Yorkshire Water Authority (Reconstitution of the Keyingham Level Drainage Board) Order 1989
items.description: This Order, made by the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food, confirms, with modifications, a Scheme submitted to him by the Yorkshire Water Authority making provision for the amendment of the method of constituting the Drainage Authority commonly known as the Keyingham Level Drainage Board constituted under a local Act of Parliament of 1802 as amended.

items.title: The Yorkshire Water Authority (Reconstitution of the Muston and Yedingham Drainage Board) Order 1989
items.description: This Order, made by the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food, confirms, with modifications, a Scheme submitted to him by the Yorkshire Water Authority making provision for the amendment of the method of constituting the Drainage Authority commonly known as the Muston and Yedingham Drainage Board constituted by a local Act of Parliament of 1800 as amended.

items.title: The Yorkshire Water Authority (Reconstitution of the Reedness and Swinefleet Drainage Board) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, made by the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food, confirms, with modifications, a Scheme submitted to him by the Yorkshire Water Authority making provision for the amendment of the method of constituting the Drainage Authority commonly known as the Reedness and Swinefleet Drainage Board constituted by the Reedness and Swinefleet Drainage Act 1884.

items.title: The Yorkshire Water Authority (Reconstitution of the Wilberfoss and Thornton Level Drainage Board) Order 1989
items.description: This Order, made by the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food, confirms, with modifications, a Scheme submitted to him by the Yorkshire Water Authority making provision for the amendment of the method of constituting the Drainage Authority commonly known as the Wilberfoss and Thornton Level Drainage Board constituted under the Land Drainage Provisional Order Confirmation Act 1915.

items.title: The Yorkshire Water Authority (Reconstitution of the Winestead Level Drainage Board) Order 1989
items.description: This Order, made by the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food, confirms, with modifications, a Scheme submitted to him by the Yorkshire Water Authority making provision for the amendment of the method of constituting the Drainage Authority commonly known as the Winestead Level Drainage Board constituted by the Winestead Level Drainage Act 1867.

items.title: The Camden (Waiting and Loading Restriction) (Amendment No. 106) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Camden (Waiting and Loading Restriction) (Amendment No. 106) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Rules of the Supreme Court (Amendment No. 2) 1990
items.description: This version of the The Rules of the Supreme Court (Amendment No. 2) 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas Designation (Wales) (Welsh Language Provisions) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State for Wales has made a series of Orders, namely the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Cambrian Mountains) Designation Order 1986, the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Cambrian Mountains — Extension) Designation Order 1987 and the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Lleyn Peninsula) Designation Order 1987, which designated areas in the Cambrian Mountains and the Lleyn Peninsula as environmentally sensitive areas. Each of those Orders, as amended by S.I. 1988/173, specified requirements (“the requirements”) as to agricultural practices, methods and operations and the installation and use of equipment which must be included in an agreement entered into by the Secretary of State under section 18(3) of the Agriculture Act 1986. The Order designating land in the Lleyn Peninsula also specifies additional provisions (“additional provisions”) as to agricultural practices, methods and operations and the installation and use of equipment which may be included in an agreement. Each of the above-mentioned Orders contains definitions (“definitions”) of terms to be used in the requirements and additional provisions.

items.title: The Protection of Wrecks (Designation No. 2)Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates as a restricted area for the purposes of the Protection of Wrecks Act 1973 an area round the site of what is thought to be the wreck of a vessel near the Shetland Islands which is of historical and archaeological importance.

items.title: The Building Societies (Transfer of Business) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations which are made under section 102 of the Building Societies Act 1986 replace regulations 9 and 10 of the Building Societies (Transfer of Business) Regulations 1988 with substituted regulations. Regulations 9 and 10 relate to the rights of members of a building society, the business of which is transferred, under the provisions of section 97 of the Act, to a company specially formed for the purpose, to receive a priority distribution out of the assets of the sucessor company in the event of its being wound up.

items.title: The Agricultural Holdings (Units of Production) Order 1990
items.description: This Order prescribes units of production for the assessment of the productive capacity of agricultural land and sets out the amount which is to be regarded as the net annual income from each such unit for the year 12th September 1990 to 11th September 1991 inclusive. In so far as the units of production prescribed in the Order are relevant to the assessment they are to be used, in conjunction with the net annual income ascribed to them, in calculating the net annual income of the unit of land when assessing whether it is a commercial unit of agricultural land for the purposes of the succession provisions in Part IV of the Agricultural Holdings Act 1986, that is to say a unit of agricultural land which is capable, when farmed under competent management, of producing a net annual income not less than the aggregate of the average annual earnings of two full-time, male agricultural workers aged 20 or over, (see paragraph 3(1) of Schedule 6 to the Act). Therefore the assessment will not necessarily be based on the system of farming carried out on the holding or on historical data from the holding.

items.title: The Patents (Fees) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules revoke and replace the Patents (Fees) Rules 1989. All fees payable under the Patents Act 1977 with the exception of those on forms 1/77, 20/77, 40/77, 41/77, 44/77, 49/77 and, under the Patents Act 1949 on forms 8, 23, 33, 36, 39, 43, 44, 45, 46, 53, 54, 55, 64, 65 and 68 and in respect of certain forms dealing with international applications are increased with effect from 10th September 1990. It is estimated that the total amount of fees collected per annum at the new levels will be 8 per cent greater than the amount collected per annum at the existing levels.

items.title: The Registered Designs (Fees) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules revoke and replace the Registered Designs (Fees) Rules 1989. The fees payable under the Registered Designs Act 1949 on forms 2A, 7, 8, 9A, 12A, 18, 25A and 31 are increased with effect from 10th September 1990. It is estimated that the total amount of fees collected per annum at the new levels will be 7½ per cent greater than the amount collected per annum at existing levels.

items.title: The Design Right (Proceedings before Comptroller)(Amendment) (No. 2) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules replace Schedule 2 to the Design Right (Proceedings before Comptroller) Rules 1989 to increase (by 8 per cent) from £50 to £54 the fees for the matters referred to in items 1, 3 and 4.

items.title: The Merseyside Residuary Body (Winding Up) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order corrects two errors in the Merseyside Residuary Body (Winding Up) Order 1989 (S.I. 1989/2470).

items.title: The Gloucestershire and Wiltshire (County Boundaries) Order 1990
items.description: This Order transfers areas from the county of Wiltshire and the district of North Wiltshire to the county of Gloucestershire and the district of Cotswold. Consequential changes to parishes and local government electoral areas are made.

items.title: The New Towns (Transfer of Housing Stock) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision under section 172 of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 for the transfer of new town housing stock.

items.title: The A41 Trunk Road (Finchley Road, Camden) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A41 Trunk Road (Finchley Road, Camden) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A41 Trunk Road (Finchley Road, Camden) (Box Junctions) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A41 Trunk Road (Finchley Road, Camden) (Box Junctions) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Companies Act 1989 (Commencement No. 7 and Transitional and Saving Provisions) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force certain of the provisions of the Companies Act 1989 (the 1989 Act).

items.title: Section 19 Minibus (Designated Bodies) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Minibus (Designated Bodies) Order 1987 and contains some provisions made in consequence of the abolition of the Inner London Education Authority (“ILEA”).

items.title: The Local Government Superannuation (Amendment)Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make a number of amendments to the Local Government Superannuation Regulations 1986 (S.I. 1986/24) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Local Government Act 1985 (Thames Water Authority Regional Land Drainage Committee) (Revocation) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, made under the Local Government Act 1985, revokes the Local Government Act 1985 (Thames Water Authority Regional Land Drainage Committee) Order 1986, which is now superseded by the Thames Regional Flood Defence Committee Order 1990 (S.I. 1990/1712).

items.title: The Thames Regional Flood Defence Committee (Appointed Day) Order 1990
items.description: Section 137 of the Water Act 1989 contains provisions for the establishment, as from the 1st September 1989 (“the transfer date”), of regional defence flood committees in place of regional land drainage committees. Subsection (4) of that section, makes provision for the membership of a regional flood defence committee during a period beginning with the transfer date and ending with a date appointed by, in the case of a regional flood defence committee in England, the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food.

items.title: The Thames Regional Flood Defence Committee Order 1990
items.description: Section 138(2) of the Water Act 1989 (hereinafter called “the Act”) provides that the total number of members of a regional flood defence committee of the National Rivers Authority shall not be less than eleven nor more than seventeen unless a determination that the total number of such members should be increased is submitted by the National Rivers Authority to the appropriate Minister (in the case of the Thames Regional Flood Defence Committee, the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food) under section 138(3) of the Act, and is confirmed by the Minister. Where the total number of members is so increased, the Minister is required by section 138(5) of the Act by Order to specify the number of members to be appointed to the committee by each of the constituent councils for the committee. The total number to be so appointed is required by section 138(6) of the Act to exceed by one the number of the other members of the committee (appointed by the Minister and the National Rivers Authority).

items.title: The Local Authorities (Allowances) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations increase the maximum rate of attendance allowance payable to members of a local authority who are councillors, and the maximum rate of financial loss allowance payable for the performance of an approved duty to members of a local authority and of other bodies who are not entitled to attendance allowance (regulation 2). The Regulations also increase the maximum rates of special responsibility allowance which may be paid by a local authority and increase the total amount which a local authority may pay by way of such allowance in any financial year, depending upon the population of that local authority area (regulations 3 and 4).

items.title: The Personal Community Charge (Relief) (No. 2) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision for reducing the liability of certain persons in Scotland to pay the personal community charge in the financial year 1989-90.

items.title: The EEC Merger Control (Distinct Market Investigations)Regulations 1990 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations confer investigative powers (based on section 3(7) of the Competition Act 1980) upon the Director General of Fair Trading for the purpose of furnishing information to the European Commission under Council Regulation (EEC) No. 4064/89 (on the control of concentrations between undertakings) when a concentration with a Community dimension (as defined in that Regulation) may impede competition in a distinct market within the United Kingdom. The enforcement provisions of section 85(6) to (8) of the Fair Trading Act 1973 apply. Sections 93B and 133 of that Act also apply.

items.title: The Set-Aside (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Set-Aside Regulations 1988 consistently with the provisions in article 1a of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 797/85, (OJ No. L93, 30.3.85, p. 1) as amended by Council Regulation (EEC) No. 1760/87 (OJ No. L167, 26.6.87, p. 1) and Council Regulation (EEC) No. 1094/88 (OJ L106, 27.4.88, p. 28) and the detailed rules in Commission Regulation (EEC) No. 1272/88 OJ No. L121, 11.5.88, p. 36. They enable farmers who had livestock in the reference period (1st July 1987–30th June 1988) to graze on their holdings no more than the equivalent of that number of livestock units, and farmers who did not have livestock in the reference period to graze deer or goats up to one livestock unit per forage hectare (regulations 5 and 9 and Schedule 3). Farmers who make use of this optional use of fallow land are required to comply with certain conditions, including conditions as to the application of fertilisers (other than manure produced by the livestock), pesticides and herbicides, and as to maintenance of environmental features. Grass must be sown and may not be cut more than once in every year (regulation 8 and Schedule 2).

items.title: The Local Government (Allowances) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make further amendments to the Local Government (Allowances) Regulations 1986. They increase the amounts of attendance allowance, financial loss allowance, and special responsibility allowance payable under the Local Government Act 1972 to members of local authorities.

items.title: The Prescription Pricing Authority Constitution Order 1990
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the Prescription Pricing Authority Constitution Order 1978. The Prescription Pricing Authority, a special health authority established under the National Health Service Act 1977 to exercise functions in relation to the examining, checking and pricing of prescriptions for the supply of pharmaceutical services under that Act, is continued in being, but differently constituted.

items.title: The Prescription Pricing Authority Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Prescription Pricing Authority Regulations 1978 to provide for the appointment and tenure of office of the members of the Prescription Pricing Authority (“the Pricing Authority”) which is a special health authority established under the National Health Service Act 1977 to exercise functions in relation to the examining, checking and pricing of prescriptions for the supply of pharmaceutical service under the Act. They are made as a consequence of the changed constitution of the Pricing Authority and as a consequence of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990.

items.title: The Land Registration Fee Order 1990
items.description: The Order, which, from the 2nd April 1990, supersedes the Land Registration Fee Order 1988, effects the following changes with respect to fees payable in connection with applications and services under the Land Registration Acts 1925 to 1986:1abatement 2 (Scale A fee abated to one fifth with a £25 minimum) is applied to the fees payable for registration of a surrender which is consideration or part consideration for the grant of a new lease to the registered proprietor and registration of a surrender otherwise than for monetary consideration (article 2(3) and (5));2provision is made for reduction or waiver by the Registrar of the fee for rectification of the register where he considers it to be unreasonable or excessive (article 2(5), proviso);3for the purpose of valuation, land subject to an application to register a surrender is to be valued at a date immediately prior to the surrender (article 4(1));4new provision is made for the fees payable for the registration of a charge which secures an obligation or liability contingent upon the happening of a future event and for the registration of an instrument containing two or more charges (article 5(4) and (5));5the additional fees payable in respect of exceptional work in connection with the voluntary first registration of land or the conversion of title are abolished and the basis upon which an additional fee may become payable for an application for a search of the index map is amended (articles 7 and 9);6provision is made with respect to the deduction of fees from the credit account of a credit account holder where an application for which credit facilities are available is made and no request for such deduction has been made nor any fee paid (article 14(3));7the maximum fee for any one application for the registration or modification of a caution, restriction, notice or inhibition is raised from £250 to £500 (article 7(1) and proviso (b) to paragraph (1) of Schedule 3, Part I);8fees are re-introduced in relation to office copies, searches of the index map, inspection of documents, telephone searches, telex searches and searches without priority (article 7(1) and Schedule 3, Part II).

items.title: The Petty Sessional Divisions (Lincolnshire) Order 1990
items.description: This Order gives effect, with modifications, to a draft order submitted to the magistrates' courts committee for the county of Lincolnshire and provides for the reorganisation of fourteen of the petty sessional divisions within the county into ten new divisions. Only the existing division of Caistor remains unaffected by this reorganisation.

items.title: The M5 Birmingham-Exeter Motorway (Strensham Junction 8) Connecting Roads Scheme 1990
items.description: This version of the The M5 Birmingham-Exeter Motorway (Strensham Junction 8) Connecting Roads Scheme 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The M5 Birmingham-Exeter Motorway (Warndon Junction 6) Connecting Roads Scheme 1990
items.description: This version of the The M5 Birmingham-Exeter Motorway (Warndon Junction 6) Connecting Roads Scheme 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The M5 Birmingham-Exeter Motorway (Warndon Junction 6) Line Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The M5 Birmingham-Exeter Motorway (Warndon Junction 6) Line Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The M5 Birmingham–Exeter Motorway (Whittington Junction 7) Connecting Roads Scheme 1990
items.description: This version of the The M5 Birmingham–Exeter Motorway (Whittington Junction 7) Connecting Roads Scheme 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A65 Trunk Road (Heber to Holme House)(De-Trunking) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A65 Trunk Road (Heber to Holme House)(De-Trunking) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A65 Trunk Road (Gargrave Bypass) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A65 Trunk Road (Gargrave Bypass) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Tryptophan in Food Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations prohibit the addition of tryptophan (an amino acid) to food intended for human consumption, and its consequent sale or supply (regulation 2(1) and (2)), subject to an exception for those who require the food on medical grounds (regulation 2(4)). These Regulations also prohibit the exposure for sale of food intended for human consumption containing added tryptophan (regulation 2(3)). Contravention of or failure to comply with these prohibitions is made an offence (regulation 2(5) and (6)).

items.title: The Housing (Revocation and Modification of Clearance Orders) Order 1990
items.description: This Order revokes the saving made in the Housing Act 1974 (Commencement No. 6) Order 1979 in relation to the repeal by that Order of section 24 of, and Schedule 3 to, the Housing Act 1961. The saving enabled an order to be made under section 24 revoking or modifying a clearance order which had become operative under section 44 of the Housing Act 1957 (c. 56) before 9th October 1979.

items.title: The Drainage Rates (Forms) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Drainage Rates (Forms) Regulations 1977 and re-enact their provisions with the modifications which are necessary to take account of the changes in the system of financing internal drainage boards introduced by the Internal Drainage Board (Finance) Regulations 1990 whereby with effect from the financial year commencing 1st April 1990 drainage rates may be assessed only on agricultural land and agricultural buildings. The Regulations prescribe the particulars to be contained in a drainage rate and the form of demand for drainage rates from the agricultural sector in relation to rates made in respect of any period between 1st April 1990 and 31st March 1993.

items.title: The Housing (Prescribed Forms) (No. 2) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations prescribe forms for use by local housing authorities for the purposes of Parts VI (repair notices), IX (slum clearance), X (overcrowding) and XI (houses in multiple occupation) of the Housing Act 1985.

items.title: The Child Resistant Packaging (Safety) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Child Resistant Packaging (Safety) Regulations 1986. The standard with which the packaging of regulated products must conform is now British Standard Specification for packagings resistant to opening by children BS 6652: 1989 (subject to any further amendments which may be made to that standard and approved by the Secretary of State) instead of British Standard Requirements and Test Procedures for packagings resistant to opening by children BS 6652: 1985. It will be sufficient for packaging to conform to the 1985 Standard if a certificate of conformity with that Standard has been issued by the British Standards Institution before these Regulations come into force and the certificate remains in force.

items.title: The Plant Health (Great Britain) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Plant Health (Great Britain) (Amendment) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The National Health Service (Service Committees and Tribunal) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (Service Committees and Tribunal) Regulations 1974 (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for the investigation and determination of questions whether doctors, dentists, chemists and opticians providing services under the National Health Service have failed to comply with their terms of service. The amendments are made principally in consequence of the reconstitution of Family Practitioner Committees as Family Health Services Authorities under the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990.

items.title: The National Health Service (Fund-holding Practices) (Applications and Recognition) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations contain provisions about applications by medical practitioners providing general medical services under the health service for recognition as fund-holding practices and the grant of recognition by Regional Health Authorities or, on appeal, by the Secretary of State. The Regulations include provision about—1the application of the Regulations in Wales (regulation 2),2applications for recognition as a fund-holding practice (regulation 3),3the timing of the application (regulation 4),4the grant of recognition as a fund-holding practice (regulation 5),5the conditions for obtaining recognition (regulation 6),6determination of the application (regulation 7),7appeals to the Secretary of State (regulation 8).

items.title: The National Health Service (Fund-Holding Practices) (Applications and Recognition) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 17th September 1990, make provision in relation to Scotland for general medical practitioners to apply for recognition as fund-holding practices and the grant of recognition by Health Boards or, on appeal, by the Secretary of State.

items.title: The National Health Service (Determination of Regions and Districts) Amendment Order 1990
items.description: This Order abolishes the districts of Bloomsbury Health Authority and Islington Health Authority and merges the districts into a new district called Bloomsbury and Islington District, with the exception of a small portion of Westminster which is transferred to Parkside District (article 2).Article 3 makes a consequential alteration to the regions of North East Thames and North West Thames Regional Health Authorities.

items.title: The National Health Service (District Health Authorities) Order 1990
items.description: This Order continues (in articles 3 and 4) the constitution of the District Health Authorities originally constituted by the Orders revoked by article 7(1). To that extent, it is made as a consequence of the new provisions for membership of District Health Authorities under the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 (c. 19). The Order also abolishes the Bloomsbury Health Authority and the Islington Health Authority (article 5) and establishes a new District Health Authority to be known as the Bloomsbury and Islington Health Authority (article 6).

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) Regulations 1974 (“the principal Regulations”) which govern the arrangements to be made by Family Practitioner Committees for the provision in their locality of general medical services and pharmaceutical services under the National Health Service Act 1977. By virtue of amendments made to that Act by the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990, Family Practitioner Committees are, from 17th September 1990, reconstituted as Family Health Services Authorities.

items.title: The Regional and District Health Authorities (Membership and Procedure) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend Part I of Schedule 1 to the Regional and District Health Authorities (Membership and Procedure) Regulations 1990 which lists District Health Authorities in England which are to have at least one non-officer member who holds a post in a university with a medical or dental school, by substituting for “Bloomsbury Health Authority”, “Bloomsbury and Islington Health Authority.” This is in consequence of the merger of Bloomsbury Health Authority with Islington Health Authority which took place under the National Health Service (District Health Authorities) Order 1990 and the National Health Service (Determination of Regions and Districts) Amendment Order 1990.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Cambridge Cable Limited) (Cambridge) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Cambridge Cable Limited on 13th July 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the whole of the Cambridge district, together with parts of the districts of South Cambridgeshire, East Cambridgeshire, and Huntingdonshire; part of the district of Forest Heath in the County of Suffolk; part of the district of Uttlesford in the County of Essex; and part of the district of North Hertfordshire. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 29th August 1990. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Cambridge Cable Limited will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its Cambridge public telecommunication systems when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Prison (Amendment) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules amend the Prison Rules 1964, as amended, in two respects.

items.title: The Young Offender Institution (Amendment) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules amend the Young Offender Institution Rules 1988, as amended, in two respects.

items.title: The County Court (Amendment No. 3) Rules 1990
items.description: This version of the The County Court (Amendment No. 3) Rules 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Local Government Reorganisation (Miscellaneous Provisions) Order 1990
items.description: This Order makes provision consequential on the Local Government Act 1985 (“the 1985 Act”), which abolished the Greater London Council and the metropolitan county councils on 1st April 1986.

items.title: The Companies (Forms Amendment No. 2 and Company’s Type and Principal Business Activities) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations prescribe new forms 10, 288 and 691 as from 1st October 1990, and prescribe new particulars for the purposes of section 692(1)(b) of the Companies Act 1985 as from the same date. This is done to give effect to the requirements to give directors' dates of birth for certain purposes introduced by the Companies Act 1989. The old forms 10, 288 and 691 are revoked as from 1st January 1991 and the old particulars as from 1st October 1990.

items.title: The Export of Goods (Control) (Amendment No. 5) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Export of Goods (Control) (Amendment No. 5) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Iraq and Kuwait (United Nations Sanctions) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order is made under the United Nations Act 1946 and amends the Iraq and Kuwait (United Nations Sanctions) Order 1990 (“the principal Order”). It inserts a new Article 4A which prohibits payments under any bond in respect of a contract the performance of which is unlawful by virtue of the principal Order or the Export of Goods (Control) (Iraq and Kuwait Sanctions) Order 1990. It also prohibits the making of or obtaining payments under certain indemnities arising under such bonds.

items.title: The Iraq and Kuwait (United Nations Sanctions)(Bermuda) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, prohibits persons in Bermuda from giving effect to any directions to make payments or part with any gold or securities or to make any change in the persons to whose credit any sum is to stand or to whose order any gold or security is to be held if the directions are given by or on behalf of the Governments of Kuwait or the Republic of Iraq or by or on behalf of any persons resident in those countries unless the transaction is permitted by the Governor.

items.title: The National Health Service (Recognition of health service bodies) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st March 1990, specifies as bodies recognised by the Secretary of State as having been formed for the purpose of performing a function connected with the provision of services under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978, Area Audit Committees established by the Health Boards in Scotland.

items.title: The Iraq and Kuwait (United Nations Sanctions) (Dependent Territories) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order is made under the United Nations Act 1946 and amends the Iraq and Kuwait (United Nations Sanctions) (Dependent Territories) Order 1990 (“the principal Order”).

items.title: The Iraq and Kuwait (United Nations Sanctions) (Channel Islands) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, applies to the Channel Islands. It prohibits the importation into the Channel Islands of certain goods exported from Iraq or Kuwait and the exportation from the Channel Islands of certain goods intended for Iraq or Kuwait. It also prohibits certain activities and dealings relating to the exportation from Iraq or Kuwait of the former goods and the supply to Iraq or Kuwait of the latter goods, including carriage of these goods in British ships, aircraft and land transport vehicles and makes provision for the investigation of any such ships, aircraft and vehicles that are suspected of contravening this Order. The Order prohibits payments under any bond in respect of a contract the performance of which is unlawful by virtue of this Order or the Export of Goods (Control) (Iraq and Kuwait Sanctions) Order 1990. It confers certain powers relating to the obtaining of evidence and information for the purposes of the Order.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Dental Services) (Miscellaneous Amendments) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Dental Services) (Scot land) Regulations 1974 (“the principal Regulations”) which regulate the terms on which general dental services are provided under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978 and make consequential and associated amendments to the Regulations concerning charges for dental treatment and appliances and service committee and other disciplinary procedures. The principal changes made by these Regulations are as follows.

items.title: The Community Charge Benefits (General) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Community Charge Benefits (General) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Family Credit (General) Amendment No. 2 Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Family Credit (General) Amendment No. 2 Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (General) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987 (the principal Regulations) in the following respects—

items.title: The Income Support (General) Amendment No. 3 Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Income Support (General) Amendment No. 3 Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Income Support (Liable Relatives) Regulations 1990
items.description: When income support is claimed by or on behalf of a person who another person is liable to maintain, section 24 of the Social Security Act 1986 enables a magistrates' court, on a complaint by the Secretary of State, to order the liable person to pay such sum as it considers appropriate. The Social Security Act 1990 inserted a new section 24A into the 1986 Act which provides that the sum which may be so ordered to be paid may include an amount, determined in accordance with regulations, in respect of income support paid to or for the claimant under such provisions as may be prescribed. Regulation 2 of these Regulations prescribes the amount and the provisions which are to be taken into account.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 1979 (“the principal Regulations”). Regulation 2 inserts a new regulation 26A into the principal Regulations, which provides for the return of Class 2 contributions paid by self-employed earners whose earnings are less than the small earnings exception amount specified in section 7(5) of the Social Security Act 1975 (c. 14)

items.title: The Housing (Preservation of Right to Buy) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: Section 164 of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (c. 42) provides for a new paragraph to be substituted for paragraph 11 of Schedule 5 to the Housing Act 1985. The new paragraph provides an exception to the right to buy in respect of dwellings for persons of pensionable age. The exception only applies where the Secretary of State has determined that the right to buy is not to be capable of being exercised.

items.title: The Petty Sessional Divisions (Essex) Order 1990
The Petty Sessional Divisions (Wigan) Order 1990
items.description: This Order gives effect to a draft Order submitted by the magistrates' courts committee for the metropolitan district of Wigan and provides for the combination of the petty sessional divisions of Makerfield and Wigan to form one new division to be known as the Wigan petty sessional division.

items.title: The Local Government (Direct Labour Organisations) (Competition) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations consolidate with amendments the Local Government (Direct Labour Organisations) (Competition) (Scotland) Regulations 1984 (“the 1984 Regulations”) and the Local Government (Direct Labour Organisations) (Competition) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988 (“the 1988 Regulations”), which instruments are revoked by these Regula tions. These Regulations come into force on 1st October 1990 insofar as they apply to local authorities and on 1st April 1991 insofar as they apply to development bodies. The revocation of the 1984 Regulations and 1988 Regulations have effect on the same basis.

items.title: The Local Government (Direct Labour Organisations) (Specified Number of Employed Persons) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: Local authorities and development bodies which did not at any one time in the previous financial year employ more than 30 persons for the carrying out of construction or maintenance work are not subject to Part III of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980. Such local authorities and development bodies were also exempt from the requirement to keep separate accounts for each of the descriptions of construction or maintenance work detailed in section 10(2) of the said Act.

items.title: The Dolgellau—South of Birkenhead Trunk Road (A 494)(Mold By-Pass) Order 1990
items.description: This Order revokes an Order made under sections 7 and 44 of the Highways Act 1959 (c. 25),and section 27 of the Local Government Act 1966 (c. 42), and section 68 of the Highways Act 1971 (c. 41), which provided for a by-pass of the Dolgellau—South of Birkenhead Trunk Road at Mold.

items.title: The Social Fund (Miscellaneous Provisions) Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Social Fund (Miscellaneous Provisions) Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Copper or Other Metal Poisoning) (England) Order 1990
items.description: This Order prohibits the movement of certain sheep and anything derived from them from a designated area (articles 3 and 4 and the Schedule). They are likely to create a hazard to human health because they may be contaminated (article 2).

items.title: The Housing (Extension of Right to Buy) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Housing (Extension of Right to Buy) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A423 Trunk Road (Sandford on Thames) Detrunking Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A423 Trunk Road (Sandford on Thames) Detrunking Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Home Energy Efficiency Grants Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations provide for the making of grants for the improvement of energy efficiency in dwellings occupied by persons on low income and in the communal areas of buildings occupied by such persons; for the appointment of administering agencies to administer the making of grants; and for the registration of network installers capable of carrying out the work for which grants may be made.

items.title: The Tryptophan in Food (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Scotland only, prohibit the addition of Tryptophan (an amino acid) to food intended for human consumption, and its consequent sale or supply (regulation 3(1) and (2)), subject to an exception for those who require the food on medical grounds (regulation 3(4)). These Regulations also prohibit the exposure for sale of food intended for human consumption containing added tryptophan (regulation 3(3)). Contravention of or failure to comply with these prohibitions is made an offence and provision is made for district and islands councils to enforce the Regulations (regulation 5). The Regulations also make provision for the application of specified procedural provisions of the Food and Drugs (Scotland) Act 1956 to these Regulations (regulation 6).

items.title: The National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 (Commencement No.3 and Transitional Provisions) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on various dates provisions of Part II (the National Health Service: Scotland) and Part V (Miscellaneous and General) of, and of Schedules 5, 9 and 10 to, the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 relating to Scotland. The Order also contains a transitional provision relative to appeals from certain decisions of the Scottish Medical Practices Committee made before 1st January 1991.

items.title: The Public Telecommunications System Designation (Mid Downs Cable Limited) (Crawley, Horley and Gatwick Airport) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Mid Downs Cable Limited on 9th July 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the whole of the Borough of Crawley including Gatwick Airport, part of the Borough of Reigate and Banstead, part of the District of Tandridge, part of the District of Mid Sussex, part of the Borough of Mole Valley and part of the District of Horsham.A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 4th September 1990.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Herts Cable Limited) (West Hertfordshire) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Herts Cable Ltd. a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the West Hertfordshire area. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 5th September 1990. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Herts Cable Limited will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its West Hertfordshire public telecommunication systems when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Trade Marks and Service Marks(Amendment) (No. 2) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules amend rules 21, 59 and 60 of the Trade Marks and Service Marks Rules 1986 as a consequence of the change made at the same time in the Trade Marks and Service Marks (Fees) Rules 1990 (S.I. 1990/1800) for the payment of a composite fee for an application to register and, if accepted, for the registration of the mark in respect of an application filed on or after 1st October 1990. A separate registration fee will continue to be charged for an application to register filed before that date. Paragraph (2) of rule 60 (the requirement to file a further representation of the mark before its entry in the register) is revoked.

items.title: The Stock Transfer (Substitution of Forms) Order 1990
items.description: This Order substitutes a new form for the Talisman Sold Transfer form set out in Schedule I to the Stock Transfer Act 1963. The previous form made provision for the transfer of securities to a nominee of the Stock Exchange only by a member of the Stock Exchange. The form substituted by this Order provides for such a transfer by non-members (article 2).

items.title: The Trade Marks and Service Marks (Fees) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules revoke and replace the Trade Marks and Service Marks (Fees) Rules 1989 (the 1989 Rules) and prescribe, in respect of all applications to register a trade mark or service mark filed on or after the date of the coming into force of these Rules (1st October 1990), a composite fee of £160 for an application to register a mark and for the registration of the mark (whether or not accepted for registration). Under the 1989 Rules an application to register a mark was subject to an initial fee of £68, with a further fee of £95 on its acceptance for registration; the latter fee will continue to apply to applications to register made before 1st October 1990. All other fees remain unchanged.

items.title: The Trade Marks and Service Marks (Forms) (Amendment) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules prescribe a new Form TM3 (to replace Form TM2) for an application to register a trade mark or service mark filed on or after the date of the coming into force (1st October 1990) of the Trade Marks and Service Marks (Fees) Rules 1990 (S.I. 1990/ 1800) under which a composite fee for application to register and for the registration of the mark is prescribed. A new Form TM10A is also prescribed (with no further fee payable) for the application for registration of a mark for which application to register was made on or after 1st October 1990. Form TM10 (which will continue to be required in the case of an application to register filed before 1st October 1990) and Form TM11 (form for renewal of registration) are revised.

items.title: The North West Regional Flood Defence Committee (Appointed Day) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: Section 137 of the Water Act 1989 contains provisions for the establishment, as from the 1st September 1989 (“the transfer date”), of regional flood defence committees in place of regional land drainage committees. Subsection (4) of that section makes provision for the membership of a regional flood defence committee during a period beginning with the transfer date and ending with a date appointed by, in the case of a regional flood defence committee in England, the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food.

items.title: The Education (School and Placing Information) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (School and Placing Information) (Scotland) Regulations 1982 (“the principal Regulations”) to take account of the fact that, by virtue of the School Boards (Scotland) Act 1988, school boards are set up and school councils established under section 125 of the Local Government (Scotland) Act 1973 are abolished. The principal Regulations are amended so as to require an education authority, when informing a parent of the particular school in which the authority propose to place his child, to supply that parent with information with respect to: whether a school board is established for the school and, if not, or if one has been disestablished, why that is the case; the composition of the school board for the school, whether or not one is in fact established, which has been specified in regulations made under the School Boards (Scotland) Act 1988 for schools of the size in question; and, where a school board is established for the school, the name of each member of the board and whether each such member is a parent, staff or co-opted member, along with the address to which all communications to the board should be sent. The principal Regulations are also amended so as to remove any requirement on an education authority to make available any information relating to school councils, given their abolition.

items.title: The Cosmetic Products (Safety) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make a number of amendments to the Cosmetic Products (Safety) Regulations 1989 (“the principal regulations”). In particular, they re-impose the marking requirements contained in regulation 8 of the Cosmetic Products (Safety) Regulations 1984 (S.I. 1984/1260) until regulation 6 of the principal regulations comes into force; they also provide that the marking requirements of regulation 6 can be met, if the cosmetic product is sold loose, by providing the information on the packaging in which the product is displayed, the container in which it is exposed for sale or a notice in immediate proximity to that container. Regulation 6 of the principal regulations will come into force for some purposes (specified in regulation 1(5)(a) of those regulations) on 1st January 1992 and for all other purposes on 1st January 1994.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Publicity Account) (Exemption) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Local Authorities (Publicity Account) (Exemption) (Scotland) Order 1988 (“the principal Order”) which specifies certain exemptions from the application of section 5(1) of the Local Government Act 1986, in terms of which a local authority is required to keep a separate account of its expenditure on publicity. Paragraph 11(c) of the Schedule to the principal Order made an exemption for publicity in the form of communications issued, in the course of the normal management and running of an educational establishment, by the education authority or by a member of a school council or college council to former, current or prospective pupils, or to their parents or guardians.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Alteration of Lists and Appeals) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Non-Domestic Rating (Alteration of Lists and Appeals) Regulations 1990.

items.title: The Assured and Protected Tenancies (Lettings to Students) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Assured and Protected Tenancies (Lettings to Students) Regulations 1988 (“the 1988 Regulations”). Regulation 4A, as substituted, specifies all housing associations registered by the Housing Corporation or Housing for Wales as bodies for the purposes of paragraph 8 of Schedule 1 to the Housing Act 1988. A tenancy granted by such a body to a student or prospective student at an educational institution specified in the 1988 Regulations is not an assured tenancy for the purposes of that Act. Schedule 2 to the 1988 Regulations, as substituted, (which lists particular bodies specified for the purposes of the said paragraph 8) accordingly omits references to those bodies previously listed which are registered housing associations. Two bodies, the London School of Economics Housing Association and the London Mission (West London) Circuit Meeting of the Methodist Church, are also added to Schedule 2.l

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Heavy Metal Poisoning) (England) (Revocation) Order 1990
items.description: This Order revokes the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Heavy Metal Poisoning) (England) Order 1990 which contained emergency prohibitions in order to prevent human consumption of food which may have been rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of animals eating zinc chromate or other poisonous heavy metal.

items.title: The Zebra Pedestrian Crossings (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the “Zebra” Pedestrian Crossings Regulations 1971. They make provision to enable “Belisha beacons” to be fitted with backing boards designed to improve the conspicuity of the beacon and shields designed to prevent or reduce light shining from the globe into adjacent properties. Such boards or shields must be firmly secured, must not prevent drivers from seeing the beacon and must not constitute a danger to road users.

items.title: The College Councils (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations relate to the new college councils which, in accordance with Part II of the Self-Governing Schools etc. (Scotland) Act 1989, require to be established to exercise certain functions in relation to colleges of further education under the management of education authorities in Scotland.

items.title: The Swansea—Manchester Trunk Road (A483) and the Shrewsbury—Dolgellau Trunk Road (A458) (Welshpool North—South Relief Road) (Variation) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Swansea—Manchester Trunk Road (A483) and the Shrewsbury—Dolgellau Trunk Road (A458) (Welshpool North—South Relief Road) (Variation) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Designation of Approval Marks)(Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Designation of Approval Marks) Regulations 1979 by prescribing a new item 70 in Schedule 2, being a marking relating to rear marking plates under ECE Regulation 70.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Type Approval) (Great Britain) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Type Approval) (Great Britain) Regulations 1984.

items.title: The Electricity Act 1989 (Fees) Order 1990
items.description: This Order specifies functions and matters which are to be taken into account in the determination of fees to be fixed in regulations made under the Electricity Act 1989 by the Secretary of State or the Director General of Electricity Supply in respect of certain matters concerning the generation, transmission and supply of electricity. The rate of return (see paragraph 3 of Schedule 2 to the Order) will be a rate agreed with the Treasury.

items.title: The Enterprise and New Towns (Scotland) Act 1990 Commencement Order 1990
items.description: This Order appoints the days on which the provisions of the Enterprise and New Towns (Scotland) Act 1990 (“the Act”), other than those which came into force on Royal Assent, are to come into force.

items.title: The Firemen’s Pension Scheme (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order further varies the Firemen’s Pension Scheme brought into operation by the Firemen’s Pension Scheme Order 1973 (“the 1973 Scheme”).

items.title: The National Health Service (Audit of Accounts) (Transitional Provisions) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations provide for the continuation of the duty of auditors appointed by the Secretary of State to audit the accounts of certain health service bodies for the period from 1st April 1989 to 31st March 1990 despite the transfer from 1st October 1990 to the Audit Commission for Local Authorities in England and Wales (after 1st October 1990 to be called the Audit Commission for Local Authorities and the National Health Service in England and Wales) of responsibility for auditing the accounts of health service bodies.

items.title: The Isles of Scilly (Variation) Order 1990
items.description: The annual limit on the amount of expenditure which a local authority may incur for purposes not otherwise authorised is determined under section 137(4) of the Local Government Act 1972 by reference to the sum appropriate to that authority by virtue of section 137(4AA) of that Act.

items.title: The Goods Vehicles (Operators' Licences, Qualifications and Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Goods Vehicles (Operators' Licences, Qualifications and Fees) Regulations 1984. They implement Council Directive (EEC) No. 438/89 of 21st June 1989 (OJ No. L212, 22.7.89, p. 101) amending Council Directive (EEC) No. 561/74 of 12th November 1974 on admission to the occupation of road haulage operator in national and international transport operations (OJ No. L308,19.11.74, p.18).

items.title: The Buckinghamshire (District Boundaries) Order 1990
items.description: This Order transfers areas in the county of Buckinghamshire between the district of Chiltern and the district of South Bucks. Consequential changes to parishes and local government electoral areas are made.

items.title: The Operation of Public Service Vehicles (Partnership) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Operation of Public Service Vehicles (Partnership) Regulations 1986 to take account of the amendments to Schedule 3 to the Public Passenger Vehicles Act 1981 made by the Public Service Vehicle Operators (Qualifications) Regulations 1990 (S.I. 1990/1851) to implement Council Directive (EEC) No. 438/89 of 21st June 1989 (OJ No. L212, 22.7.89, p. 101) amending Council Directive (EEC) No. 562/74 of 12th November 1974 on admission to the occupation of road passenger transport operator in national and international transport operations (OJ No. L308, 19.11.74, p.23).

items.title: The Public Service Vehicle Operators (Qualifications) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 3 to the Public Passenger Vehicles Act 1981 to implement Council Directive (EEC) No. 438/89 of 21st June 1989 (OJ No. L212, 22.7.89, p. 101) amending Council Directive (EEC) No. 562/74 of 12th November 1974 on admission to the occupation of road passenger transport operator in national and international transport operations (OJ No. L308, 19.11.74, p. 23).

items.title: The Public Service Vehicles (Operators' Licences) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Schedule to the Public Service Vehicles (Operators' Licences) Regulations 1986 so as to include within the definition of relevant convictions for the purposes of the Public Passenger Vehicles Act 1981, to which the Traffic Commissioners are to have regard in relation to public service vehicle operator licensing, convictions of the serious offences and the road transport offences specified in paragraph 1 of Schedule 3 to that Act (as amended by the Public Service Vehicle Operators (Qualifications) Regulations 1990).

items.title: The Annual Close Time (Rivers Irvine and Garnock Salmon Fishery District) Order 1990
items.description: In relation to the annual close time of the Rivers Irvine and Garnock salmon fishery district (10th September to 24th February) this Order prescribes the period from 10th September to 15th November as the period during which fishing for salmon by rod and line is permitted in a specified part of the district. This part comprises the catchment area and estuary of the River Irvine and the adjacent area of sea between Irvine Bar in the north and the southern boundary of the salmon fishery district, in the south.

items.title: Authorities for London Post-Graduate Teaching Hospitals (Constitution) Amendment Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Authorities for London Post-Graduate Teaching Hospitals (Constitution) Order 1990 by changing the name of the former Board of Governors of the National Heart and Chest Hospital to the Board of Governors of the Royal Brompton National Heart and Lung Hospitals.

items.title: The Education (Training Grants) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations consolidate, with amendments, the Education (Training Grants) Regulations 1987, as amended. In addition to minor and drafting amendments, the following changes of substance are made.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Devon Cablevision Limited) (South Devon) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Devon Cablevision Limited on 17th July 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the whole of the Cities of Exeter and Plymouth, the southern part of the District of Teignbridge, part of the District of East Devon centring on Exmouth, all of Torbay, a small part of mid South Hams district and the eastern tip of the district of Caradon in the County of Cornwall.

items.title: The Electricity (Non-Fossil Fuel Sources) (England and Wales) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order imposes on public electricity suppliers in England and Wales an obligation to make arrangements to secure the availability during specified periods of specified aggregate amounts of generating capacity from non-fossil fuel generating stations.The arrangements must be made, and evidence of their making must be produced to the Director General of Electricity Supply, before 30th September 1990.

items.title: The A516 Trunk Road (Etwall Bypass) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A516 Trunk Road (Etwall Bypass) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A516 Trunk Road (Etwall Bypass) (Detrunking) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A516 Trunk Road (Etwall Bypass) (Detrunking) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Bovine Animals (Identification, Marking and Breeding Records) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Bovine Animals (Identification, Marking and Breeding Records) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Movement of Animals (Records) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order further amends the Movement of Animals (Records) Order 1960. It provides that movement records relating to bovine animals shall be kept for ten years instead of three years which was previously the case (article 2).

items.title: The Tuberculosis (England and Wales) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Tuberculosis (England and Wales) Order 1984 by omitting the provisions relating to the identification of bovine animals and the restriction on the sale of unidentified bovine animals in markets (article 2). Those provisions now form part of the Bovine Animals (Identification, Marking and Breeding Records) Order 1990 (S.I. 1990/1867).

items.title: The Time Off for Public Duties Order 1990
items.description: Section 29 of the Employment Protection (Consolidation) Act 1978 requires an employer to allow time off during working hours, subject to the provisions of the section, to an employee of his who is a justice of the peace, member of a local authority or member of certain other bodies specified in subsection of the section.

items.title: The Social Security (Attendance Allowance and Claims and Payments) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Attendance Allowance) (No. 2) Regulations 1975 (“the Attendance Allowance Regulations”)and the Social Security (Claims and Payments) Regulations 1987.

items.title: The Cheshire County Council (Shropshire Union Canal Tunnel, Chester) Scheme 1990 Confirmation Instrument 1990
items.description: This version of the The Cheshire County Council (Shropshire Union Canal Tunnel, Chester) Scheme 1990 Confirmation Instrument 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Industrial Training (Construction Board) Order 1964(Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order redefines the activities in relation to which the Construction Industry Training Board (the Board) exercises its functions. The Board was established on 21st July 1964 by the Industrial Training (Construction Board) Order 1964 (the 1964 Order). Its activities were redefined by the Industrial Training (Construction Board) Order 1980 (the 1980 Order), the Industrial Training (Construction Board) Order 1964 (Amendment) Order 1982 (the 1982 Order) and the Electricity Act 1989 (Consequential Modifications of Subordinate Legislation) Order 1990.

items.title: The A12 Trunk Road (Eastern Avenue, Havering)(Prescribed Routes) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A12 Trunk Road (Eastern Avenue, Havering)(Prescribed Routes) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Opencast Coal (Rate of Interest on Compensation) Order 1990
items.description: Section 35 of the Opencast Coal Act 1958 provides that interest shall be payable in addition to compensation in certain circumstances. This Order increases the rate of interest from 11X per cent. to 155/16 per cent. per annum and revokes the Opencast Coal (Rate of Interest on Compensation) (No. 2) Order 1988.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (City Centre Communications Ltd) (North West London) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to City Centre Communications Ltd on 31st August 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the whole of the London Boroughs of Barnet, Brent, and Hammersmith and Fulham. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 24th September 1990. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, City Centre Communications Ltd will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its North West London public telecommunications systems when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Leicester Communications Limited) (Leicester and Loughborough) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Leicester Communications Limited on 3rd September 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the whole of the City of Leicester and adjacent parts of the Districts of North West Leicestershire, Blaby and Harborough and the Boroughs of Charnwood (including all of the town of Loughborough) and Oadby & Wigston. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 24th September 1990. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Leicester Communications Limited will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its Leicester and Loughborough public telecommunication systems when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Employment Act 1989 (Commencement and Transitional Provisions) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 26th February 1990 all the provisions of the Employment Act 1989 (“the 1989 Act”) which were not brought into force earlier by virtue of section 30(2) and (3) of that Act, except for the provisions mentioned in article 2(2) (which relate to duty of factory occupier to give notice of employment of a young person to the local careers office).

items.title: The Social Security (Categorisation of Earners) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Categorisation of Earners) Regulations 1978 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Railway Employment Exemption Regulations 1962 (Revocation) Order 1990
items.description: This Order revokes on 15th March 1990 the Railway Employment Exemption Regulations 1962. Those Regulations exempt employment by the British Railways Board on railways as engine cleaners, firemen or signal box lads of male persons who have attained the age of sixteen from section 1(3) of the Emploment of Women, Young Persons, and Children Act 1920 (c. 65) (prohibition on employment of young persons at night in industrial undertakings). Section 1(3) of the 1920 Act is repealed by section 10(1) of, and Part I of Schedule 3 to, the Employment Act 1989.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Payment of Interest) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations provide for the payment of interest in certain cases in which, in consequence of an alteration to a valuation list compiled under Part III of the Local Government Finance Act 1988 (non-domestic rating), an amount falls to be repaid or credited by a charging authority or the Secretary of State.

items.title: The Special Hospitals Service Authority (Establishment and Constitution) Amendment Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Special Hospitals Service Authority (Establishment and Constitution) Order 1989 by specifying the Authority for the purposes of paragraph 9(7)(b) of Schedule 5 to the National Health Service Act 1977. The amendment enables the Secretary of State to pay remuneration to members of the Authority.

items.title: The Health and Safety (Fees) Regulations 1990
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Regulations)

items.title: The Tuberculosis (Scotland) Amendment Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Tuberculosis (Scotland) Order 1984 by removing the provisions relating to the identification and marking of bovine animals and the restriction on the sale of unidentified bovine animals in markets (article 3). Those provisions now form part of the Bovine Animals (Identification, Marking and Breeding Records) Order 1990 (S.I. 1990/1867).

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Social Security Act 1986) (Transfer of Orders to maintain and Enforcement of Maintenance Orders) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules are made in consequence of the passage of the Social Security Act 1990 which amends the Social Security Act 1986 in particular so as to allow for the transfer from the Secretary of State to the dependent parent of the right to receive payments under an order made under section 24 of the 1986 Act and for that right to be transferred back to the Secretary of State.

items.title: The Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (Commencement No. 4) Order 1990
items.description: Article 3 of the Order brings into force on 26th February 1990 paragraph 4 of Schedule 11 to the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 which inserts a new paragraph into Part II of Schedule 1 to the Education Act 1944 (c. 31). The new paragraph empowers the Secretary of State to give directions to local education authorities requiring specified education committees to include persons appointed in accordance with the directions.

items.title: The Nuclear Installations Act 1965 (Repeal and Modifications) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations repeal part of section 1(1) of the Nuclear Installations Act 1965 (“the 1965 Act”), removing the exemption of the United Kingdom Atomic Energy Authority (“the Authority”) from licensing under that Act.

items.title: The Combined Probation Areas (Lincolnshire)Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Combined Probation Areas Order 1986 to take account of the reorganisation of petty sessional divisions in Lincolnshire effected by the Petty Sessional Divisions (Lincolnshire) Order 1990 (S.I. 1990/1720) which comes into force on 15th October 1990.

items.title: The Electricity (Applications for Licences and Extensions of Licences) Regulations 1990
