items.description: Anyone who generates, transmits or supplies electricity requires, unless exempted by an order made under section 5 of the Electricity Act 1989, a licence to do so under section 6 of the 1989 Act and applications for licences or where appropriate for an extension thereto shall be made in the manner prescribed in regulations made by the Secretary of State.

items.title: The Oxfordshire County Council (Wallingford Bypass River Thames Bridge) Scheme 1989 Confirmation Instrument 1990
The Gipsy Encampments (Borough of Hertsmere) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates the Borough of Hertsmere as an area to which section 10 of the Caravan Sites Act 1968 applies.

items.title: The Gipsy Encampments (District of East Cambridgeshire) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates the District of East Cambridgeshire as an area to which section 10 of the Caravan Sites Act 1968 applies.

items.title: The Gipsy Encampments (London Borough of Harrow) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates the London Borough of Harrow as an area to which section 10 of the Caravan Sites Act 1968 applies.

items.title: The Electricity (Class Exemptions from the Requirement for a Licence) Order 1990
items.description: This Order grants exemption from the requirements of section 4(1)(a) of the Electricity Act 1989 (which prohibits the generation of electricity without a licence) and section 4(1)(c) of that Act (which prohibits the supply of electricity to premises without a licence) to persons of various classes. The classes of persons who are exempt from the requirement to hold a licence to generate electricity are set out in Schedule 2 and the classes of persons who are exempt from the requirement to hold a licence to supply electricity to premises are set out in Schedule 3.

items.title: The Bovine Spongiform Encephalopathy (No. 2) Amendment Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Bovine Spongiform Encephalopathy (No. 2) Order 1988 so as to prohibit the sale, supply and use of specified bovine offal and feedingstuffs containing specified bovine offal (which comprises the brain, spinal cord, spleen, thymus, tonsils and intestines of a bovine animal) or animal protein derived from it for feeding to any kind of mammal, except man, and any kind of four-footed beast which is not a mammal and any bird.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) Amendment (No.4) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend Schedule 1 to the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 1979 (“the principal Regulations”) which applies certain income tax provisions to earnings-related contributions under the Social Security Act 1975 (“the 1975 Act”) and includes provisions for returns relating to earnings-related contributions to be made in a similar manner to tax returns.

items.title: The Export of Sheep (Prohibition) (No. 2) Amendment No. 3 Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Export of Sheep (Prohibition) (No. 2) Amendment No. 3 Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (England) Amendment No. 4 Order 1990
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (England) Order 1987 contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent the human consumption of food which has been or which may have been rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the USSR.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Dental Services) (Miscellaneous Amendments) (No. 2) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (General Dental Services) Regulations 1973, which regulate the terms on which general dental services are provided under the National Health Service Act 1977, so as to correct the omission, from regulation 16 and paragraphs 3, 13 and 16 of the dentists' terms of service, of provision for the treatment under general dental services of a patient on referral by another dentist who has been providing occasional treatment or who has been treating the patient otherwise than under general dental services.

items.title: The Electricity (Register) Order 1990
items.description: This Order prescribes the hours during which the register maintained by the Director General of Electricity Supply under section 49 of the Electricity Act 1989 is to be avail(able for public inspection and the fees to be paid for the supply of a copy of, or extract from, the register.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Dental Services) (Miscellaneous Amendments) (Scotland) (No.2) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (General Dental Services) (Scotland) Regulations 1974, which regulate the terms on which general dental services are provided under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978, so as to correct the omission, from regulation 19 and paragraphs 3, 13 and 16 of the Terms of Service for Dentists, of provision for the treatment under general dental services of a patient on referral by another dentist who has been providing occasional treatment or who has been treating the patient otherwise than under general dental services.

items.title: The Borough of Burnley (Electoral Arrangements)Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Borough of Burnley (Electoral Arrangements)Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Social Security Act 1990 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Social Security Act 1990 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Cash Accounting) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations which come into force on 1st October 1990 amend the Value Added Tax (Cash Accounting) Regulations 1987 S.I. No. 1987/1427. Eligibility for membership of the cash accounting scheme is determined by the value of taxable supplies (excluding VAT) made in a year. The Regulations increase the limit from £250,000 to £300,000.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions)(Wales) (No. 5) Amendment No. 4 Order 1990
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Wales) (No. 5) Order 1987, as amended by S.I. 1988/9, 951, 1329, 1680, 1989/2, 660, 1279, 1770, 1990/34, 1161 and 1523 contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent the human consumption of food which has been or which may have been rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the USSR.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) Amendment (No. 4) Order 1990
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) Order 1989 contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape in April 1986 of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the Ukraine, USSR.

items.title: The Housing Support Grant (Scotland) Variation Order 1990
items.description: This Order increases for the year 1989-90 the aggregate amount of housing support grants and its general and hostel portions payable to certain local authorities in Scotland.

items.title: The Local Government Reorganisation (Property)(West Midlands) Order 1990
items.description: This Order transfers property, rights and liabilities formerly vested in the West Midlands County Council to councils of districts in that county, to certain parish councils and to the council of the county of Staffordshire. The transfers made are in addition to those effected by S.I. 1987/1077, 1988/783 and 1989/1399.

items.title: The Veterinary Surgeons and Veterinary Practitioners (Disciplinary Committee) (Procedure and Evidence) (Amendment) Rules Order of Council 1990
items.description: The Rules approved by this Order amend the Veterinary Surgeons and Veterinary Practitioners Disciplinary Committee (Procedure and Evidence) Rules 1967 empowering the Chairman of the Disciplinary Committee to determine the procedure of any aspect of the conduct of the proceedings not already covered by specific provision in the Rules.

items.title: The Housing Support Grant (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order fixes for the year 1990-91 the estimated aggregate amount of the housing support grants payable to certain local authorities under section 191 of the Housing (Scotland) Act 1987 and prescribes the method of apportionment among those authorities of the general and hostel portions of that amount.

items.title: The Import and Export (Plant Health Fees) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, which applies to Scotland only, and which revokes and replaces the Import and Export (Plant Health Fees) (Scotland) Order 1989, prescribes fees in respect of the services listed in Schedules 1 and 2 to the Order (articles 3 and 4).

items.title: The East Anglian, Essex and York (Pipelaying and Other Works) (Codes of Practice) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The East Anglian, Essex and York (Pipelaying and Other Works) (Codes of Practice) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Partnership (Unrestricted Size) No. 7 Regulations 1990
items.description: Section 716 of the Companies Act 1985 prohibits the formation of partnerships of more than 20 persons. These Regulations exempt from that prohibition partnerships formed for the purpose of carrying on business as town planners and consisting of persons at least three-quarters of whom are members (as defined) of the Royal Town Planning Institute.

items.title: The Electricity Act 1989 (Transfer Date) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order appoints 31st March 1990 as the transfer date on which the property, rights and liabilities of the Scottish Electricity Boards will be transferred, in accordance with schemes made under section 67 of the Electricity Act 1989, to successor companies nominated for the purpose by order of the Secretary of State. Those companies are nominated by the Electricity Act 1989 (Nominated Companies) (Scotland) Order 1989 (S.I. 1989/2448).

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation(Derbyshire Cablevision Limited) (Derby) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Derbyshire Cablevision Limited on 3rd September 1990a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the whole of the City of Derby and small parts of the Borough of Erewash and the District of South Derbyshire. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 3rd October 1990. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Derbyshire Cablevision Limited will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its Derby public telecommunication systems when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The River Don Catchment Area (Part) Protection Order 1990
items.description: This Order prohibits the fishing for or taking of freshwater fish in inland waters in the area prescribed, without legal right or written permission from someone having that right.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Registration Appeal Court) 1990
items.description: This Act of Sederunt appoints the three judges for hearing registration appeals under section 57 of the Representation of the People Act 1983 and revokes the Act of Sederunt (Registration Appeal Court) 1989.

items.title: The British Railways (Penalty Fares) Act 1989 (Activating No. 1) Order 1990
items.description: This Order gives effect to the penalty fares provisions contained in sections 4 to 9 of the British Railways (Penalty Fares) Act 1989, with respect to the train services specified in the Schedule, as from the 29th October 1990.

items.title: The London Government Reorganisation (Pensions etc.) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the London Government Reorganisation (Pensions etc.) Order 1989 so as to apply to the London Pensions Fund Authority the provisions of section 73 of the Local Government Act 1985 and to disapply the provisions of section 151 of the Local Government Act 1972 (c. 70.). Both sections are concerned with responsibility for financial administration.

items.title: The Commission for the New Towns (Specified Date) (Tenancies) Order 1990
items.description: Section 38 of the Housing Act 1988 governs the status of certain public sector residential tenancies transferred to the private sector. The tenancies concerned are those existing on the commencement of the Act or entered into pursuant to a contract made before the commencement. In the case of a disposal by the Commission for the New Towns in pursuance of a direction under section 37 of the New Towns Act 1981 (c. 64), “the commencement of the Act” is to be interpreted as 14th November 1990 or such other date as may be specified by Order, This Order specifies 31st March 1996 for the purposes of section 38(4).

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) (No. 4) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations 1986. Apart from minor drafting changes, the Regulations make the following changes of substance.

items.title: The Trustee Savings Banks Act 1985 (Appointed Day)(No. 7) Order 1990
items.description: This Order appoints 31st October 1990 as the date for the purpose of sections 2(4) and 4(2) of the Trustee Savings Banks Act 1985 (abolition of the Central Board and dissolution of the trustee savings banks certified under the Trustee Savings Banks Act, 1969 or 1981) and for the repeal of those provisions of the Trustee Savings Banks Act 1981 which are set out in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Lead in Cattle) (England) (Revocation) Order 1990
items.description: This Order revokes the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Lead in Cattle) (England) Order 1990 which contained emergency prohibitions in order to prevent human consumption of food which may have been rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of animals consuming lead.

items.title: The Fire Safety and Safety of Places of Sport Act 1987 (Commencement No. 6) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 31st December 1990 section 47 of the Fire Safety and Safety of Places of Sport Act 1987 which makes fresh provision regarding the voltage above which a luminous tube sign may be required to be fitted with a firemen’s cut-off switch.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Medical Examination) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Merchant Shipping (Medical Examination) Regulations 1983. The principal changes are to regulation 9, to enable an approved medical practitioner to suspend or cancel a medical fitness certificate if, when the certificate was issued the seafarer could not in fact have met the appropriate medical standards, or if the certificate when issued was incorrect.

items.title: The Personal Community Charge (Students)(Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Personal Community Charge (Students) Regulations 1989 which make provision, as regards the community charges of charging authorities in England and Wales, with respect to persons undertaking full-time courses of education.

items.title: The Iraq and Kuwait (United Nations Sanctions) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, imposes restrictions pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No. 670 of 25th September 1990, on aircraft overflying the United Kingdom or the Channel Islands or the Isle of Man, where their destination is Iraq or Kuwait. It also imposes restrictions on aircraft registered in, or operated from the United Kingdom, the Channel Islands or the Isle of Man, overflying other states where their destination is Iraq or Kuwait. This Order also imposes restrictions on ships registered in Iraq entering ports in the United Kingdom or the Channel Islands or the Isle of Man.

items.title: The Iraq and Kuwait (United Nations Sanctions) (Dependent Territories) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order made under the United Nations Act 1946, imposes restrictions pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No. 670 of 25th September 1990, on aircraft overflying each of the territories specified in the Schedule where their destination is Iraq or Kuwait. This Order also imposes restrictions on aircraft registered in or operated from those territories overflying other states where their destination is Iraq or Kuwait. It also imposes restrictions on ships registered in Iraq entering ports in those territories.

items.title: The Education (Grant) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make three categories of provision. First, they revoke and, with modifications, replace the provisions of the Education (Grant) Regulations 1983. They also revoke a number of other Regulations made under section 100(1)(b) of the Education Act 1944, being Regulations which are spent or which authorise grants to be paid to a specified body (regulation 5 and the Schedule).

items.title: The Official Secrets Act 1989 (Commencement) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st March 1990 the Official Secrets Act 1989.

items.title: The Personal Community Charge (Relief) (England) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations set out a scheme for reducing the liability of individuals in England to pay a personal community charge. The scheme applies for the three successive financial years starting with the year beginning 1st April 1990 (regulation 3).

items.title: The Banking Act 1987 (Exempt Transactions) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Banking Act 1987 (Exempt Transactions) Regulations 1988 (the 1988 Regulations).

items.title: The Official Secrets Act 1989 (Prescription) Order 1990
items.description: This Order prescribes certain persons and bodies for the purposes of the Official Secrets Act 1989. The effect of the Order is that those persons who are prescribed, under articles 2 and 3, taken with Schedules 1 and 2 respectively, become Crown servants for all purposes of the Act. The relevant persons are those listed in the right-hand column of Schedule 1 and in both columns of Schedule 2. Accordingly, the information which they hold by virtue of their position as persons prescribed by this Order becomes information to which the Act applies if it otherwise falls within the relevant sections (1 to 4 and 8), and such persons will be treated as if they were Crown servants for the purposes of the provisions of the Act relating to the prosecution of offenders and the authorisation of disclosures. Three bodies are prescribed under article 4, taken with Schedule 3. The Order enables them for the purposes of the Act to authorise or restrict disclosures and also, in one case, to direct the return or disposal of documents.

items.title: The London Cab (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the London Cab Order 1934 (“the 1934 Order”) primarily to provide for an extra charge of £1.20 for hirings of motor cabs arranged by telephone. In addition provisions of paragraph 35 (approved taximeters) rendered otiose by the revocation of Schedule E (fares during transitional period) by the London Cab Order 1989, (S.I. 1989/848), have been omitted.

items.title: The Revenue Support Grant (Specified Bodies) Regulations 1990
items.description: Sections 78 and 79 of the Local Government Finance Act 1988 provide for revenue support grant to be payable to any body which provides services for local authorities and which is specified by the Secretary of State in regulations.

items.title: The Copyright (Certification of Licensing Scheme for Educational Recording of Broadcasts) (Guild Sound and Vision Limited) (Revocation) Order 1990
items.description: Under section 143(2) of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 (“the Act”), the Secretary of State certified with effect from 30th May 1990 a licensing scheme operated by Guild Sound and Vision Limited for the purposes of section 35 of the Act (recordings by educational establishments of broadcasts and cable programmes).

items.title: The Copyright (Certification of Licensing Scheme for Educational Recording of Broadcasts) (Open University Educational Enterprises Limited) Order 1990
items.description: Under section 35 of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 recordings of broadcasts and cable programmes may be made by or on behalf of educational establishments without thereby infringing copyright. The section does not, however, apply if and to the extent that there is a licensing scheme certified for the purposes of that section providing for the grant of licences.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation(Cable Television Limited) (Borough of Northampton) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Cable Television Limited on 4th September 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the Borough of Northampton area. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 17th October 1990. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Cable Television Limited will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its Borough of Northampton public telecommunication systems when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Welfare Food Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Welfare Food Regulations 1988 (the principal Regulations).

items.title: The A421 Trunk Road (Wendlebury to Bicester Section, Slip Roads) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A421 Trunk Road (Wendlebury to Bicester Section, Slip Roads) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Civil List (Increase of Financial Provision) Order 1990
items.description: This Order increases the annual payment for the Queen’s Civil List, namely that sum appropriated to the salaries and expenses of Her Majesty’s Household, and royal bounty, alms and special services. The amount now authorised by this Order is £7,900,000, in place of £3,850,000 fixed by the Civil List (Increase of Financial Provision) Order 1984 (“the 1984 Order”) (article 2).

items.title: The Annual Close Time (River Tay Salmon Fishery District) Order 1990
items.description: In relation to the annual close time of the River Tay salmon fishery district (21st August to 4th February) this Order prescribes the period from 21st August to 31st October as the period during which fishing for salmon and sea trout by rod and line is permitted in a specified part of the district. This part comprises the catchment area of the River Eden, all other waters flowing into St Andrews Bay and the adjacent area of sea between, in the north, a line drawn due east from the eastern end of Abertay Sands and westwards from there along the ridge of the Sands to Tentsmuir Point and, in the south, the southern boundary of the salmon fishery district. Within that specified part it will be permissible to fish for salmon and sea trout by rod and line during the period in each year from 5th February to 31st October. By virtue of section 1(1) of the Salmon Act 1986 the area of the salmon fishery district extends seaward for a distance of 3 miles from mean low water springs of the coastal limits of the district and landward to include the catchment area of each river flowing directly or indirectly into the sea within those coastal limits.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Modification) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations are made under section 64 of the Social Security Act 1973.

items.title: The Charging Authorities (Notification of Precept Population) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Charging Authorities (Notification of Precept Population) (Wales) Regulations 1989 so that the relevant day for the purposes of those regulations will be 15th November, instead of 1st November, of the financial year preceding the financial year concerned. (The relevant day is the day which governs the time for the notification of certain population figures required for precepting purposes.)

items.title: The Charging Authorities (Population for Precepts) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Charging Authorities (Population for Precepts) (Wales) Regulations 1989 so that the relevant day used in calculations under those regulations will be 15th November (instead of 1st November) of the financial year preceding that for which the calculation is to be made. (The relevant day is the day in relation to which register data is to be extracted to enable the relevant population of an area to be calculated).

items.title: The National Health Service Trusts (Membership and Procedure) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision concerning the membership and procedure of NHS trusts established under the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. They include in Part II provisions relating to the number, appointment, qualifications and description of directors and joint directorships (regulations 2 to 6), the tenure of office of directors and the termination of tenure of office and eligibility for re-appointment (regulations 7 to 10) and for disqualification for appointment as director and cessation of disqualification (regulations 11 and 12). In Part III provisions are included relating to vice-chairmen (regulations 13 and 14), the appointment of and the exercise of functions by committees and sub-committees (regulations 15 to 18) and meetings and proceedings of a trust including disability for taking part in proceedings on account of pecuniary interest (regulations 19 and 20 and the Schedule).

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Alteration of Lists and Appeals) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Non-Domestic Rating (Alteration of Lists and Appeals) Regulations 1990 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Control of Substances Hazardous to Health (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Control of Substances Hazardous to Health Regulations 1988 (“the principal Regulations”) by substituting for Schedule 1 to the principal Regulations the Schedule set out in the Schedule to these Regulations. Schedule 1 specifies maximum exposure limits for certain substances which are hazardous to health.

items.title: The Fees for Inquiries (Standard Daily Amount) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to England and Wales, apply where a Minister is authorised under or by virtue of specified provisions to recover his costs in connection with the holding of an inquiry.

items.title: The Land Registration Fees (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: The Order, which, as from 3rd December 1990, supersedes the Land Registration Fee Order 1990, revises the land registration fee scales and makes certain other changes.

items.title: The Education (Eligibility of Primary Schools for Grant-maintained Status) Order 1990
items.description: Section 52(6) of the Education Reform Act 1988 provides that primary schools which have less than 300 pupils are not eligible for grant-maintained status. This Order removes that limitation and provides that all primary schools shall be so eligible, subject to the general provisions in subsections (8) and (9) of section 52. Subsections (6) and (7), which in consequence of this Order are spent, are repealed.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning General Development (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order substitutes a new Class G in Part 17 of Schedule 2 to the Town and Country Planning General Development Order 1988.

items.title: The International Carriage of Perishable Foodstuffs (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the definition of the Agreement on the International Carriage of Perishable Foodstuffs and the Special Equipment Used for such Carriage (ATP) of 1st September 1970 in the International Carriage of Perishable Foodstuffs Regulations 1985 to take account of a further amendment to the agreement.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions)(Copper or Other Metal Poisoning) (England) (Revocation) Order 1990
items.description: This Order revokes the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Copper or Other Metal Poisoning) (England) Order 1990 which contained emergency prohibitions in order to prevent human consumption of food which may have been rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of animals consuming copper or some other metal.

items.title: The Overhead Lines (Exemption) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the cases where the consent of the Secretary of State is not required under section 37 of the Electricity Act 1989 to the installation or keeping installed of an electric line above ground. The cases are specified in regulation 3 subject in certain circumstances to the limitations in regulation 4 and the restrictions in regulation 5.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Insurance) Order 1990
items.description: The effect of this Order is to extend to insurers established outside the United Kingdom the exemption from valued added tax of the provision of insurance and reinsurance against any of the risks or other things described in Schedules 1 and 2 to the Insurance Companies Act 1982 (c. 50).

items.title: The Industrial Training (Clothing and Allied Products Board) (Revocation) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Industrial Training (Clothing and Allied Products Board) (Revocation) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemption) (England) (No. 3) Order 1990
items.description: Under Part I of the Local Government Act 1988 work falling within certain defined activities may only be carried out by local authorities if particular conditions are fulfilled.

items.title: The Sea Fish (Specified Manx Waters) Licensing Order 1990 (revoked)
items.description: This Order consolidates with variations the Herring and White Fish (Specified Manx Waters) Licensing Order 1983, as varied, (“the 1983 Order”) which prohibited fishing for herring and for descriptions of white fish specified in the Order by British fishing boats within the twelve-mile belt around the Isle of Man but outside territorial waters without a licence. This Order extends the scope of that prohibition to include fishing for any sea fish (other than salmon or migratory trout) (article 3(1)). This Order extends to the United Kingdom and the Isle of Man (article 1(2)).

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Specified Western Waters) (Restrictions on Landing) Order 1990
items.description: This Order replaces the the Sea Fishing (Specified Western Waters) (Restrictions on Landing) Order 1987 (“the 1987 Order”) which prohibited, with exceptions, the landing in the United Kingdom of sea fish of descriptions specified in that Order caught in ICES sub-area VII or VIII. This Order extends the scope of that prohibition to include the landing of any sea fish (other than salmon or migratory trout), subject to exceptions (articles 3 and 4). The prohibition on landing in the 1987 Order did not apply to landing by any British fishing boat whose length was not more than 10 metres. This Order disapplies that exemption in the case of herring caught in certain divisions of ICES sub-area VII (article 4(b)) and amends the definition of “length” to that calculated in accordance with the rules specified in Article 2(1) of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2930/86 (OJ No. L274, 25.9.86, p. 1) (article 2). The Order also makes two new exemptions to the prohibition on landing (article 4(e) and (f)).

items.title: The Deposit Protection Board (Increase of Borrowing Limit) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order prescribes a limit of £50 million for the borrowing power of the Deposit Protection Board in place of the limit of £10 million stipulated in section 64(1) of the Banking Act 1987. It also revokes an earlier Order which was defective.

items.title: The Housing (Right to Buy) (Prescribed Forms) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the prescribed form of notice to be used by a landlord to accept or reject a secure tenant’s claim to exercise the right to buy under Part V of the Housing Act 1985. They are consequential on section 164 of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 which substitutes in Schedule 5 to the 1985 Act (exceptions to the right to buy) a new paragraph 11 (certain dwelling-houses for persons of pensionable age).

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Independent Trustee) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations are made under sections 57C and 57D of the Social Security Pensions Act 1975 and section 21 of the Social Security Act 1990. Sections 57C and 57D were inserted into the Social Security Pensions Act 1975 by paragraph 1 of Schedule 4 to the Social Security Act 1990.

items.title: The London—Brighton Trunk Road (A23 Bolney Flyover Slip Roads) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The London—Brighton Trunk Road (A23 Bolney Flyover Slip Roads) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Competition) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: Regulation 5 of the Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Competition) (England) Regulations 1988 (S.I. 1988/1371) provides that section 6 of the Local Government Act 1988 (restrictions on carrying out functional work) shall apply where certain authorities propose to carry out certain work after the dates specified in Schedules 2, 3 and 4 of those Regulations.

items.title: The Police Cadets (Scotland) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Police Cadets (Scotland) Regulations 1968.

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1988 (Commencement No. 12) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 26th November 1990 for certain purposes section 32 of the Criminal Justice Act 1988 so far as it is not already in force. The effect of the Order is to enable evidence to be given at a trial on indictment or an appeal through a live television link where the witness is outside the United Kingdom for the following purposes: proceedings for murder, manslaughter or any other offence of killing any person; proceedings being conducted by the Director of the Serious Fraud Office as appearing to him to involve serious or complex fraud; and proceedings for serious and complex fraud where there has been given a notice of transfer(which has the effect of bypassing the committal stage).

items.title: The Jam and Similar Products (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which apply in England and Wales only, further amend the Jam and Similar Products Regulations 1981 (“the principal Regulations”). These Regulations implement Council Directive No. 88/593/EEC (OJ No. L318, 25.11.88, p. 44) which amends Council Directive No. 79/693/EEC on the approximation of the laws of the Member States relating to fruit jams, jellies and marmalades and chestnut puree (OJ No. L205, 13.8.79, p. 5). The Regulations also make other provision for products to which the Directives do not apply.

items.title: The East of Abercynon – East of Dowlais Trunk Road (A4060) (Phase 2 – Improvement between Pentrebach and Mountain Hare and Slip Roads) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The East of Abercynon – East of Dowlais Trunk Road (A4060) (Phase 2 – Improvement between Pentrebach and Mountain Hare and Slip Roads) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Petty Sessional Divisions (Cornwall) Order 1990
items.description: This Order gives effect to a draft Order submitted by the magistrates' courts committee for the county of Cornwall and provides for the combination of the petty sessional divisions of Helston and Kerrier and Falmouth and Penryn to form one new division to be known as the Falmouth and Kerrier petty sessional division and for the combination of the petty sessional divisions of Dunheved and Stratton to form one new division to be known as the Dunheved and Stratton petty sessional division.

items.title: The Medicines (Fees Relating to Medicinal Products for Human Use) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Medicines (Fees Relating to Medicinal Products for Human Use) Regulations 1989 which prescribe fees in connection with applications and inspections relating to licences and certificates granted under the Medicines Act 1968 so far as they apply to medicinal products for human use only.

items.title: The Petty Sessional Divisions (Somerset) Order 1990
items.description: This Order gives effect to a draft Order submitted by the magistrates' courts committee for the county of Somerset and provides for the combination of the petty sessional divisions of Ilminster, Somerton, Wincanton and Yeovil to form a new division to be known as the South Somerset petty sessional division and for the combination of the petty sessional divisions of Frome, Shepton Mallet and Wells to be combined to form a new division to be known as the Mendip petty sessional division.

items.title: The Retirement Benefits Schemes (Continuation of Rights of Members of Approved Schemes) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which have effect as from 14th March 1989–

items.title: The Testing in Primary Schools (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations require education authorities to test the level of certain aspects of the educational attainment of primary school pupils, who are attending schools under their management and who are at certain stages in their primary education.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Amendment of Sheriff Court Ordinary Cause, Summary Cause, and Small Claim, Rules) (No.2) 1990
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Ordinary Cause, Summary Cause, and Small Claim, Rules by removing the requirement to lodge a sum of money with the court on applying to be reponed or to recall a decree.

items.title: Act of Adjournal (Consolidation Amendment No.2) (Miscellaneous) 1990
items.description: This version of the Act of Adjournal (Consolidation Amendment No.2) (Miscellaneous) 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The LondonFolkestoneDover Trunk Road A20 (Court Wood to Dover Section) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The LondonFolkestoneDover Trunk Road A20 (Court Wood to Dover Section) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A1 Trunk Road (Gatenby Lane Junction, Leeming Slip Roads) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A1 Trunk Road (Gatenby Lane Junction, Leeming Slip Roads) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Finance Act 1985 (Interest on Tax) (Prescribed Rate)(No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order reduces from 13 per cent to 12.25 per cent the prescribed rate of interest for the purposes of section 18 of the Finance Act 1985 (interest on tax etc. recovered or recoverable by assessment).

items.title: The A5 London—Holyhead Trunk Road (Little Brickhill Bypass and Slip Roads) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A5 London—Holyhead Trunk Road (Little Brickhill Bypass and Slip Roads) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A5 London—Holyhead Trunk Road (Little Brickhill) Detrunking Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A5 London—Holyhead Trunk Road (Little Brickhill) Detrunking Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Applications under section 57C of the Social Security Pensions Act 1975) 1990
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes provision for the manner of application for an order requiring an insolvency practitioner to discharge his duties under section 57C of the Social Security Pensions Act 1975 (which require him to satisfy himself that one of the trustees of an occupational pension scheme to which the section applies is an independent person or to appoint such a person to that scheme).

items.title: The Offshore Installations (Safety Zones) (No. 6) Order 1990
items.description: This Order establishes, under section 22 of the Petroleum Act 1987, a safety zone, having a radius of 500 metres from a specified point, around the installation specified in the Schedule to this Order and stationed in waters to which section 21(7) of that Act applies (these include territorial waters and waters in areas designated under section 1(7) of the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29)).

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No.5) (Miscellaneous) 1990
items.description: This Act of Sederunt–1amends the Rules of the Court of Session by–amaking new provision for the lodging of caveats (and consequential amendments) (paragraph 2(2), (3), (9), (10) and (16)(c));bremoving the requirement in rule 91C to lodge Form 64 and omitting that Form from the Appendix (paragraph 2(4) and (16)(a));cinserting a rule in relation to the issue of a jury precept where a diet for a jury trial has been fixed (paragraph 2(5));daltering the definition of commercial cause (paragraph 2(6));ealtering the reference in rule 188J(8) to paragraph (7) of rule 91 to that of paragraph (6) of rule 91 (paragraph 2(7));fproviding for applications under section 245B of the Companies Act 1985 (c. 6) to be by petition presented to the Outer House (paragraph 2(8));ginserting references to the Trade Marks Act 1938 (c. 22) and the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 (c. 48) in rule 251 (paragraphs 2(11));hremoving the provision for application to treat as commercial causes certain applications and references under certain patent, designs and defence contracts legislation under rule 257E (paragraph 2(12));iinserting a reference in rule 260P (1) to dependent territories in the definition of “appropriate court” in relation to registration and enforcement of custody orders under the Family Law Act 1986 (c. 55), and making consequential amendments (paragraph 2(13) and (14));jmaking minor drafting amendments to rule 268 and introducing a procedure for applying for leave to appeal from an inferior court out of time (paragraph 2(15));kmaking a minor amendment in the heading of Form 69 in the Appendix to include a reference to a tutor and a curator (paragraph 2(16)); and2revokes the Act of Sederunt to regulate proceedings under the Workmen’s Compensation (Transfer of Funds) Act 1927, 1935 and the Acts of Sederunt which amended it following the repeal of the Workmen’s Compensation (Transfer of Funds) Acts 1927 (c. 15) and 1943 (c. 6) by the Statute Law (Repeals) Act 1989 (c. 43), section 1 and Schedule 1, Part II (paragraph 3 and Schedule 2).

items.title: The London-Folkestone-Dover Trunk Road A20 (Dawkinge Wood to Court Wood Section and Slip Roads) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The London-Folkestone-Dover Trunk Road A20 (Dawkinge Wood to Court Wood Section and Slip Roads) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Load Line) (Amendment) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules amend the Merchant Shipping (Load Line) Rules 1968. The types of ships for which stability information may be approved by an Assigning Authority, other than the Secretary of State, are extended to include ships which are mobile offshore units as defined in these Rules and in paragraph 1.3 of Chapter 1 of the Code for the Construction and Equipment of Mobile Offshore Drilling Units adopted by the International Maritime Organisation, and ships which are mobile offshore support units including accommodation units, crane barges and other types of column stabilised units.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Charities) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order adds National Health Service trusts to the list of eligible bodies entitled to receive, as zero-rated supplies, the supply of certain relevant goods which are purchased from funds provided by a charity or from voluntary contributions. It places National Health Service trusts on a par with health authorities in England and Wales and Health Boards in Scotland.

items.title: The London-Folkestone-Dover Trunk Road A20 (Folkestone-Dover Section Detrunking) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The London-Folkestone-Dover Trunk Road A20 (Folkestone-Dover Section Detrunking) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Drainage Charges Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the method of ascertaining, under section 49 of the Land Drainage Act 1976 (“the 1976 Act”), the amount per hectare of a general drainage charge raised after 31st March 1990 by the National Rivers Authority (“the Authority”) in respect of a local flood defence district under section 48 of the 1976 Act.

items.title: The European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (Immunities and Privileges) Order 1990
items.description: This Order confers privileges and immunities on the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development, its officers, employees and experts performing missions for the Bank. These privileges and immunities are conferred in accordance with Chapter VIII of the Agreement establishing the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (Cm.1116 ). It will enable Her Majesty’s Government to give effect to Chapter VIII of the Agreement, and will come into force on the date on which the Agreement enters into force with respect to the United Kingdom.

items.title: The European Communities (Designation) (No. 3)Order 1990 (revoked)
items.description: This is a further Order in the series designating the Minister who may exercise powers to make regulations conferred by section 2(2) of the European Communities Act 1972 and specifying the matters in relation to which those powers may be exercised.

items.title: The Iraq and Kuwait (United Nations Sanctions)(Second Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order is made under the United Nations Act 1946 and further amends the Iraq and Kuwait (United Nations Sanctions) Order 1990 (“the principal Order”). The amendments to article 6(8), article 8 and the Schedule to the principal Order increase the maximum term of imprisonment for a conviction on indictment for an offence against the Order (with the exception of offences against article 6(8) and paragraph 5 of the Schedule) from two years to five years. As regards offences against article 6(8) and paragraph 5 of the Schedule, certain offences will now be triable summarily and other offences will be triable either summarily or on indictment and the maximum term of imprisonment for a conviction on indictment will be two years. The amendment to article 8 also provides for all offences against the Order to be arrestable offences within the meaning of the Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 and for the extension of the time limits for bringing summary proceedings. The Order also amends article 10 of the principal Order by deleting paragraph (2).

items.title: The Civil Aviation Act 1982 (Jersey) Order 1990
items.description: This Order extends those provisions of the Civil Aviation Act 1982 which are set out in Part I of Schedule 1 to this Order to the Bailiwick of Jersey; the provisions are subject to the modifications specified in Part II of that Schedule. The Order also revokes the Orders specified in Schedule 2 to this Order to the extent that they relate to the extension to the Bailiwick of Jersey of enactments replaced by that Act.

items.title: The Education (Unrecognised Degrees) (Guernsey) Order 1990
items.description: This Order extends sections 214 to 216 (unrecognised degrees) of the Education Reform Act 1988 to the Bailiwick of Guernsey with the adaptations and modifications specified in article 2 of the Order.

items.title: The Fishing Vessels (Life-Saving Appliances) (Guernsey) Order 1990
items.description: This Order extends the Fishing Vessels (Life-Saving Appliances) Regulations 1988 to the Bailiwick of Guernsey with the modifications specified in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Fishing Vessels (Safety Provisions) (Guernsey)Order 1990
items.description: This Order extends to the Bailiwick of Guernsey, with the exceptions, adaptations and modifications specified in the Schedule, the Fishing Vessels (Safety Provisions) Act 1970 and section 86 of the Merchant Shipping Act 1970 together with the Fishing Vessels (Safety Provisions) Rules 1975 made thereunder.

items.title: The Local Government Boundaries Commissioner (Northern Ireland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends section 50(1) of the Local Government Act (Northern Ireland) 1972. That provision provides for the appointment of a Local Government Boundaries Commissioner whose function is to review the number, boundaries and names of districts in Northern Ireland and the number, boundaries and names of the wards into which each district is divided.

items.title: The Prevention of Terrorism (Temporary Provisions) Act 1989 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 5th March 1990 the provisions of the Prevention of Terrorism (Temporary Provisions) Act 1989 so far as not already in force (ie paragraphs 10, 20 and 30 of Schedule 4 (enforcement in England and Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland respectively of orders made in designated countries) and paragraph 34 of that Schedule (insolvency practitioners in the Islands and designated countries)) except the repeal by Schedule 9 to that Act of paragraph 9 of Schedule 7 to the Act which, by virtue of section 27(4), shall come into force on the coming into force of the Land Registration Act 1988 (c. 3)

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Safety Convention) (Guernsey) Order 1990
items.description: This Order extends to the Bailiwick of Guernsey, with the exceptions, adaptations and modifications specified in the Schedule, certain provisions of the Merchant Shipping (Safety Convention) Act 1949 and the Merchant Shipping Act 1964.

items.title: The Prevention of Terrorism (Temporary Provisions) Act 1984 (Isle of Man) (Revocation) Order 1990
items.description: This Order revokes the Prevention of Terrorism (Temporary Provisions) Act 1984 (Isle of Man) Order 1984, which extended that Act to the Isle of Man with exceptions, adaptations and modifications. The Prevention of Terrorism (Temporary Provisions) Act 1989 which repealed and replaced the 1984 Act is not extended to the Isle of Man, where a corresponding Act of Tynwald applies.

items.title: The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Netherlands) Order 1990
items.description: The Protocol scheduled to this Order makes certain alterations to the Convention set out in the Schedule to the Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Netherlands) Order 1980 (S.I. 1980/1961), as amended by the Protocol set out in the Schedule to the Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Netherlands) Order 1983 (S.I. 1983/1902) (“the 1983 Protocol”).

items.title: The Copyright (Application to Other Countries) (No. 2) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order further amends the Copyright (Application to Other Countries) (No. 2) Order 1989 to take account of Malaysia’s accession to the Berne Copyright Convention and the making of provisions under its laws, in respect of sound recordings and broadcasts, giving adequate protection to owners of copyright in such works under Part I of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988.

items.title: The Air Navigation (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Air Navigation Order 1989.

items.title: The Criminal Appeal (Amendment) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules amend the Criminal Appeal Rules 1968 so as to provide for applications under section 32(1)(a) of the Criminal Justice Act 1988 (Evidence through television links of persons outside the United Kingdom). Section 32(1)(a) came into force for the purposes of proceedings for homicide, proceedings being conducted by the Director of the Serious Fraud Office and proceedings for serious and complex fraud where there has been given a notice of transfer, on 26th November 1990 (Criminal Justice Act 1988 (Commencement No. 12) Order 1990 (S.I. 1990/2084)).

items.title: The Crown Court (Amendment) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules amend the Crown Court Rules 1982 so as to provide for applications under section 32(1)of the Criminal Justice Act 1988 (evidence through live television link where a witness is outside the United Kingdom). Section 32(1)(a) was brought into force for the purposes of proceedings for homicide, proceedings being conducted by the Director of the Serious Fraud Office and proceedings for serious and complex fraud where there has been given a notice of transfer, on 26th November 1990, (Criminal Justice Act 1988 (Commencement No. 12) Order 1990 (S.I. 1990/2084)).

items.title: The Credit Cards (Merchant Acquisition) Order 1990
items.description: On and after 7th March 1991, this Order makes it unlawful for a payment card organization to make or carry out any agreement or arrangement to the extent that it requires or has the effect of requiring a person authorized to carry on business as a payment card issuer in the United Kingdom to issue any payment cards before that person can act as a merchant acquirer in the United Kingdom. It also prohibits a payment card organization from making or carrying out any agreement or arrangement to the extent that it prevents or has the effect of preventing a person authorized to carry on business as a payment card issuer from acting as a merchant acquirer after the person commences to carry on business in the United Kingdom as a credit card issuer, or from withholding supplies or services relative to the conduct of the business of merchant acquisition after that time, unless the payment card organization has reasonable grounds to believe that the card issuer is not capable of efficiently conducting the business of merchant acquisition or is unlikely to comply with the operating requirements of the payment card organization with respect to the conduct of such business. All such agreements or arrangements must be terminated before 7th March 1991.

items.title: The Credit Cards (Price Discrimination) Order 1990
items.description: On and after 7th March 1991, this Order makes it unlawful for any person to make or carry out any agreement relating to credit cards to the extent that it imposes or requires the imposition of a “no discrimination rule”. A “no discrimination rule” prohibits merchants from charging different prices to those who pay by credit card rather than by another means of payment. All such existing agreements must be terminated before 7th March 1991.

items.title: The Offshore Installations (Safety Zones) Order 1990
items.description: This Order establishes, under section 22 of the Petroleum Act 1987, safety zones, having a radius of 500 metres from a specified point, around the installations specified in the Schedule to the Order and stationed in waters to which section 21(7) of that Act applies (these include territorial waters and waters in areas designated under section 1(7) of the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29)).

items.title: The National Health Service Trusts(Membership and Procedure) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service Trusts (Membership and Procedure) Regulations 1990 (“the principal Regulations”) which provide, amongst other things, for the membership of NHS trusts constituted under the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990.

items.title: The Nursing Homes and Mental Nursing Homes (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations increase the fees payable under the Nursing Homes and Mental Nursing Homes Regulations 1984.

items.title: The Human Fertilisation and Embryology Act 1990 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 7th November 1990 those provisions of the Human Fertilisation and Embryology Act 1990 which relate to the setting up of the Human Fertilisation and Embryology Authority established by the Act, and to the offence of disclosure of certain confidential information by members or employees of the Authority, together with related provisions about enforcement. It also brings into force on the same date the provision of the Act dealing with the preparation of the Authority’s Code of Practice.

items.title: The Channel Tunnel (Customs and Excise) Order 1990
items.description: The effect of this Order is to make such additional provision as is considered necessary or expedient for customs and excise control consequent upon the establishment of a tunnel link between the United Kingdom and France.

items.title: The Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 (Commencement No. 6) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 7th January 1991 the last remaining provisions of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988, namely, certain of the provisions (contained in Schedule 5) which amend the Patents Act 1949 (c. 87)and the Patents Act 1977, and the repeal of certain provisions of the latter Act (contained in Schedule 8).

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation(Coastal Cablevision of Dorset Ltd) (West Dorset)Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Coastal Cablevision of Dorset Ltd on 28th September 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the greater part of West Dorset District and the whole of the Borough of Weymouth and Portland. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 7th November 1990. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Coastal Cablevision of Dorset Ltd will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) (Revocation) Order 1990
items.description: This Order revokes the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) Order 1990, which contained emergency prohibitions on various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of lead into material used in the manufacture of imported animal feeding stuff.

items.title: The Courts and Legal Services Act 1990 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st November 1990 a provision in the repeals schedule of the Courts and Legal Services Act 1990. This provision is section 53 of the Administration of Justice Act 1956 which provides that only certain barristers or solicitors are eligible for appointment as Chief Land Registrar or as registrar or assistant registrar in the Land Registry.

items.title: The Mid Southern, Northumbrian and Southern (Pipelaying and Other Works) (Codes of Practice) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Mid Southern, Northumbrian and Southern (Pipelaying and Other Works) (Codes of Practice) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Newcastle and Gateshead Water (Constitution and Regulation) Order 1990
items.description: Section 101(2) of the Water Act 1989 provides for the replacement of provisions relating to the constitution and regulation of a statutory water company contained in its local Acts and Orders by provisions contained in a memorandum and articles which have been approved by special resolution of the members of the company. The replacement provisions have effect only if they are approved by the Secretary of State and come into force on such date as he determines.

items.title: The Sunderland and South Shields (Constitution and Regulation) Order 1990
items.description: Section 101(2) of the Water Act 1989 provides for the replacement of provisions relating to the constitution and regulation of a statutory water company contained in its local Acts and Orders by provisions contained in a memorandum and articles which have been approved by special resolution of the members of the company. The replacement provisions have effect only if they are approved by the Secretary of State and come into force on such date as he determines.

items.title: The Building Standards (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Building Standards (Scotland) Regulations 1981 to 1987. The Regulations apply to any construction, alteration or change of use of a building, subject to certain exceptions (regulation 3 and Schedule 1). The Regulations come into force on 1st April 1991 but do not apply where application for the building warrant was made before that date (regulation 34(2)).

items.title: The Statutory Maternity Pay (Compensation of Employers) and Statutory Sick Pay (Additional Compensation of Employers) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Statutory Maternity Pay (Compensation of Employers) Regulations 1987 (“the 1987 Regulations”) and the Statutory Sick Pay (Additional Compensation of Employers and Consequential Amendments) Regulations 1985 (“the 1985 Regulations”).

items.title: The Jam and Similar Products (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Scotland only, further amend the Jam and Similar Products (Scotland) Regulations 1981 (“the principal Regulations”). These Regulations implement Council Directive 88/593/EEC (OJ No L318, 25.11.88, p.44) which amends Council Directive 79/693/EEC (OJ No. L205, 13.8.79, p.5) on the approximation of the laws of the Member States relating to fruit jams, jellies and marmalades and chestnut puree. The Regulations also make other provision for products to which the Directives do not apply.

items.title: The Electricity (Amendment of Scottish Pension Schemes) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make certain amendments to the 2 pension schemes for the electricity industry in Scotland, namely the Hydro-Electric Pension Scheme (formerly called the Hydroboard Superannuation Fund) (“the North Scheme”) and the ScottishPower Pension Scheme (formerly called the South of Scotland Electricity Board’s Superannuation Scheme) (“the South Scheme”). The amendments are related to the transfer, under the Electricity Act 1989, of property, rights and liabilities of the North of Scotland Hydro-Electric Board and the South of Scotland Electricity Board to the successor companies, Scottish Hydro-Electric plc, Scottish Power plc and Scottish Nuclear Limited. The transfer date was 31st March 1990. After that date employees of Scottish Hydro-Electric plc generally participate in the North Scheme and employees of the other two successor companies generally participate in the South Scheme.

items.title: The River Nairn Salmon Fishery District (Baits and Lures) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations specify natural prawns and shrimps and any part of them for the purposes of the definition of “rod and line” in section 24(1) of the Salmon and Freshwater Fisheries (Protection) (Scotland) Act 1951. They apply throughout the River Nairn salmon fishery district for the whole of each year. The effect is to prohibit the use of natural prawns and shrimps (or any part of them) as bait when fishing by rod and line for salmon and sea trout.

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Registration and Licensing) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Road Vehicles (Registration and Licensing) Regulations 1971 (“the primary Regulations”).

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Registration and Licensing) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Road Vehicles (Registration and Licensing) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1973 (“the primary Regulations”).

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Exceptions) (Wales) Regulations 1990
items.description: Section 10(3) of the Education Reform Act 1988 imposes on local education authorities, governing bodies and head teachers of schools the duty to teach the subjects comprising the National Curriculum for a reasonable time during each of the key stages defined in the Act. This duty is replaced by the provisions of the attainment targets and programmes of study in a subject in the National Curriculum when these come into force by order of the Secretary of State. In respect of Welsh, the attainment targets and programmes of study come into force over a period of time by virtue of the provisions of the Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in Welsh) Order 1990. (S.I. 1990/1082).

items.title: The Winchester—Preston Trunk Road (A34) (Stratford-upon-Avon) De-Trunking Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Winchester—Preston Trunk Road (A34) (Stratford-upon-Avon) De-Trunking Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The London-Portsmouth Trunk Road (A3 Milford Bypass and Slip Roads) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The London-Portsmouth Trunk Road (A3 Milford Bypass and Slip Roads) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (Wales) (Revocation) Order 1990
items.description: This Order revokes The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (Wales) Order 1990 which contained emergency prohibitions on various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of lead into material used in the manufacture of imported animal feeding stuff.

items.title: The London—Portsmouth Trunk Road (A3 Milford De-Trunking) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The London—Portsmouth Trunk Road (A3 Milford De-Trunking) (No. 2) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The London—Portsmouth Trunk Road (A3 Milford De-Trunking) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The London—Portsmouth Trunk Road (A3 Milford De-Trunking) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Jones Cable Group of South Hertfordshire Limited) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Jones Cable Group of South Hertfordshire Limited on 15th October 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the South Hertfordshire area. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 13th November 1990. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Jones Cable Group of South Hertfordshire Limited will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its South Hertfordshire public telecommunication systems when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1988 (Commencement No. 10) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 12th March 1990 certain provisions of the Criminal Justice Act 1988 concerning the issue of letters of request following the institution of criminal proceedings.

items.title: The (A69) Carlisle-Sunderland Trunk Road (Derwenthaugh to Scotswood Road De-Trunking) Order 1986 Amendment Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The (A69) Carlisle-Sunderland Trunk Road (Derwenthaugh to Scotswood Road De-Trunking) Order 1986 Amendment Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Edinburgh College of Art (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the First Schedule to the Edinburgh College of Art Order 1959 so as to permit governors of the College to appoint proxies to vote for them on certain questions coming before meetings of the governing body. The proxy must also be a governor.

items.title: The Building Societies (Investigation of Complaints) Order 1990
items.description: This Order varies Part II of Schedule 12 to the Building Societies Act 1986 which prescribes the matters action in relation to which must be subject to investigation under a complaints scheme if it is to qualify for recognition for the purposes of investigations under section 83 of the Act.

items.title: The Medicines (Pharmacies) (Applications for Registration and Fees) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Medicines (Pharmacies) (Applications for Registration and Fees) Regulations 1973 (“the principal regulations”). They increase the fees for registration of premises at which a retail pharmacy business is or is to be carried on, the subsequent annual fees (retention fees) and the penalty (payable in circumstances specified in section 76(2) of the Medicines Act 1968) for failure to pay retention fees.

items.title: The Social Security (Miscellaneous Provisions)Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Attendance Allowance) (No. 2) Regulations 1975, the Mobility Allowance Regulations 1975, the Mobility Allowance (Vehicle Scheme Beneficiaries) Regulations 1977, the Social Security Benefit (Computation of Earnings) Regulations 1978, the Social Security (Claims and Payments) Regulations 1987, the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987, the Community Charge Benefits (General) Regulations 1989 and the Social Security (Refunds) (Repayments of Contractual Maternity Pay) Regulations 1990.

items.title: The Social Security (Severe Disablement Allowance) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Social Security (Severe Disablement Allowance) Amendment Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Education (Training Grants) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Education (Training Grants) Regulations 1987.

items.title: The British Nationality (Hong Kong) Act 1990 (Commencement) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force forthwith the provisions of the British Nationality (Hong Kong) Act 1990 (“the Act of 1990”) except section 2(2) thereof (which relates to the loss of British Dependent Territories citizenship by those registered as British citizens under the Act of 1990).

items.title: The British Nationality (Hong Kong) (Registration of Citizens) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations contain provisions relating to the registration as British citizens by the Secretary of State of up to 50,000 persons recommended to him for that purpose by the Governor of Hong Kong under section 1(1) of the British Nationality (Hong Kong) Act 1990. Provision is made in relation to the notification of decisions (regulation 3), the taking of oaths of allegiance (regulation 4) and the certification of documents as true copies for the purpose of their production as evidence (regulation 5).

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) (No. 5) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations 1986.

items.title: The Veterinary Surgery (Blood Sampling) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Veterinary Surgery (Blood Sampling) Order 1983 by permitting the taking of blood from badgers by persons not qualified as veterinary surgeons or practitioners, which would otherwise be prohibited by section 19(1) of the Veterinary Surgeons Act 1966.

items.title: The National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 (Commencement No. 4 and Transitional Provision) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st April 1991 the provisions of Part III (Community Care: England and Wales) and Part V (Miscellaneous and General) of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 specified in the Schedule to this Order and makes a transitional provision in respect of inquiries under the National Assistance Act 1948.

items.title: The Housing (Change of Landlord) (Payment of Disposal Cost by Instalments) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: Part IV (that is, sections 93 to 114) of the Housing Act 1988 (Change of Landlord: secure tenants) confers the right to acquire the fee simple estate in buildings and other property from a public sector landlord. Where, in the exercise of that right, it is determined that the property to which the acquisition relates would not realise any price, section 99(3) of that Act provides that there is a disposal cost attributable to that property. Section 104(2A) provides for such a disposal cost to be paid by instalments, which may include interest, in prescribed cases.

items.title: The Bovine Spongiform Encephalopathy Compensation Order 1990
items.description: This Order prescribes the amount of compensation payable for bovine animals caused to be slaughtered by the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food under section 32 of the Animal Health Act 1981 because they are affected, or suspected of being affected, with bovine spongiform encephalopathy.

items.title: The Environmental Protection Act 1990 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 5th November 1990 sections 128 to 139 (save for the amendments made by sections 128, 130 and 132) of the Environmental Protection Act 1990 which relate to the creation of new Councils for nature conservation in Great Britain and countryside matters in Wales. The new Councils are called the Nature Conservancy Council for England, the Nature Conservancy Council for Scotland, and the Countryside Council for Wales. The new Councils will first exercise their functions on days to be appointed by order of the Secretary of State.

items.title: The Channel Tunnel (Fire Services, Immigration and Prevention of Terrorism) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, made under the Channel Tunnel Act 1987, affects legislation relating to fire services, immigration and prevention of terrorism in its application to the Channel Tunnel system.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Driver Licensing and Information Systems) Act 1989 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st December 1990 the provisions of the Road Traffic (Driver Licensing and Information Systems) Act 1989 specified in the Schedule to the Order. The bringing into force of section 6 of the Act has the effect of requiring motor cyclists who have yet to pass a test of competence to drive a motor cycle to undergo compulsory training unless exempted by Regulations from having to do so. The Regulations implementing section 6 are the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) (No 5) Regulations 1990 (S.I. 1990/2334). Many of the other provisions brought into force by this Order concern compulsory training for motor cyclists.

items.title: The General Drainage Charges (Anglian Region) Order 1990
items.description: This Order concerns general drainage charges which may be raised by the National Rivers Authority in the local flood defence districts within that Authority’s Anglian Region for any time after 31st March 1990.
