It specifies in relation to each local flood defence district a factor to be applied in the formula, prescribed in section 49(1) of the Land Drainage Act 1976, read with the Drainage Charges Regulations 1990, for ascertaining the amount of the general drainage charge raised by the Authority for each such district.

items.title: The National Health Service (Local Health Councils) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations revoke the National Health Service (Local Health Councils) (Scotland) Regulations 1974 and substitute therefor provisions made in the light of amendments to section 7 of the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978 effected by the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 (“the 1990 Act”). The Regulations come into force immediately with respect to the submission of schemes for the new health councils to be established under the amended provisions of the section and for the establishment of those new councils under such schemes.

items.title: The Income Tax (Building Societies) (Dividends and Interest) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, made under section 477A(1) of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (“the 1988 Act”) and paragraph 16 of Schedule 5 to the Finance Act 1990, prescribe the cases in which a building society is required to deduct income tax from any dividend or interest paid or credited in any year of assessment in respect of shares in, or deposits with or loans to, the society, and to account for and pay income tax deducted to the Board.

items.title: The Income Tax (Deposit-takers) (Interest Payments)Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, made under sections 480B and 482(11) of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (“the 1988 Act”), prescribe the conditions which, if fulfilled, enable payments of interest by a deposit-taker to be made without deduction of tax, thereby disapplying section 480A of the 1988 Act (“section 480A”). They also provide for recovery of tax paid in the tax year in which the conditions are fulfilled, and for the furnishing of information to, and inspection of documents by, the Board in connection with payments of interest by deposit-takers under deduction of tax pursuant to section 480A or, in accordance with these Regulations, without deduction of tax.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Isles of Scilly) Order 1990
items.description: This Order applies to the Isles of Scilly, as if they were a separate county, the provisions of the Town and Country Planning Act 1990 and of the Planning (Consequential Provisions) Act 1990 specified or referred to in Part I of the Schedule, subject to the modifications specified in Part II.

items.title: The Financial Services Act 1986 (Electricity Industry Exemptions) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: Article 2 of the Order exempts the persons mentioned in it from the requirement to obtain authorisation under the Financial Services Act 1986 in order to carry on the business of engaging in the activities mentioned in the article.

items.title: The Land Registration (Solicitor to H M Land Registry) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations specify the acts of the Chief Land Registrar which may be done by a legally qualified registrar, to be called the Solicitor to H M Land Registry. They further provide for those acts to be done by another legally qualified registrar when there is a vacancy in the office of, or in the absence of, the Solicitor to H M Land Registry.

items.title: The Planning (Listed Buildings and Conservation Areas) (Isles of Scilly) Order 1990
items.description: This Order applies to the Isles of Scilly all the provisions specified in section 92(2) and (3) of the Planning (Listed Buildings and Conservation Areas) Act 1990 subject to the modifications specified in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Applications under the Social Security Act 1986) 1990
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes provision for rules of procedure in the Sheriff Court to regulate the manner in which proceedings for recovery of benefits are pursued against liable relatives under section 24 of the Social Security Act 1986; and for a consequential amendment of the Ordinary Cause Rules of the Sheriff Court.

items.title: The Electricity Act 1989 (Nominated Companies) (England and Wales) Order 1990
items.description: Sections 65(1) and 66(5) of the Electricity Act 1989 provide that, on the transfer date (being a day appointed by the Secretary of State by order under section 65(1) of the 1989 Act), all the property, rights and liabilities of the Electricity Boards in England and Wales and the Electricity Council (subject in the case of the Electricity Council to the exceptions specified in section 66(2) and (6) of the 1989 Act) shall become by virtue of the provision in question property, rights and liabilities of successor companies nominated for the purpose by the Secretary of State.

items.title: The Rules of the Air Regulations 1990
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Regulations)

items.title: The Housing (Grants for Fire Escapes in Houses in Multiple Occupation) (Prescribed Percentage) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: Section 249 of the Housing (Scotland) Act 1987 provides for the making of grants by local authorities for the provision of fire escapes in houses in multiple occupation. The amount of grant is limited to 75 per cent of the maximum amount of expenses incurred in carrying out the work. The maximum amount of expenses can be determined by the local authority up to certain limits.

items.title: The Environmental Protection Act 1990 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 13th November 1990 section 89(7), (8), (9), (11), (12) and (13) of the Environmental Protection Act 1990 which relate to the preparation, issuing, consideration and modification or withdrawal of a code of practice for the purpose of providing practical guidance in Great Britain on the duty to keep certain land clear of litter and refuse and to keep highways and roads clear of litter and refuse and clean.

items.title: Local Authority Social Services (Complaints Procedure) Order 1990
items.description: This Order requires local authorities in England and Wales to establish a procedure for considering representations (including complaints) by qualifying individuals as defined in section 7B(2) of the Local Authority Social Services Act 1970, or anyone acting on their behalf, in relation to the discharge of, or any failure to discharge, any of their social services functions in respect of such individuals.

items.title: The Electricity Act 1989 (Transfer Date) (England and Wales) Order 1990
items.description: By the Electricity Act 1989 (Nominated Companies) (England and Wales) Order 1990/S.I. 1990/224, the Secretary of State nominated the companies mentioned in the Schedules to that Order as successor companies to the Electricity Boards in England and Wales and the Electricity Council.

items.title: The Water Byelaws (Milngavie Waterworks, Loch Katrine, Loch Arklet, Glen Finglas) Extension Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Water Byelaws (Milngavie Waterworks, Loch Katrine, Loch Arklet, Glen Finglas) Extension Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Government Stock (Amendment)Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make three groups of amendments to the Government Stock Regulations 1965 (S.I. 1965/1420—“the principal Regulations”). First, they insert a new regulation 3B, which deals with the procedure to be followed by the Bank of England when redeeming stock. A notice is to be sent to the registered holder of stock which is to be redeemed, not later than eight weeks before the redemption date (regulation 3B(2) ). This notice must contain particulars of the redemption date and inform the holder that, unless a request to the contrary signed by the holder is received by the Bank by a date specified in the notice (which date cannot be more than four weeks before the redemption date) a warrant in respect of the redemption monies drawn in favour of the holder will be sent to him at the address recorded on the register (regulation 3B(3) ). Where any stock which is to be redeemed is registered in the names of more than one person as joint holders, the notice is to be addressed to all joint holders, and is to state that, unless a request to the contrary signed by each registered joint holder is received by the Bank, the warrant in respect of the redemption monies will be drawn in favour of, and sent to, the first named holder. The right of the Bank of England to require the written consent of the stockholder in any case where it considers it appropriate is, however, preserved.

items.title: The Education (Schools and Further Education) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 10 of the Education (Schools and Further Education) Regulations 1981, which relates to the duration of the school year and day, in two ways.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Civilian Fine Enforcement Officers) (No. 2) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules prescribe classes of authorities the authorised employees of which, by virtue of sections 125(2) and 136(2) (a) of the Magistrates' Courts Act 1980, as amended by section 65 of the Criminal Justice Act 1988, are empowered to execute, in the area in which their employing authority exercises its functions, certain categories of warrants in connection with the enforcement of fines and other sums adjudged to be paid by a summary conviction and the manner of authorisation of civilian employees of such authorities.

items.title: The Social Security (Industrial Injuries)(Prescribed Diseases) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Industrial Injuries) (Prescribed Diseases) Regulations 1985. They provide where the degree of disablement due to industrial deafness is assessed at less than 20 per cent. that percentage shall be disregarded for the purposes of assessing disablement. These Regulations also ensure that “metal” for the purposes of sensorineural hearing loss does not include stone, concrete, aggregate or similar substances for use in road or railway construction.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Cable Hackney and Islington Limited) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Cable Hackney and Islington Limited on 29th October 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the Hackney and Islington area. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 20th November 1990. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Cable Hackney and Islington Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Southdown Cablevision Limited) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Southdown Cablevision Limited on 28th September 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the whole of the Districts of Brighton, Worthing and Adur, the whole of the Borough of Hove and a small part of the District of Mid Sussex. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 20th November 1990. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Southdown Cablevision Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Offshore Installations (Safety Zones) (No. 7) Order 1990
items.description: This Order establishes, under section 22 of the Petroleum Act 1987, a safety zone, having a radius of 500 metres from a specified point, around the installation specified in the Schedule to this Order and stationed in waters to which section 21(7) of that Act applies (these include territorial waters and waters in areas designated under section 1(7) of the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29)).

items.title: The Statutory Nuisance (Appeals) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision with respect to appeals to magistrates' courts against abatement notices served under section 80 of the Environmental Protection Act 1990. Regulation 2 sets out grounds on which such appeals may be made, describes the procedure to be followed in certain cases in which the appellant claims that a notice should have been served on some other person, and the action which the court may take to give effect to its decision on an appeal. Regulation 3 prescribes the cases in which an abatement notice is to be suspended pending the abandonment of, or a decision by a magistrates' court on, an appeal. Regulation 4 revokes certain provisions of the Control of Noise (Appeals) Regulations 1975.

items.title: The Occupational and Personal Pension Schemes (Levy) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision for a levy towards meeting the cost of the Pensions Ombudsman, the register of occupational and personal pension schemes and certain grants made to advisory bodies by the Occupational Pensions Boards.

items.title: The Register of Occupational and Personal Pension Schemes Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations provide for the establishment of a register of occupational and personal pension schemes and for the appointment of the Occupational Pensions Board to be the registrar of such schemes. Regulations 2, 3 and 6, and regulation 1 in so far as it applies to those regulations, extend to Northern Ireland. Provision for Northern Ireland corresponding to other provisions of these Regulations will be contained in separate regulations to be made by the Department of Health and Social Services for Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Education (Grant-maintained Schools) (Finance) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations revoke regulation 1 of the Education (Grant-maintained Schools) (Finance) Regulations 1990 by virtue of which those Regulations apply only to England. Thus, with effect from the coming into force of these Regulations, the earlier Regulations will extend to both England and Wales.

items.title: The Cambridgeshire, Norfolk and Suffolk (County Boundaries) Order 1990
items.description: This Order transfers areas between the counties of Cambridgeshire, Norfolk and Suffolk. Consequential changes to districts, parishes, local government electoral areas and parish electoral arrangements are made.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities)(Competition) (Wales) (No. 2) Regulations 1990
items.description: Regulation 5 of the Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Competition) (Wales) Regulations 1988 (S.I. 1988/1468) provides that section 6 of the Local Government Act 1988 (restrictions on carrying out functional work) shall apply where certain authorities propose to carry out certain work after the dates specified in the Schedule to the Regulations.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Competition) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: Regulation 5 of the Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Competition) (Scotland) Regulations 1988 (S.I. 1988/1413) (“the principal Regulations”) provides that section 6 of the Local Government Act 1988 (“the 1988 Act”) (restrictions on carrying out functional work) applies where local authorities, placed in groups in Schedule 1 to the principal Regulations, propose to carry out functional work described in section 2(2) of the 1988 Act after the dates specified in Schedule 2 to the principal Regulations for the different groups.

items.title: The A47 Trunk Road (East Dereham to North Tuddenham Improvement) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A47 Trunk Road (East Dereham to North Tuddenham Improvement) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Child Abduction and Custody (Parties to Conventions) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Child Abduction and Custody (Parties to Conventions) Order 1986. It adds the Federal Republic of Germany to the list of the Contracting States to the Convention on the Civil Aspects of International Child Abduction signed at the Hague on 25th October 1980 (Cm.33) and to the European Convention on Recognition and Enforcement of Decisions Concerning Custody of Children and on the Restoration of Custody of Children, signed at Luxembourg on 20th May 1980 (Cm. 191).

items.title: The European Commission and Court of Human Rights (Immunities and Privileges) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the European Commission and Court of Human Rights (Immunities and Privileges) Order 1970 (S.I. 1970/1941) in order to confer exemption from income tax on the members of the European Commission of Human Rights and judges of the European Court of Human Rights. It will enable Her Majesty’s Government to give effect to the Fifth Protocol to the General Agreement on Privileges and Immunities of the Council of Europe (Cm. 1121).

items.title: The Protection of Trading Interests Act 1980 (Hong Kong) Order 1990
items.description: This Order extends the Protection of Trading Interests Act 1980 to Hong Kong with certain exceptions, adaptations and modifications; the text of the Act, as extended to Hong Kong, is set out in full in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The British Nationality (Hong Kong) (Selection Scheme) Order 1990
items.description: Section 1(1) of the British Nationality (Hong Kong) Act 1990 provides that the Secretary of State shall register as British citizens up to 50,000 persons recommended to him for that purpose by the Governor of Hong Kong under a scheme or schemes made and approved in accordance with Schedule 1 to that Act. Paragraph 1 of that Schedule provides that effect shall be given to any such scheme by an Order in Council setting out the terms of the scheme. Paragraph 2(a) of the Schedule provides that the scheme set out in the Order in Council shall, in the case of the first Order to be made, be a scheme submitted by the Governor with such modifications (if any) as the Secretary of State may think necessary or expedient.

items.title: The Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 (Isle of Man) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order extends to the Isle of Man, subject to the modification in article 2(3), certain amendments in the Patents Act 1949 (c. 87) and the Patents Act 1977 which are effected by Schedule 5 to the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 and extends there the repeal of certain provisions in the Patents Act 1977 effected by section 303(2) of and Schedule 8 to that Act of 1988. Other provisions of that Act of 1988 have already been extended to the Isle of Man by the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 (Isle of Man) (No. 2) Order 1989 (S.I. 1989/1292) and the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 (Isle of Man) Order 1990 (S.I. 1990/1505).

items.title: The Patents Act 1977 (Isle of Man) (Variation)Order 1990
items.description: This Order varies the Patents Act 1977 (Isle of Man) Order 1978 by further modifying the Patents Act 1977, as amended, in its extension to the Isle of Man.

items.title: The Prevention of Terrorism (Temporary Provisions) Act 1984 (Guernsey) (Revocation) Order 1990
items.description: This Order revokes the Prevention of Terrorism (Temporary Provisions) Act 1984 (Guernsey) Order 1984, which extended that Act to the Bailiwick of Guernsey with exceptions, adaptations and modifications. The Prevention of Terrorism (Temporary Provisions) Act 1989 which repealed and replaced the 1984 Act is not extended to the Bailiwick of Guernsey where a corresponding law (the Prevention of Terrorism (Bailiwick of Guernsey) Law 1990) applies.

items.title: The Judicial Committee (General Appellate Jurisdiction) Rules (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Judicial Committee (General Appellate Jurisdiction) Rules by substituting the “standard” and “indemnity” bases of taxation for the scale of costs previously prescribed.

items.title: The Parliamentary Constituencies (Scotland) (Miscellaneous Changes) Order 1990
items.description: This Order gives effect without modification to the recommendations contained in the report of the Boundary Commission for Scotland dated 5th July 1990. The report contains proposals for changes to the areas of certain parliamentary constituencies where local government area changes have resulted in inconsistencies between local government boundaries and the boundaries of parliamentary constituencies. Articles 2 to 8 of, and the Schedule to, this Order set out and describe the new constituencies, the boundaries of which will be consistent with the revised local government boundaries at 5th July 1990.

items.title: The A27 Trunk Road (Crossbush Bypass) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A27 Trunk Road (Crossbush Bypass) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Superannuation (Children’s Pensions) (Earnings Limit) Order 1990
items.description: Subject to certain conditions section 21 of the Judicial Pensions Act 1981 permits the payment of a children’s pension under that Act to continue after the child reaches the age of 16 if the child is undergoing training for a trade, profession or vocation. One of the conditions is that the emoluments received during training, excluding any emoluments receivable or payable by way of return of any premium paid in respect of the training, do not exceed £1299 a year. This Order revokes the Superannuation (Children’s Pensions) (Earnings Limit) Order 1989 and increases the limit on such emoluments from £1299 to £1398 a year.

items.title: The A27 Trunk Road (Crossbush Bypass Detrunking) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A27 Trunk Road (Crossbush Bypass Detrunking) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A27 Trunk Road (Crossbush Bypass Slip Roads) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A27 Trunk Road (Crossbush Bypass Slip Roads) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Social Fund Cold Weather Payments (General) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Fund Cold Weather Payments (General) Regulations 1988 by substituting a Schedule which revises in part the description of the areas covered by national climatological message stations and introduces new national climatological message stations to cover parts of the country.

items.title: The Crown Agents (Additional Powers) Order 1990
items.description: This Order enables Crown Agents to provide a range of services in their own right to the private sector which could previously only be provided to public bodies. In certain instances (which are the same as those specified in the Crown Agents Act 1979 in respect of a similar range of services to public bodies) the provision of these services is conditional upon the consent of the Secretary of State.

items.title: The Plaice (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Plaice (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Electricity Council and Electricity Boards Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the appointment and tenure of office of the members of the Electricity Council, the Central Electricity Generating Board and the Area Boards (which continue in existence by virtue of section 84(2) of the Electricity Act 1989 (c. 29) to carry out limited functions under that Act), the proceedings and quorum of these bodies, the execution of instruments by them and proof of documents executed by or on behalf of them.

items.title: The Occupational Pensions (Revaluation) Order 1990
items.description: This Order is made, as required by section 52A of the Social Security Pensions Act 1975, in the revaluation year beginning on 1st January 1990. The revaluation percentages specified are relevant to the revaluation of benefits under ocupational pension schemes, as required by section 52B of, and Schedule 1A to, that Act.

items.title: The Portsmouth, Severn Trent and Wessex (Pipelaying and Other Works) (Codes of Practice) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Portsmouth, Severn Trent and Wessex (Pipelaying and Other Works) (Codes of Practice) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Crown Office Fees Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Crown Office Fees Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Poultry (Seizure of Hatching Eggs) Order 1990
items.description: This Order empowers an inspector of the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food to seize and dispose of any hatching eggs (the eggs of domestic fowls, turkeys, geese or ducks intended for incubation) in order to prevent the spread of salmonella (article 3).

items.title: The Income Support (General and Transitional) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 by providing for a reduction in the applicable amount of a person in certain circumstances where he has been notified of a course but has failed to attend it (regulations 2 and 3).

items.title: The Control of Industrial Major Accident Hazards (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Control of Industrial Major Accident Hazards Regulations 1984 (“the principal Regulations”) for the purpose of implementing in full in respect of Great Britain Council Directive No. 88/610/EEC (OJ No L 336, 7.12.88, p.14) (“the Directive”) amending Council Directive No. 82/501/EEC (OJ No L230, 5.8.1982, p.1) on the major accident hazards of certain industrial activities which was implemented by the principal Regulations.

items.title: The Medicines (Fees Relating to Medicinal Products for Human Use) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Medicines (Fees Relating to Medicinal Products for Human Use) Regulations 1989 which prescribe fees in connection with applications and inspections relating to licences and certificates granted under the Medicines Act 1968 so far as they apply to medicinal products for human use only and insert a transitional provision in respect of applications made by specified health authorities (regulation 8).

items.title: The Law Reform (Miscellaneous Provisions) (Scotland) Act 1990 Commencement (No.1) Order 1990
items.description: This Order appoints 1st December 1990 as the date for the coming into force of the provisions of the Law Reform (Miscellaneous Provisions) (Scotland) Act 1990 (c. 40) (“the Act”) which are specified in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Transitional Period) (Amendment and Further Provision) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision, further to that made in the Non-Domestic Rating (Transitional Period) Regulations 1990 (“the principal Regulations”), in respect of the transition between the amounts of rates payable in respect of certain non-domestic property under the General Rate Act 1967 (c. 9), and under the Local Government Finance Act 1988.

items.title: The Commons Regulation (Therfield) Provisional Order Confirmation Act 1888 (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: The Conservators of Therfield Heath and Greens in Hertfordshire include three persons required by virtue of the provisional order confirmed by and contained in the Schedule to the Commons Regulation (Therfield) Provisional Order Confirmation Act 1888 to be elected by the inhabitant ratepayers of he special drainage district for Royston, which district was created by order under the Public Health Act 1875 (c. 55)

items.title: The Guarantee Payments (Exemption) (No. 28)Order 1990
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 27th December 1990, excludes from the operation of section 12 of the Employment Protection (Consolidation) Act 1978 employees to whom the collective agreement made between the parties described in Schedule 1 to the Order relates. The Order is necessary because with effect from 24th May 1990 Bridon Ropes Limited ceased to be a member of the Wire and Wire Rope Employers Association with the consequences set out at paragraph 8 of the collective agreement.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) (No. 5) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations insert a new Part—Part IIIA—into the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) Regulations 1987 (“the 1987 Regulations”). They also revoke and re-enact the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) (No. 4) Regulations 1990, which were intended to insert Part IIIA into the 1987 Regulations but which, on being found to contain three incorrect references to provisions, were revoked before they came into force. Part IIIA implements section 6 of the Road Traffic (Driver Licensing and Information Systems) Act 1989 which was brought into force on 1st December 1990 by the Road Traffic (Driver Licensing and Information Systems) Act 1989 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1990 (S.I. 1990/2228). In the light of the introduction of Part IIIA, these Regulations also make consequential amendments to the 1987 Regulations.

items.title: Care of Cathedrals Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules are made under section 16 of the Care of Cathedrals Measure 1990.

items.title: The Agricultural or Forestry Tractors and Tractor Components (Type Approval) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which apply throughout the United Kingdom, further amend the Agricultural or Forestry Tractors and Tractor Components (Type Approval) Regulations 1988. The Regulations implement Council Directives 89/680/EEC (OJ No. L398, 30.12.89, p.26), 89/681/EEC (OJ No. L398, 30.12.89, p.27) and 89/682/EEC (OJ No. L398, 30.12.89, p.29) which amend Directives relating to roll-over protection structures (regulation 3(b)(i), (iii) and (iv) and (c)(i), (iii) and (iv)).

items.title: The Conservation of Seals (Common Seals) (Shetland Islands Area) Order 1990
items.description: Section 2(1) of the Conservation of Seals Act 1970 (“the Act”) prescribes an annual close season for Common Seals extending from 1st June to 31st August both inclusive. The Conservation of Seals (Common Seals) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/2023) extended this close season throughout the whole year by prohibiting the killing, injuring and taking of seals in Great Britain and adjacent waters for a period of 2 years from 19th December 1988. That Order ceases to have effect on 18th December 1990.

items.title: The Conservation of Seals (England) Order 1990
items.description: This Order prohibits for three years with effect from 19th December 1990 the killing, injuring or taking of grey seals and common seals in counties of England bordering the North Sea and adjacent territorial waters. The Conservation of Seals (England and Wales) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/2024)and the Conservation of Seals (Common Seals) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/2023) cease to have effect on that date.

items.title: The Protection of Wrecks (Designation No. 1) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates as a restricted area for the purposes of the Protection of Wrecks Act 1973 an area round the site of what is thought to be the wreck of a vessel which is of historical and archaeological importance lying off Lundy Island, North Devon. No part of the site is above the high water mark of ordinary spring tide.

items.title: The M20 Motorway (A249 Sittingbourne Road) Connecting Roads Scheme 1990
items.description: This version of the The M20 Motorway (A249 Sittingbourne Road) Connecting Roads Scheme 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The M20 Motorway (Chrismill Bridge Section)Scheme 1990
items.description: This version of the The M20 Motorway (Chrismill Bridge Section)Scheme 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The M20 Motorway (Coldharbour Lane to Sandling Section) Connecting Roads Scheme 1990
items.description: This version of the The M20 Motorway (Coldharbour Lane to Sandling Section) Connecting Roads Scheme 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The M20 Motorway (Medway Bridge Section)Scheme 1990
items.description: This version of the The M20 Motorway (Medway Bridge Section)Scheme 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Oil Pollution (Compulsory Insurance) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Oil Pollution (Compulsory Insurance) Regulations 1981 by increasing the fee to be paid on application for a certificate of compulsory insurance from £25 to £33.

items.title: The A16 Trunk Road (Ludborough Bypass) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A16 Trunk Road (Ludborough Bypass) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Broadcasting Act 1990 (Commencement No. 1 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st December 1990 the provisions of the Broadcasting Act 1990 which are specified in Schedule 1 to this Order. The provisions specified in Schedules 2 and 3 come into force on 1st January 1991 and 1st January 1993 respectively, subject to the transitional provisions in articles 3 and 7. The provisions specified in articles 4, 5 and 6 come into force on 1st February 1991, 1st March 1991 and 1st April 1991 respectively, subject to the transitional provisions in those articles.

items.title: The Electricity (Restrictive Trade Practices Act 1976) (Exemptions) (No. 4) Order 1990
items.description: This Order specifies agreements and a description of agreements relating to the generation, transmission or supply of electricity to which the Restrictive Trade Practices Act 1976 (“the 1976 Act”) is deemed not to apply and never to have applied. By virtue of section 100(1) of the Electricity Act 1989 electricity is treated as goods for the purposes of the 1976 Act. This Order provides that the agreements specified in Part I of the Schedule to the Order, together with agreements meeting the description specified in Part II of the Schedule and satisfying the condition specified therein, will nevertheless fall outside the provisions of the 1976 Act.

items.title: The Atomic Energy (Mutual Assistance Convention) Order 1990
items.description: This Order certifies that a declaration has been made by Her Majesty’s Government under paragraph 9 of Article 8 of the Convention on Assistance in the Case of a Nuclear Accident or Radiological Emergency 1986. Section 5(3) of the Atomic Energy Act 1989 provides that at any time when a declaratio under paragraph 9 is in force the provisions set out in the Schedule to that Act (which reproduce paragraphs 2 and 3 of Article 8 of the Countries relating to privileges and immunities) shall only have the force of law in the United Kingdom to the extent indicated in the declaration.

items.title: The Peak Rail Light Railway Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Peak Rail Light Railway Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Education (School Premises) (Amendment)Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (School Premises) Regulations 1981 so as to extend the effect of the transitory provisions in Schedule 1 thereto until 1st September 1996.

items.title: The International Monetary Fund (Increase in Subscription) Order 1990
items.description: This Order authorises an increase in the amount of the United Kingdom’s subscription to the International Monetary Fund in accordance with a proposal for an increase in the United Kingdom’s quota under section 3(a) of Article III of the Fund’s Articles of Agreement. (The subscription of each member of the Fund is equal to its quota).

items.title: The Aberdeen Harbour Revision Order 1990
items.description: Paragraphs (a) to (f) of section 3(3) of the Aberdeen Harbour Order 1987 require the Secretary of State to consult with certain named organisations before making appointments to the Aberdeen Harbour Board. Due to the fact that two of those organisations have ceased to exist after the making of that Order, those provisions are amended to enable the Secretary of State to make appointments under any paragraph where an organisation has ceased to exist after consultation if appropriate with such other persons appearing to him to be representative of the interests the paragraph in question relates to.

items.title: The European Communities (Definition of Treaties) (European School) Order 1990 (revoked)
items.description: This Order declares the Agreement between the United Kingdom Government and the Board of Governors of the European School concerning the European School at Culham to be a Community Treaty as defined in section 1(2) of the European Communities Act 1972. The Agreement sets out the privileges of the European School established at Culham. The principal effect of declaring the Agreement to be a Community Treaty as so defined is to bring into play, in relation to it, the provisions of section 2 of the European Communities Act 1972 which provides for the implementation of Treaties so specified.

items.title: The Public Lending Right Scheme 1982 (Commencement of Variations) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 27th December 1990 variations to the Public Lending Right Scheme 1982.

items.title: The Tax-exempt Special Savings Account Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 19th December 1990, are made under section 326C(1) of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988. They make provision for tax-exempt special savings accounts (“accounts”) in addition to that provided in sections 326A and 326B of that Act. They prescribe an additional condition which must be satisfied by accounts and provide for building societies and institutions authorised under the Banking Act 1987 to notify the Board that they intend to operate accounts, specify the conditions for applications by individuals for such accounts to be opened, provide for societies and institutions to deliver returns in connection with such accounts, for the transfer of such accounts and for societies and institutions to be prohibited from operating new accounts in certain circumstances, and make provision for the circumstances arising when an institution ceases, or ceases to be entitled, to operate accounts.

items.title: The Building Societies (Aggregation) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules provide for the aggregation of assets and liabilities of subsidiaries and other associated bodies of a building society with those of the society for the purposes of the limits in sections 7, 8 and 20 of the Building Societies Act 1986.

items.title: The Building Societies (Non-Retail Funds and Deposits) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends section 7 of the Building Societies Act 1986, which empowers a building society to raise funds and borrow money and places a limit on non-retail funds and borrowing. This Order removes with effect from 31st December 1991 certain time deposits from the category of non-retail funds. It creates with effect from 31st December 1990 a new category of retail funds, that is to say shares held and sums deposited under savings schemes by intermediaries which are authorised banking institutions or friendly societies.

items.title: The Building Societies (Accounts and Related Provisions) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations remove references to Time Deposits in the Building Societies (Accounts and Related Provisions) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/2072); building societies will cease to mention such deposits in their annual accounts compiled after the coming into operation of the Regulations.

items.title: The Goods Vehicles (Authorisation of International Journeys) (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Goods Vehicles (Authorisation of International Journeys) (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The New Towns (Transfer of Housing Stock) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the New Towns (Transfer of Housing Stock) Regulations 1990 (which make provision requiring the transfer of new town housing stock) and provide that those Regulations shall not prevent a new town corporation from making a disposal under the New Towns Act 1981 or affect their duty, in the period before disposing of a dwelling under the Regulations, to act in accordance with Part V of the Housing Act 1985 with respect to the dwelling. Where a corporation transfer an estate or interest in housing stock under those Acts, the Regulations cease to apply for the disposal of that estate or interest.

items.title: The European Communities (Privileges of the European School) Order 1990
items.description: This Order confers privileges on the European School at Culham, and on its staff members. These privileges are conferred in accordance with the Agreement between the Government of the United Kingdom and the Board of Governors of the European School concerning the European School at Culham (Cm. 596).

items.title: The Local Statutory Provisions (Postponement of Repeal) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: Section 225(6) of the Local Government (Scotland) Act 1973 as amended provides that all local statutory provisions to which the subsection applies shall cease to have effect at the end of 1984. Previous Orders (S.I. 1984/1926, 1985/1629, 1986/2034, 1987/2090,1988/2106 and 1989/2223) have postponed the date on which such local statutory provisions applying to the local government areas or parts thereof listed in the Schedules to those Orders were to cease to have effect. The date was last postponed to the end of 1990. This Order further postpones that date until the end of 1991 in respect of those local government areas listed in the Schedule to this Order.

items.title: The Channel Tunnel (Amendment of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food Import Legislation) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends agriculture, fisheries and food import legislation mentioned in Schedules 1 and 2 in their application to the tunnel system.

items.title: The Food Safety Act 1990 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st December 1990 the following provisions of the Food Safety Act 1990: sections 1 to 6, 16 to 19, 25, 26, 27(2) and (5), 28(2), 30(9), 31, 40, 45, 48, 49(2), 53, 57(1) and 59(2) and (3) and Schedules 1 and 4.

items.title: The Army Terms of Service (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend one of the provisions determining the “notice period” for the purposes of regulation 5 of the Army Terms of Service Regulations 1986.

items.title: The Royal Air Force Terms of Service (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Royal Air Force Terms of Service Regulations 1985 by (i) reducing the minimum age for men enlisted on a notice engagement from 17 years 6 months to 16 years; (ii) bringing the right to transfer to the reserve given to women into line with that given to men; and (iii) preserving that right notwithstanding the extension of terms of air force service under regulation 14(1).

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Harrogate Telecommunications Complex Limited) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Harrogate Telecommunications Complex Limited on 29th October 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in part of the Borough of Harrogate. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 4th December 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Harrogate Telecommunications Complex Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (York Telecommunications Complex Limited) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to York Telecommunications Complex Limited on 29th October 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the whole of the City of York and adjacent parts of the Districts of Selby and Ryedale. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 4th December 1990. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, York Telecommunications Complex Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Electrical Equipment for Explosive Atmospheres (Certification) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 2(1) of the Electrical Equipment for Explosive Atmospheres (Certification) Regulations 1990 (the principal regulations) to give effect with respect to Great Britain to the provisions of Council Directive 90/487/EEC (OJ No. L270, 2.10.90, p. 23) concerning electrical equipment for use in potentially explosive atmospheres employing certain types of protection . The principal regulations enable a manufacturer to apply to an appropriate certification body appointed under those regulations for a certificate attesting that the electrical equipment, the subject of the application, conforms to the harmonised standards as defined in the regulations or offers a degree of safety at least equivalent to those standards.

items.title: The Employment Act 1990 (Commencement and Transitional Provisions) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings the provisions of the Employment Act 1990 concerned with the merger of the Redundancy Fund and the National Insurance Fund into force on 1st February 1991 and all other provisions of the Act into force on 1st January 1991 (other than those brought into force on Royal Assent).

items.title: The Funds for Trade Union Ballots (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Funds for Trade Union Ballots Regulations 1984 (as amended), which set out the terms of a scheme for payment of certain expenditure incurred by independent trade unions in holding secret postal ballots for specified purposes.

items.title: The Security Service Act 1989 (Overseas Territories) Order 1990
items.description: This Order directs that section 3(1) (warrants) of the Security Service Act 1989 shall extend to the colonies specified in the Schedule hereto.

items.title: The Local Government (Compensation for Redundancy and Premature Retirement) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make amendments, consequential to the Education Reform Act 1988, to the Local Government (Compensation for Premature Retirement) Regulations 1982 (“the 1982 Regulations”) and the Local Government (Compensation for Redundancy and Premature Retirement) Regulations 1984 (“the 1984 Regulations”).

items.title: The A16 Trunk Road (Stickford Bypass) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A16 Trunk Road (Stickford Bypass) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A16 Trunk Road (Stickford Bypass) (Detrunking) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A16 Trunk Road (Stickford Bypass) (Detrunking) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Patents Rules 1990
items.description: This version of the The Patents Rules 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) (No. 6) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) Regulations 1990 and the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) Regulations 1987.

items.title: The Central Institutions (Recognition) (Scotland) (No.2) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations recognise certain educational establishments for the provision of further education as central institutions and revoke the Central Institutions (Recognition) (Scotland) Regulations 1990. These regulations are made to take account of the changes of names by Glasgow College of Technology, Glasgow to Glasgow Polytechnic, Glasgow and Robert Gordon’s Institute of Technology, Aberdeen to The Robert Gordon Institute of Technology, Aberdeen.

items.title: The Glasgow College of Technology and Robert Gordon’s Institute of Technology (Change of Name) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations re-name Glasgow College of Technology as “Glasgow Polytechnic” and Robert Gordon’s Institute of Technology as “The Robert Gordon Institute of Technology” and make consequential amendments to relevant Regulations.

items.title: The Broadcasting (Number of Houses in an Area for Purposes of Diffusion Service Licence) Order 1990
items.description: Paragraph 7(1) of Part II of Schedule 12 (transitional provisions relating to existing cable services) to the Broadcasting Act 1990 disapplies certain paragraphs of that Part in respect of a licence to provide a diffusion service in an area in which there are not more dwelling-houses than the number prescribed by the Secretary of State by order. This Order exercises that power and the number 1,000 is prescribed. Paragraph 7(1) also provides that such a licence shall cease to have effect.

items.title: The Broadcasting (Local Delivery Services) Order 1990
items.description: This Order classifies services provided under section 72(1) of the Broadcasting Act 1990 which are to be defined as local delivery services for the purposes of Part II of that Act.

items.title: The United Kingdom Forces (Jurisdiction of Colonial Courts) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order adds the Virgin Islands to the list of overseas territories to which the United Kingdom Forces (Jurisdiction of Colonial Courts) Order 1965 (S.I. 1965/1203) (which provides for withdrawing the jurisdiction of the civil courts of certain overseas territories to try members of Her Majesty’s forces or of a civilian component of those forces for certain offences) extends.

items.title: The Housing (Right to Buy) (Priority of Charges) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: Under the Housing Act 1985 (“the 1985 Act”) liability to repay discount following the exercise of the right to buy or the right to be granted a shared ownership lease is secured by a charge on the dwelling-house. Such a charge does not have priority over certain charges securing advances by bodies which are approved lending institutions for the purposes of section 156 of the Act.

items.title: The Mortgage Indemnities (Recognised Bodies)(No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order specifies one additional body as a recognised body for the purposes of sections 442 and 443 of the Housing Act 1985. (Other bodies have been specified by previous Orders).

items.title: The Cream (Heat Treatment) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Cream (Heat Treatment) (Scotland) Regulations 1983 by restricting the prohibition on the sale of cream unless it satisfies the heat treatment requirements specified in the principal Regulations to retail sales only.

items.title: The Cod (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1990
items.description: This Order prohibits, with an exception, fishing for cod (Gadus morhua) during the period beginning with the date of coming into force of the Order and ending immediately before 1st January 1991–

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Cable Communications (Wigan) Limited) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Cable Communications (Wigan) Limited on 14th November 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the Wigan area. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 10th December 1990. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Cable Communications (Wigan) Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Plant Health (Forestry) (Great Britain) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, which applies to Great Britain, further amends the Plant Health (Forestry) (Great Britain) Order 1989 (“the principal Order”) which implements Council Directive 77/93/EEC (OJ No. L26, 31.1.77, p. 20), as amended by the instruments listed in Schedule 6 of the principal Order.

items.title: The Accommodation of Children (Charge and Control) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Accommodation of Children (Charge and Control) Regulations 1988 by making provision for the immediate placement under the 1988 Regulations of a child where a local authority consider this to be necessary. The requirements specified in the new provision are to be satisfied, pending compliance with the requirements which apply to placements under the 1988 Regulations other than immediate placements as soon as is practicable after any immediate placement has been made.

items.title: The United Kingdom Forces (Jurisdiction of Colonial Courts) (Amendment No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order adds Anguilla to the list of overseas territories to which the United Kingdom Forces (Jurisdiction of Colonial Courts) Order 1965 (S.I. 1965/1203) (which provides for withdrawing the jurisdiction of the civil courts of certain overseas territories to try members of Her Majesty’s forces or of a civilian component of those forces for certain offences) extends.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (United Artists Communications (North Thames Estuary) Limited) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to United Artists Communications (North Thames Estuary) Limited on 14th November 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the Thames Estuary North area. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 10th December 1990. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, United Artists Communications (North Thames Estuary) Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Anglian Harbours National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order establishes Anglian Harbours National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. It also provides for the functions of the trust both before (article 6) and after (article 3) its operational date (the date on which it assumes all its functions). It specifies the operational date and the accounting date of the trust (article 5) and makes provision for assistance to the trust by health authorities before its operational date (article 7).

items.title: The Visiting Forces (Designation) (Colonies) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: Section 1(1) of the Visiting Forces Act 1952 provides that references in the Act to a country to which a provision of the Act applies are references to Commonwealth countries and to any country designated for the purposes of that provision by Order in Council. The Visiting Forces (Designation) Order 1954 (S.I. 1954/634) designates certain countries including the United States of America and the provisions of that Order are, subject to certain modifications, extended by the Visiting Forces (Designation) (Colonies) Order 1954 to certain colonial territories in so far as those provisions relate to the United States of America. This Order extends the provisions of the last mentioned Order, as amended, to Anguilla, the Cayman Islands, Montserrat and the Virgin Islands.

items.title: The Visiting Forces Act (Application to Colonies) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order extends to Anguilla, the Cayman Islands, Montserrat and the Virgin Islands the provisions of the Visiting Forces Act 1952, subject to the adaptations, modifications and exceptions set out in the Second Schedule to the Visiting Forces Act (Application to Colonies) Order 1954 (S.I. 1954/636) as modified by the present Order. items.title: The Census Order 1990
items.description: This Order provides for the taking of a census for Great Britain on 21st April 1991. The Order specifies the persons by whom and with respect to whom the census returns are to be made and sets out the particulars to be stated in the returns.

items.description: This Order establishes the Royal Free Hampstead National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. It specifies the operational date and the accounting date of the trust (article 5) and makes provision for assistance to the trust by health authorities before its operational date (article 7).

items.title: The Health Service Commissioner for England (Special Hospitals Service Authority) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates the Special Hospitals Service Authority as a “relevant body” within the meaning of section 109 of the National Health Service Act 1977 so that the Health Service Commissioner for England may investigate complaints which relate to the Authority or its officers.

items.title: The Wirral Hospital National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order establishes the Wirral Hospital National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. It also provides for the functions of the trust both before (article 6) and after (article 3) its operational date (the date on which it assumes all its functions). It specifies the operational date and the accounting date of the trust (article 5) and makes provision for assistance to the trust by health authorities before its operational date (article 7).

items.title: The Smoke Control Areas (Exempted Fireplaces) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: Section 11 of the Clean Air Act 1956 empowers local authorities to declare the whole or any part of their district to be a smoke control area in which the emission of smoke is generally prohibited. Specified classes of fireplace may be exempted from control by order.

items.title: The Liquor Licensing (Fees) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the fees payable from 1st January 1991 by applicants to a licensing board under the Licensing (Scotland) Act 1976. The Order increases the existing fees where still applicable by approximately 20%, and introduces new fees for the issue of duplicate licences and to take account of the alterations to the licensing system including the arrangements for transfer of licences, Sunday openings and the introduction of a children’s certificate effected by the Law Reform (Miscellaneous Provisions) (Scotland) Act 1990.

items.title: The Animals (Scientific Procedures) Act (Fees) Order 1990 (revoked)
items.description: This Order, which does not extend to Northern Ireland, prescribes the fees payable by the holders of certificates under section 6 of the Animals (Scientific Procedures) Act 1986 (scientific procedure establishments) and under section 7 of the Act (breeding and supplying establishments).

items.title: The Insurance Brokers Registration Council (Indemnity Insurance and Grants Scheme) (Amendment) Rules Approval Order 1990
items.description: This Order approves Rules made by the Insurance Brokers Registration Council under section 12 of the Insurance Brokers (Registration) Act 1977 and paragraph 6(1), (2) and (3) of the Financial Services Act 1986. They amend the Rules approved by the Insurance Brokers Registration Council (Indemnity Insurance and Grants Scheme) Rules Approval Order 1987 (S.I. 1987/1496) by extending the scope of the professional indemnity insurance requirements and the Grants Scheme to all categories of investment business which practising registered brokers and enrolled bodies corporate are permitted to undertake.

items.title: The Food Safety (Enforcement Authority) (England and Wales) Order 1990
items.description: This Order assigns responsibility for the enforcement of certain provisions of the Food Safety Act 1990 as respects non-metropolitan districts of England and Wales and the Temples. The provisions of that Act are enforced in England and Wales jointly by food authorities (as defined in section 5(1) of the Act) except where an Order provides that they shall be enforced solely by one authority.

items.title: The Food Safety (Sampling and Qualifications) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations specify the qualifications necessary to be a public analyst, food analyst or food examiner for the purposes of the Food Safety Act 1990. They prohibit specified persons from carrying out analyses or examinations.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Specified Periods) (Inner London) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations specify, for the purposes of Part I of the Local Government Act 1988, the minimum and maximum periods for which inner London borough councils and the Common Council of the City of London may invite offers to carry out certain work in connection with property transferred from the former Inner London Education Authority.

items.title: The Petroleum Revenue Tax (Nomination Scheme for Disposals and Appropriations) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 28th December 1990, amend the Petroleum Revenue Tax (Nomination Scheme for Disposals and Appropriations) Regulations 1987 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Hampshire (Minley Interchange) (M3) Motorway Scheme 1989 Confirmation Instrument 1990
The Enrolment of Deeds (Change of Name) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Enrolment of Deeds (Change of Name) Regulations 1983. They provide that the power to require additional evidence to support an application for enrolment of a deed poll evidencing the change of name of a minor may be exercised by any judge of the Court of Appeal or of the High Court as well as by the Master of the Rolls.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating Contributions (England) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: Under Part II of Schedule 8 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988, charging authorities are required to pay amounts (called non-domestic rating contributions) to the Secretary of State. Provisional amounts are paid during the financial year, final calculations and payments being made after the year ends. These Regulations amend the rules for the calculation of payments contained in the Non-Domestic Rating Contributions (England) Regulations 1989 (“the 1989 Regulations”).

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications and Deemed Applications) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications and Deemed Applications) Regulations 1989.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications and Deemed Applications) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications and Deemed Applications) (Scotland) Regulations 1990 (“the principal Regulations”), which make provision for the payment of fees to planning authorities in respect of applications made under Part III of the Town and Country Planning (Scotland) Act 1972 for planning permission for development or for approval of matters reserved by an outline planning permission and in respect of applications for consent for the display of advertisements; and for the payment of fees to the Secretary of State in respect of applications for planning permission which are deemed to have been made, by virtue of the provisions of section 85(7) or 91(5) of the 1972 Act, in connection with an appeal against an enforcement notice or in connection with an application for an established use certificate respectively.

items.title: The Community Charges (Administration and Enforcement) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 59(5)and consolidate, incorporating further amendments, regulation 62 of the Community Charges (Administration and Enforcement) Regulations 1989. They have effect in relation to financial years beginning on or after 1st April 1991.

items.title: The Parish and Community Councils (Committees)Regulations 1990
items.description: Section 13(1) of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 prevents members of local authority committees and sub-committees having voting rights if they are not members of the parent authority. These Regulations provide for exceptions to that rule in relation to parish and community councils. The rule is not to apply to a committee established exclusively to discharge one of the functions described (regulation 3). Neither does it apply to members of a sub-committee established exclusively to discharge such a function, and in addition a member of such a sub-committee may have a vote without being a member of the committee which appointed the sub-committee (regulation 4).

items.title: The Local Elections (Parishes and Communities)(Declaration of Acceptance of Office) Order 1990
items.description: This Order prescribes the form of declaration of acceptance of office to be made by the chairman or a councillor of a parish or community council. The person making the declaration is required to undertake to be guided by the National Code of Local Government Conduct. (The Code was issued as DoE Circular 8/90 and Welsh Office Circular 23/90, and is available from HMSO (ISBN 0 11 752289 9).)

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Fenland Cablevision Limited) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Fenland Cablevision Limited on 29th October 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunica-tion systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in all but the northernmost tip of the District of Fenland. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 12th December 1990. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunica-tion systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Fenland Cablevision Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (South Yorkshire Cablevision Limited) (the Boroughs of Doncaster and Rotherham) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to South Yorkshire Cablevision Limited on 1st November 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the whole of the Boroughs of Doncaster and Rotherham. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 12th December 1990. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, South Yorkshire Cablevision Limited will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its Doncaster and Rotherham public telecommunication systems when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Outer Space Act 1986 (Guernsey) Order 1990
items.description: This Order extends the Outer Space Act 1986 to bodies incorporated under the law of the Bailiwick of Guernsey and applies that Act to the Bailiwick subject to the exceptions and modifications specified in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Local Government Superannuation (Investments) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation P3 of the Local Government Superannuation Regulations 1986, which regulates the manner in which superannuation funds may be invested.

items.title: The Sole (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Sole (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Civil Aviation (Route Charges for Navigation Services) (Second Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Regulations)

items.title: The Statutory Nuisance (Appeals) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations correct a defect in the Statutory Nuisance (Appeals) Regulations 1990.

items.title: The Courts and Legal Services Act 1990 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st January 1991 a number of provisions in the Courts and Legal Services Act 1990. These provisions relate to the allocation of family proceedings in county courts (section 9); the Legal Services Ombudsman (sections 21 to 26 and Schedule 3); rights of audience (section 27); rights to conduct litigation (section 28); rights of audience for barristers and solicitors (sections 31 and (2) and 32 (1) and (2)); solicitors' rights to conduct litigation (section 33(1) and (2)); rights of Scottish and Northern Ireland lawyers to practise in England and Wales (section 60); the right of barristers to contract for their services (section 61); the immunity of advocates from actions in negligence and contract (section 62); multi-disciplinary and multi-national practices (section 66); solicitors' rights of audience in certain Crown Court centres (section 67); the preparation of documents by registered patent agents and trade mark agents (section 68); offences (section 70); qualifications for judicial and other appointments (section 71 and Schedule 10); district judges (section 74); restrictions on judges and others from providing certain legal services (section 75 and Schedule 11); judicial oaths (section 76); retirement age of certain Supreme Court officers (section 77); the merger of the offices of Registrar of Criminal Appeals and Queen’s Coroner and Attorney and Master of the Crown Office (section 78); provisions relating to judicial pensions (sections 83 and 84); an amendment to the Child Care Act 1980 (section 116(1) and schedule 16, paragraph 8); functions of the Treasury (section 118); and minor and consequential amendments, transitionals and savings and repeals.

items.title: The Legal Services Ombudsman (Jurisdiction) Order 1990
items.description: This Order is made under two provisions in section 22 of the Courts and Legal Services Act 1990 that relate to the Legal Services Ombudsman’s jurisdiction to investigate allegations about the manner in which a complaint made to a professional body has been dealt with by that body. Article 2 and Part I of the Schedule provide for the professional bodies over which the Ombudsman has jurisdiction. Article 3 and Part II of the Schedule secure that any issue which is being or has been determined by the Discipline and Appeals Committee of the Council for Licensed Conveyancers shall not be investigable by the Ombudsman. Section 22 of the 1990 Act (which establishes the Ombudsman) will come into force on 1st January 1991 as will section 21 (under which the Ombudsman is appointed) and related provisions.

items.title: The Food Safety Act 1990 (Consequential Modifications) (England and Wales) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends various Orders and Regulations applying to England and Wales which refer to the Food and Drugs Act 1955 and the Food Act 1984. The 1955 Act was consolidated by the 1984 Act, which has now been largely repealed by the Food Safety Act 1990.

items.title: The Food Safety Act 1990 (Consequential Modifications) (No 2) (Great Britain) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends various Orders and Regulations applying to Great Britain which refer to the Food and Drugs Act 1955, the Food and Drugs (Scotland) Act 1956 and the Food Act 1984. The 1955 Act was consolidated by the 1984 Act, and the 1956 and 1984 Acts have been largely repealed by the Food Safety Act 1990.

items.title: The Food Labelling (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to England and Wales only, further amend the Food Labelling Regulations 1984 (“the principal Regulations”). They implement provisions of Council Directive No. 79/112/EEC (OJ No. L33, 8.2.79, p.1) on the approximation of the laws of the Member States relating to the labelling, presentation and advertising of foodstuffs as added or varied by Council Directive No. 89/395/EEC (OJ No. L186, 30.6.89, p. 17).

items.title: The Food Labelling (Amendment) (Irradiated Food) Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Food Labelling (Amendment) (Irradiated Food) Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Security Service Act 1989 (Channel Islands) Order 1990
items.description: This Order extends to the Channel Islands, with modifications, the provisions of sections 3(1) (warrants) and 7(1) (short title) of the Security Service Act 1989.

items.title: The Food (Control of Irradiation) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Great Britain, revoke previous regulations which prohibited the sale of irradiated food (regulation 11), and permit the sale of specified types of irradiated food (regulation 6 as read with regulation 2, 3, 4 and Schedules 1 and 2) where the irradiation has taken place under licence.

items.title: The Milk & Dairies (Semi-skimmed and Skimmed Milk) (Heat Treatment and Labelling) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to England and Wales only, further amend the Milk and Dairies (Semi-skimmed and Skimmed Milk) (Heat Treatment and Labelling) Regulations 1988 by deleting provisions permitting a “sell by” date and introducing provisions requiring a “use by” date in labelling conditions for semi-skimmed or skimmed milk which is microbiologically highly perishable. New offences are introduced for selling semi-skimmed or skimmed milk bearing an expired “use by” date and for anyone other than the person originally responsible for applying the date mark to change it.

items.title: The Milk (Special Designation) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to England and Wales only, further amend the Milk (Special Designation) Regulations 1989 by deleting provisions permitting a “sell by” date and introducing provisions requiring a “use by” date in labelling conditions for milk which is microbiologically highly perishable. New offences are introduced for selling milk bearing an expired “use by” date and for anyone other than the person originally responsible for applying the date mark to change it.

items.title: The Fresh Meat Export (Hygiene and Inspection) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Fresh Meat (Hygiene and Inspection) Regulations 1987, thereby implementing in part the provisions of Council Directive 88/288/EEC No. L124, 8.5.88, p.28) further amending Council Directive 64/433/EEC (OJ No. L121, 29.7.64; OJ/SE 1963—64, p.185) on health problems affecting intra-Community trade in fresh meat.

items.title: The Fresh Meat and Poultry Meat (Hygiene, Inspection and Examinations for Residues) (Charges) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations implement in part the provisions of Council Decision 88/408/EEC(OJ No.L194, 22.7.88, p.24) on the level of the fees to be charged for health inspections and controls of fresh meat pursuant to Council Directive 85/73/EEC (OJ No.L32, 5.2.85, p.14).

items.title: The Meat Inspection (Amendment) Regulations 1990
