items.description: This version of the The A41 LondonBirmingham Trunk Road (East of Tring to East of Bourne End, Berkhamsted) Detrunking Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A41 London - Birmingham Trunk Road (Kings Langley Bypass) Order 1985 Amendment Order and New Trunk Roads Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A41 London - Birmingham Trunk Road (Kings Langley Bypass) Order 1985 Amendment Order and New Trunk Roads Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Personal Community Charge (Relief) (Wales) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations set out a scheme for reducing the liability of certain individuals in Wales to pay a personal community charge. The scheme applies for the financial year beginning 1st April 1990.

items.title: The (A500) Hanford-Etruria Principal Road (Trunking) and (A34) Winchester-Preston Trunk Road (De-trunking from Hanford to Talke) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The (A500) Hanford-Etruria Principal Road (Trunking) and (A34) Winchester-Preston Trunk Road (De-trunking from Hanford to Talke) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Firearms (Variation of Fees) Order 1990 (revoked)
items.description: This Order, which extends only to England and Wales, increases the fees payable under the Firearms Act 1968. Article 4 increases the fees payable on the grant, renewal or replacement of firearm and shot gun certificates and the variation of firearm certificates. (There is no increase in the lower fee payable under section 11(3) of the Firearms (Amendment) Act 1988 for the grant or renewal of a shot gun certificate when a firearm certificate is granted or renewed on the same occasion.) Article 5 retains the exemption from payment of fees in respect of firearm certificates for certain signalling devices. Article 6 increases the fees payable in relation to the registration of firearms dealers.

items.title: The Petty Sessional Divisions (Avon) Order 1990
items.description: This Order gives effect to a draft order submitted by the magistrates' courts committee for the county of Avon and provides for the petty sessional divisions of Avon North and Thornbury to be combined to form a new petty sessional division of North Avon, and for the petty sessional divisions of Bath and Wansdyke to be combined to form a new petty sessional division of Bath and Wansdyke.

items.title: The Community Charges and Non-Domestic Rating (Demand Notices) (Wales) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations provide for the form and contents of community charge demand notices, and for the contents of rate demand notices, which are issued by charging authorities (district councils) in Wales, and for the information to be supplied when such notices are served by them.

items.title: The Borders and Lothian Regions and Tweeddale and Midlothian Districts (Carlops) Boundaries Amendment Order 1990
items.description: This Order transfers on 1st April 1990 an area of approximately 0.35 hectare at Paties Mill in the vicinity of Carlops from Midlothian District in Lothian Region to Tweeddale District in Borders Region and makes consequential changes to electoral arrangements.

items.title: The Schools (Safety and Supervision of Pupils) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations provide that, without prejudice to any other duty owed under statute or common law, an education authority shall take reasonable care for the safety of pupils under their charge. They are also specifically required to secure that at each school under their management which is either a primary school attended by 50 or more pupils, or a special school, pupils are supervised by at least one adult when in a playground during any break time. This specific duty is stated to be without prejudice to the provision of such supervision as is necessary to comply with the general duty to take reasonable care for pupils' safety in any circumstances.

items.title: The Borders Regional Council (Galashiels Mill Lade) (Amendment) Water Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Borders Regional Council (Galashiels Mill Lade) (Amendment) Water Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Welfare Food Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Welfare Food Regulations 1988.

items.title: The Export of Sheep (Prohibition) (No. 2) Amendment Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Export of Sheep (Prohibition) (No. 2) Amendment Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Petty Sessional Divisions (Hertfordshire) Order 1990
items.description: This Order gives effect to a draft Order submitted by the magistrates' courts committee for the county of Hertfordshire and provides for the combination of the petty sessional divisions of Hatfield and Welwyn to form one new division to be known as the Mid Hertfordshire petty sessional division.

items.title: The Dangerous Substances (Notification and Marking of Sites) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations require the notification and marking of sites where there is a total quantity of 25 tonnes or more of dangerous substances present at the site.

items.title: The Potteries etc. (Modifications) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Pottery (Health) Special Regulations 1947 of which regulation 4 (relating to the prohibition of lead glazes) is the only substantive provision now in force and modify the Control of Lead at Work Regulations 1980 to provide equivalent prohibitions.

items.title: The Education (Grants) (Travellers and Displaced Persons) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the Secretary of State to pay grants to local education authorities in England and Wales in respect of expenditure for the time being approved by him and incurred or to be incurred by them in making provision the purpose (or main purpose) of which is to promote and facilitate the education of Travellers, refugees and displaced or similar persons who fall within the provisions of regulation 4 (regulations 3 and 4).

items.title: The Census Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations provide for the detailed arrangements necessary for the conduct of the census directed to be taken by the Census Order 1990.

items.title: The Hill Livestock (Compensatory Allowances) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Great Britain, further amend the Hill Livestock (Compensatory Allowances) Regulations 1984 (“the principal Regulations”), which implemented part of Council Directive No. 75/268/EEC (O.J. No. L128, 19.5.75, p. 1) on mountain and hill farming and farming in certain less-favoured areas, and comply with Articles 13 to 15 of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 797/85 (O.J. No. L93, 30.3.85, p. 1), on improving the efficiency of agricultural structures, as amended.

items.title: The Diseases of Animals (Approved Disinfectants) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Diseases of Animals (Approved Disinfectants) Order 1978 by substituting new schedules for Schedules 1 and 2 to that Order (article 2(a) and (b)). Schedule 1, which lists approved disinfectants, includes newly approved disinfectants, and Schedule 2 lists disinfectants which are now omitted from Schedule 1 but which may nevertheless continue to be used as approved disinfectants until 30th June 1990 (article 2(c)).

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (England) Amendment Order 1990
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (England) Order 1987 contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent the human consumption of food which has been or which may have been rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the USSR.

items.title: The Data Protection (Regulation of Financial Services etc.) (Subject Access Exemption) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: Section 30 of the Data Protection Act 1984 provides that personal data held for the purpose of discharging statutory functions which are designated by the Secretary of State by an order made under that section shall be exempt from the subject access provisions of the Act in any case in which the pplication of those provisions to the data would be likely to prejudice the proper discharge of those functions. The functions which may be designated include those conferred by or under any enactment appearing to the Secretary of State to be designed for protecting members of the public against fnancial loss due to dishonesty, incompetence or malpractice by persons concerned in the provision of banking, insurance, investment or other financial services or in the management of companies or to the conduct of discharged or undischarged bankrupts.

items.title: The Common Land (Rectification of Registers) Regulations 1990
items.description: The Common Land (Rectification of Registers) Act 1989 (c. 18) enables the removal from the registers of common land and town and village greens, maintained under the Commons Registration Act 1965 (c. 64), of land on which there is a dwellinghouse or which is ancillary to a dwellinghouse, and in respect of which that requirement has been satisfied at all times since 5 August 1945. These Regulations prescribe the procedure and, in the Schedules, the forms.

items.title: The Social Security Act 1989 (Commencement No. 4) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Social Security Act 1989 (Commencement No. 4) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Education (Inner London Education Authority) (Property Transfer) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order corrects a defect in article 11 of the Education (Inner London Education Authority) (Property Transfer) Order 1990 (S.I. 1990/124). The powers conferred on the London Residuary Body by the article as originally drafted are incapable of exercise since the article only comes into force on the date (27th February 1990) by which the powers purportedly have to be exercised. The amendment permits the exercise of the powers before 15th March.

items.title: The Land Registration Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules:1Provide that any person may apply to the proper office for:aan official certificate of the result of search in the Index Map or General Map, Parcels Index and the list of pending applications for first registration;ba personal inspection of the Index Map or General Map and the Parcels Index and to be informed whether there is a pending application for first registration;prescribe the forms to be used for such purposes and revoke rules 12 and 286 of the Land Registration Rules 1925 (“the principal rules”) which previously regulated such applications.2Provide:athat during the currency of any notice given pursuant to rule 5(2) of these Rules and subject to and in accordance with the limitations contained in that notice, an application may be made by facsimile transmission to the proper office for:ian office copy of the entries on the register or a title plan of a registered title or a certificate of official inspection of the filed plan;iian office copy of a document referred to in the register of a title;iiian official certificate of the result of search of the Index Map;and prescribe the forms to be used and make a consequential amendment to rule 296 of the principal rules (Office Copies of entries on register);bthat no other applications may be made by facsimile transmission.3Amend the following rules of the principal rules:arule 1(5A) (which defines “proper office”) to refer to the Land Registration (District Registries) Order 1989 rather than to the Land Registration (District Registries) Order 1984;brules 24 and 83 (Delivery of applications) to provide that where an application under the principal rules would otherwise be required to be delivered under either of those rules to two or more proper offices it may be delivered to any one of those offices and if so delivered shall be treated as duly delivered to all of those proper offices;crule 85 (Date of delivery of applications) to refer to any proper office;drule 70 (Inspection of cautions) to provide that the registrar shall allow any person interested in any land which is affected by a caution against first registration to inspect the caution and the statutory declaration in support of it;erule 317 (Applications not in order) to provide that a pending application may be cancelled if a cheque given in payment of the application fee is dishonoured.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Cars) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Value Added Tax (Cars) Order 1980 in respect of the tax to be charged on supplies of used motor cars.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Capital Finance) (Rate of Discount) Regulations 1990
items.description: Part IV of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 makes provision for the capital finance of local authorities. Section 49(2) sets out a formula for determining for the purposes of Part IV the value of the consideration falling to be given by a local authority under a credit arrangement in any financial year after the one in which the arrangement comes into being. The percentage rate of discount prescribed for a financial year is one of the factors referred to in the formula. These Regulations prescribe 11 per cent. for the financial year beginning on 1st April 1990.

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations 1986 by increasing the permitted overall length of articulated vehicles and of semi-trailers so long as certain conditions are met.

items.title: The Electricity Supply Pension Scheme (Transfer Date Amendments) Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Electricity Supply Pension Scheme (Transfer Date Amendments) Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Smoke Control Areas (Authorised Fuels) Regulations 1990
items.description: Section 11 of the Clean Air Act 1956 makes the occupier of a building within a smoke control area guilty of an offence if smoke is emitted from the chimney of that building, unless it can be proved that the emission of smoke was caused solely by the use of an authorised fuel.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) Amendment Order 1990
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) Order 1989 contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape in April 1986 of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the Ukraine, USSR.

items.title: The Social Security Benefits Up-rating Order 1990
items.description: This Order, a draft of which has been laid before and approved by resolution of each House of Parliament, is made as a consequence of a review under section 63(1) of the Social Security Act 1986 (“the 1986 Act”) and includes details of the sums mentioned in that section.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Re-rating) Order 1990
items.description: This Order provides for matters in respect of which the Social Security (Contributions and Allocation of Contributions) (Re-rating) Order 1989 (S.I.1989/26) contained provisions.

items.title: The Social Security (Recoupment) Regulations 1990,
items.description: This version of the The Social Security (Recoupment) Regulations 1990, is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Land Charges (Fees) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, with the Land Charges Fees Rules 1990 (S.I. 1990/327), revokes the Land Charges Fees Order 1985. It provides for fees payable under the Land Charges Act 1972 to be collected in money.

items.title: The Caithness Salmon Fishery District Designation Order 1990
items.description: This Order creates a new salmon fishery district, to be known as the Caithness Salmon Fishery District, by amalgamating the salmon fishery districts of Forss, Thurso, Wick, Dunbeath and Berriedale (which are abolished) along with the island of Stroma (article 2 and Schedule 1).

items.title: The Firearms (Variation of Fees) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, which extends only to Scotland, increases the fees payable under the Firearms Act 1968. Article 4 increases the fees payable on the grant, renewal or replacement of firearm and shot gun certificates and the variation of firearm certificates. (There is no increase in the lower fee payable under section 11(3) of the Firearms (Amendment) Act 1988 for the grant or renewal of a shot gun certificate when a firearms certificate is granted or renewed on the same occasion.) Article 5 retains the exemption from payment of fees in respect of firearm certificates for certain signalling devices. Article 6 increases the fees payable in relation to the registration of firearms dealers.

items.title: The Census (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, provide for the detailed arrangements necessary for the conduct of the census directed to be taken by the Census Order 1990.

items.title: The Land Charges Fees Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules, which replace the Land Charges Fees Order 1985, prescribe the fees payable in respect of the services provided by the Land Charges Department of H.M. Land Registry and the methods of payment of such fees.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemption) (England) Order 1990
items.description: This Order has the effect of not requiring the Wrekin District Council to put out to competitive tendering catering at Barclay Lodge so long as it is used, wholly or partly, as housing accommodation for elderly or infirm persons who need assistance with eating or who need special diets.

items.title: The Fixed Penalty (Increase) Order 1990
items.description: This Order increases the amount of the fixed penalty for a fixed penalty offence to £32 from £24, in the case of an offence involving obligatory endorsement, and £16 from £12, in any other case.

items.title: The Fixed Penalty Offences Order 1990
items.description: This Order provides for certain offences relating to pedestrian crossings regulations to be fixed penalty offences for purposes of Part III of the Road Traffic Offenders Act 1988 and makes modifications to Schedule 3 to the 1988 Act, which specifies offences which are fixed penalty offences for those purposes, in consequence.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Witnesses' Addresses) Rules 1990
items.description: This version of the The Magistrates' Courts (Witnesses' Addresses) Rules 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The River Ness Salmon Fishery District (Baits and Lures) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations specify natural prawns and shrimps and any part of them for the purposes of the definition of “rod and line” in section 24(1) of the Salmon and Freshwater Fisheries (Protection) (Scotland) Act 1951. They apply throughout the River Ness salmon fishery district for the whole of each year. The effect is to prohibit the use of natural prawns and shrimps (or any part of them) as bait when fishing by rod and line for salmon and sea trout.

items.title: The Certification Officer (Amendment of Fees) Regulations 1990
The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Wales) (No. 5) Amendment Order 1990
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Wales) (No. 5) Order 1987, as amended by S.I. 1988/9, 951, 1329, 1680, 1989/2, 660, 1279 and 1770 contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food which has been or which may have been rendeed unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the USSR.

items.title: The City of London (Non-Domestic Rating Multiplier) Order 1990
items.description: This Order makes provision as to the calculation of the required minimum and maximum within which the Common Council of the City of London must set its non-domestic rating multiplier for the financial year beginning in 1990. (The Common Council is the only charging authority which meets the definition of “special authority” set out in section 144(6) of the Local Government Finance Act 1988.)

items.title: The Smoke Control Areas (Exempted Fireplaces) Order 1990
items.description: Section 11 of the Clean Air Act 1956 empowers local authorities to declare the whole or any part of their district to be a smoke control area in which the emission of smoke is generally prohibited. Specified classes of fireplace may be exempted from control by order.

items.title: The Electricity (Protected Persons) (England and Wales) Pension Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations provide for the protection of the pension rights of existing and former employees and Board Members in the electricity supply industry in England and Wales on the transfer date as defined in section 95(1) of the Electricity Act 1989 (and which will be 31st March 1990), and their dependants (“protected persons”), who are or were participants in the Electricity Supply Pension Scheme (“the Pension Scheme”).

items.title: The Poultry Breeding Flocks and Hatcheries (Registration and Testing) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Poultry Breeding Flocks and Hatcheries (Registration and Testing) Order 1989. The requirement for the appropriate Minister to make the Breeding Flocks Register and the Hatcheries Register available for public inspection is omitted (article 2(b)). The Order confirms that the duty of a registered person under article 6(a) of the 1989 Order concerning the taking of samples is applied even where only one bird is kept in a house (article 2(c)(i)). However, as before, the 1989 Order will require a person to be registered only if he keeps a breeding flock which consists of not less than 25 birds.

items.title: The Poultry Laying Flocks (Testing and Registration etc.) (Amendment) Order 1990
The Financial Services Act 1986 (Restriction of Scope of Act and Meaning of Collective Investment Scheme) Order 1990
items.description: This Order restricts the scope of the Financial Services Act 1986 in two respects. It also consolidates the provisions of earlier orders amending the references to a collective investment scheme in Schedule 1 to and elsewhere in the Financial Services Act 1986 and makes certain amendments to Schedule 1 to that Act to clarify the effect of earlier orders amending that Schedule.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Norwich Cablevision Ltd) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Norwich Cablevision Ltd on 19th February 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the whole of the City of Norwich and a small part of each of the Districts of outh Norfolk and Broadlands. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 1st March 1990. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Norwich Cablevision Ltd will be a public telecommunicatios operator when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Building Societies (Designation of Qualifying Bodies) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Building Societies (Designation of Qualifying Bodies) Order 1988, which designates corporate bodies and types of corporate body as suitable for investment and support or for support by building societies under section 18 of the Building Societies Act 1986.

items.title: The Building Societies (Designation of Qualifying Bodies) (Amendment) (No 2) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Building Societies (Designation of Qualifying Bodies) (Amendment) (No 2) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Education (School Financial Statements) (Prescribed Particulars etc.) Regulations 1990
items.description: Section 42 of the Education Reform Act 1988 requires local education authorities whose financial provision for county and voluntary schools maintained by them is subject to regulation by a scheme made under section 33 of that Act to prepare before the beginning of each financial year a statement of the financial provision they plan to make for those schools in that year (a “budget statement”) and after the end of each financial year a statement of the expenditure actually incurred for the purposes of those schools in that year (an “outturn statement”).

items.title: The Companies Act 1989 (Commencement No. 3, Transitional Provisions and Transfer of Functions under the Financial Services Act 1986) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 15th March 1990 certain provisions of the Companies Act 1989 (“the 1989 Act”) which amend the Financial Services Act 1986 (“the 1986 Act”).

items.title: The Companies Act 1989 (Commencement No. 4 and Transitional and Saving Provisions) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force certain of the provisions of the Companies Act 1989.

items.title: The Central and Tayside Regions and Stirling and Perth and Kinross Districts (Loch Earn/Loch Tay) Boundaries Amendment Order 1990
items.description: This Order transfers on 1st April 1990 from Perth and Kinross District in Tayside Region to Stirling District in Central Region, 2 areas of ground which are under water, and extend respectively to approximately 263 hectares at Loch Earn and 565 hectares at Loch Tay, and makes consequential changes to electoral arrangements.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (United Cable Television (London South) Plc) (Merton & Sutton) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to United Cable Television (London South) Plc on 24 January 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the whole of the London Boroughs of Merton and Sutton. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 28th February 1990. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, United Cable Television (London South) Plc will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its Merton and Sutton public telecommunication systems when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The A339 Basingstoke to Newbury Trunk Road (Basingstoke Northern Bypass Trunking and Detrunking) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A339 Basingstoke to Newbury Trunk Road (Basingstoke Northern Bypass Trunking and Detrunking) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Blood Tests (Evidence of Paternity) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations abolish the maximum charge which may be made by blood testers in respect of blood tests carried out for the purpose of determining paternity in civil proceedings under the Blood Tests (Evidence of Paternity) Regulations 1971.

items.title: The Bournemouth and District, South West, Tendring Hundred and West Hampshire (Pipelaying and Other Works) (Codes of Practice) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Bournemouth and District, South West, Tendring Hundred and West Hampshire (Pipelaying and Other Works) (Codes of Practice) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Education (Areas to which Pupils and Students Belong) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make corrections in the Education (Areas to which Pupils and Students Belong) Regulations 1989 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Education (Inner London Education Authority) (Horniman and Geffrye Museums) (Transfer of Functions) Order 1990
items.description: This Order transfers to the London Residuary Body (“L.R.B.”) the functions of the Inner London Education Authority (ILEA) in relation to the Horniman and Geffrye Museums. The transfer takes effect on 1st April 1990, the date on which ILEA is abolished by section 162 of the Education Reform Act 1988 and those Museums transfer to the L.R.B. under section 180 of that Act.

items.title: The Landlord and Tenant Act 1954 (Appropriate Multiplier) Order 1990
items.description: This Order prescribes the appropriate multiplier to be used in calculating the amount of compensation to which a tenant may be entitled, under Part II of the Landlord and Tenant Act 1954, where an order for a new tenancy is precluded on certain grounds. The amount of compensation is arrived at bymultiplying the rateable value of the holding (as determined for the purposes of section 37(2) of the 1954 Act) or twice that rateable value, as the case may be, by the appropriate multiplier. The Order is consequential on the discontinuance of domestic rating and the re-rating of non-domestic property under the Local Government Finance Act 1988 (c. 41)

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Light Dues) Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Merchant Shipping (Light Dues) Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Load Lines) (Exemption) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Merchant Shipping (Load Lines) (Exemption) Order 1968 to remove the exemption for sailing ships which, if they were under 80 tons register engaged solely in the coasting trade, were exempt from the requirements of the Merchant Shipping (Load Lines) Act 1967 while not carrying cargo. Sailing ships which come within one of the other classes in the Exemption Order will still be exempt (as will pleasure yachts which are excluded from the application of the Act).

items.title: The Community Charges and Non-Domestic Rating (Demand Notices) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations clarify certain provisions of the Community Charges and Non-Domestic Rating (Demand Notices) (England) Regulations 1990 dealing with the supply of information to charging authorities by precepting authorities (regulation 2(1)) and the calculation of amounts to be so supplied (regulation 2(3)), extend the cases where in giving such information precepting authorities may use estimates of relevant population (regulation 2(5)), and correct errors in provisions of those Regulations dealing with the calculation of amounts to be shown in a demand notice and the information to accompany a demand notice (regulation 2(2), (4) and (6)). 50p net

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Assessment of Environmental Effects) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations are concerned with the implementation in England and Wales of Council Directive 85/337/EEC (OJ No. L175, 5.7.85, p. 40) on the assessment of the effects of certain public and private projects on the environment. They amend the Town and Country Planning (Assessment of Environmental Effects) Regulations 1988, which apply where planning permission is sought of a local planning authority under Part III of the Town and Country Planning Act 1971 (c. 78).

items.title: The Lothian and Strathclyde Regions and West Lothian and Motherwell Districts (Westcraigs) Boundaries Amendment Order 1990
items.description: This Order transfers on 1st April 1990 an area of approximately 191 hectares at Westcraigs from Motherwell District in Strathclyde Region to West Lothian District in Lothian Region and makes consequential changes to electoral arrangements.

items.title: The Community Charges and Non-Domestic Rating (Demand Notices) (City of London) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations provide for the contents of community charge demand notices and rate demand notices issued by the Common Council of the City of London, and for the information to be supplied when such notices are served.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuffs) (England) Order 1990
items.description: This Order replaces the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) (No.4) Order 1989.It contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of lead into material used in the manufacture of imported animal feeding stuff.

items.title: The Home Purchase Assistance (Winding Up of Scheme) Order 1990
items.description: This Order provides for the winding up of the home purchase assistance scheme. No notice can be given by a purchaser prior to the commencement of saving with a view to qualifying for assistance after 31st March 1990, and no qualifying period of saving in the absence of such a notice can commence after the same date. An application for assistance must be made before 1st April 1993.

items.title: The A663 Broadway (A627(M) to Manchester City Boundary) (Trunking) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A663 Broadway (A627(M) to Manchester City Boundary) (Trunking) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers-at-Arms) 1990
items.description: This Act of Sederunt provides for regulations to apply to the charging of fees by, and for fees of, Messengers-at-Arms and revokes the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers-at-Arms) 1978 and the Acts of Sederunt which amended it. The Table of Fees in the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers-at-Arms) 1978 has been restructured in this Act of Sederunt, but the overall level of fees in the Table of Fees in this Act of Sederunt should remain the same because no percentage increase in fees has been applied to those fees chargeable since the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers-at-Arms) 1989.£1.35p net

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Copyright, Designs and Patents) 1990
items.description: This version of the Act of Sederunt (Copyright, Designs and Patents) 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees of Sheriff Officers) 1990
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Act of Sederunt)

items.title: The National Health Service (Superannuation) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (Superannuation) (Scotland) Regulations 1980 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Teachers' Superannuation (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Teachers' Superannuation (Scotland) Regulations 1977.

items.title: The Employment Protection (Variation of Limits) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st April 1990, varies certain of the limits which are required to be reviewed annually by the Secretary of State under section 148 of the Employment Protection (Consolidation) Act 1978. The limit on the amount of guarantee payment payable under section 15(1)of the 1978 Act in respect of any day is increased from £11.85 to £12.65. The limit on the amount for the purpose of calculating the sum payable by the Secretary of State under section 122 of the 1978 Act in respect of a debt due to an employee whose employer becomes insolvent is increased from £12 to £184. The limits on the amount of “a week’s pay” for the purposes of calculating redundancy payments and basic and additional awards of compensation for unfair dismissal are increased from £172 to £184.

items.title: The Sugar Beet (Research and Education) Order 1990
items.description: This Order provides for the carrying into effect for the year 1990/91 of the programme, as set out in the Schedule, of research and education in matters affecting the growing of home-grown beet.

items.title: The Gaming Act (Variation of Fees) Order 1990
items.description: This Order increases the fees to be charged in England and Wales under the Gaming Act 1968 for the matters mentioned in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Lotteries (Gaming Board Fees) Order 1990
items.description: This Order increases the fees which are payable to the Gaming Board under paragraph 7(1) of Schedule 2 to the Lotteries and Amusements Act 1976 in connection with the registration of lottery schemes and with lotteries promoted under schemes registered by the Board. The fees payable in cases to which paragraphs (a)and (b) of article 2 apply are increased from £100 and £350 to £120 and £420 respectively. The fees payable in cases to which paragraphs (a), (b) and (c) of article 3 apply are increased from £30, £60 and £75 to £36, £72 and £90 respectively.

items.title: The Assistance for Minor Works to Dwellings Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations deal with the giving of assistance under section 131 of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989, for the provision of minor works to dwellings. Assistance may be in the form of either a grant or the provision of materials.

items.title: The Electricity Supply (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Electricity Supply Regulations 1988 (“the 1988 Regulations”), which by virtue of paragraph 3(a) of Schedule 17 to the Electricity Act 1989 (“the Act”) have effect as if made under section 29 of the Act.

items.title: The Education Reform Act 1988 (Commencement No. 8 and Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 31st March 1990 three of the remaining provisions of the Education Reform Act 1988 not yet in force.

items.title: The Electricity Act 1989 (Requirement of Consent for Hydro-electric Generating Stations) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: Section 36 of the Electricity Act 1989 requires the consent of the Secretary of State to construction, extension or operation of an electricity generating station with a capacity exceeding 50 megawatts. The Secretary of State has power to vary this limit, and in doing so may make different provision for generating stations of different classes or descriptions.

items.title: The Electricity Act 1989 (Modification of Local Enactments) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Order)

items.title: The Electricity Act 1989 (Scottish Nuclear Limited) Extinguishment of Loans Order 1990
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Order)

items.title: The Potassium Bromate (Prohibition as a Flour Improver) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Potassium Bromate (Prohibition as a Flour Improver) (Scotland) Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Rent Officers (Additional Functions) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order revokes and re-enacts with minor modifications the Rent Officers (Additional Functions) (Scotland) Order 1989 (as amended by the Rent Officers (Additional Functions) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1989) which conferred additional functions on rent officers in connection with housing benefit and rent allowance subsidy.

items.title: The Gaming Act (Variation of Fees) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order increases the fees to be charged in Scotland under the Gaming Act 1968 for the matters mentioned in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The City of Edinburgh, East Lothian and Midlothian Districts (Whitehill Mains) Boundaries Amendment Order 1990
items.description: This Order transfers on 1st April 1990 four adjacent areas amounting in total to approximately 64.2 hectares between East Lothian and Midlothian Districts, and between East Lothian and the City of Edinburgh Districts, and makes consequential changes to electoral arrangements.

items.title: The Potassium Bromate (Prohibition as a Flour Improver) Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Potassium Bromate (Prohibition as a Flour Improver) Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Social Security Benefit (Persons Abroad) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security Benefit (Persons Abroad) Regulations 1975 (the 1975 Regulations).

items.title: The Immigration (Registration with Police) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations substitute a new regulation for regulation 10 of the Immigration (Registration with Police) Regulations 1972. The new regulation increases the fee payable when aliens are required to register with the police from £25 to £36. The new regulation also deletes the special provision which was previously made in regulation 10 in respect of registration by Community nationals to reflect the fact that such nationals are no longer granted leave under the Immigration Act 1971 and are therefore no longer required to register with the police.

items.title: The Police (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Police Regulations 1987 in relation to rent allowance, supplementary rent allowance and compensatory grant.

items.title: The Personal Community Charge (Relief) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make a number of amendments to the Personal Community Charge (Relief) (England) Regulations 1990.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemptions) (England) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: Under Part I of the Local Government Act 1988 work in connection with certain activities may only be undertaken by local authorities if particular conditions are complied with. The conditions effectively require that the work be put out to tender.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Capital Finance) (Prescribed Bodies) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the London Pensions Fund Authority and the South Yorkshire Pensions Authority as bodies to which the new capital finance system for local authorities under Part IV of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 is to apply, subject to the modification specified in regulation 3.

items.title: The London Residuary Body (Transfer of Property etc) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The London Residuary Body (Transfer of Property etc) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Local Government (Politically Restricted Posts) Regulations 1990
items.description: Local Authorities are under a duty to prepare and maintain a list of posts in respect of which the annual rate of remuneration is £19,500 or above.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Cash Accounting) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations which come into force on 1st April 1990 amend the Value Added Tax (Cash Accounting) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1427) (“the principal regulations”).

items.title: The Personal Community Charge (Relief) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision, in Scotland, for reducing, in certain circumstances, a person’s liability to pay the personal community charge for the financial years 1989-90, 1990-91 and 1991-92.

items.title: The Local Government Superannuation (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Local Government Superannuation (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in English) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: Section 4(2) of the Education Reform Act 1988 places a duty on the Secretary of State to establish the National Curriculum by specifying appropriate attainment targets, programmes of study and assessment arrangements for each of the foundation subjects.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in Technology) Order 1990
items.description: Section 4(2) of the Education Reform Act 1988 places a duty on the Secretary of State to establish the National Curriculum by specifying appropriate attainment targets, programmes of study and assessment arrangements for each of the foundation subjects.

items.title: The Oil Pollution (Compulsory Insurance) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Oil Pollution (Compulsory Insurance) Regulations 1981 by increasing the fee to be paid on application for a certificate of compulsory insurance from £23 to £25.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Capital Finance) (Approved Investments) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations contain a list of investments which are approved by the Secretary of State for the purposes of Part IV of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989, which establishes a new capital finance system for local authorities from 1st April 1990.

items.title: The Rent Assessment Committees (England and Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Rent Assessment Committees (England and Wales) Regulations 1971 (which regulate the procedure to be followed by rent assessment committees) to take account of the functions of rent assessment committees under Schedule 10 to the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 in relation to long residential tenancies.

items.title: The Rent Officers (Additional Functions) Order 1990
items.description: This Order revokes and re-enacts with minor modifications the Rent Officers (Additional Functions) Order 1989, as amended by the Rent Officers (Additional Functions) (Amendment) Order 1989, which conferred functions on rent officers in connection with housing benefit and rent allowance subsidy. Article 3 provides that where a local authority applies (and is required, by regulations made under section 30(2B) or (2C) of the Social Security Act 1986, to apply) to a rent officer for determinations relating to a tenancy or licence of a dwelling, the rent officer must make the determinations and give notice to the local authority in accordance with Schedule 1 to the Order. The determinations relate to the level of rent, the size of the dwelling and rent attributable to the provision of services. Article 4 provides for a rent officer, with the advice of one or two other rent officers, to make a redetermination if a local authority applies for one.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (Wales) Order 1990
items.description: This Order replaces the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (Wales) (No. 4) Order 1989. It contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of lead into material used in the manufacture of imported animal feeding stuff.

items.title: The Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (Commencement No. 5 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 5th March 1990 provisions in the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (section 194(2), partially, and section 194(3)) which enable the Secretary of State to make an order in respect of repeals and amendments consequential upon Part IV of that Act (revenue accounts and capital finance of local authorities).

items.title: The Local Authorities (Capital Finance) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which are made under Part IV of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (“the Act”), are concerned with the new capital finance system for local authorities which operates from 1st April 1990. The new system replaces the controls on capital expenditure contained in Part VIII of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980. The Regulations apply to England and Wales and are to come into force on 1st April 1990.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Goods and Services) (Public Bodies) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates the City Literary Institute and the Medway Housing SocietyLimited as public bodies under the Local Authorities (Goods and Services) Act 1970.The effect of the designation is to allow a local authority to provide goods and services tothese bodies. In the case of Medway Housing Society Limited this is limited to theprovision of administrative, professional or technical services in connection with certainhousing.

items.title: The References to Rating (Housing) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend certain housing legislation in consequence of the discontinuance of rating of domestic property. The enactments affected include those which relate to leasehold enfranchisement, assured tenancies, regulated tenancies, housing association tenancies, long tenancies at low rents, agricultural occupancies and statutory tenancies and control orders relating to houses in multiple occupation. Regulation 3 preserves a definition of agricultural land in the General Rate Act 1967 for the purposes of the Rent Act 1977, Parts IV and V of the Housing Act 1985 and paragraph 6 of Schedule 1 to the Housing Act 1988.

items.title: The Accounts and Audit (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Accounts and Audit Regulations 1983 by deleting the reference in regulation 7 to the Inner London Education Authority (which is abolished from 1st April 1990), and by amending the reference in regulation 2(2) to the accounts of the City so as to refer to the accounts mentioned in section 12(3) of the Local Government Finance Act 1982 (as substituted by paragraph 5 of Schedule 12 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988). These amendments have effect for accounts for periods beginning on or after 1st April 1990.

items.title: The Companies (Unregistered Companies) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: The Companies (Unregistered Companies) (Amendment) Regulations 1985 applied certain provisions of the Companies Act 1985 to unregistered companies.

items.title: The Insolvency Practitioners Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Insolvency Practitioners Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees in the Scottish Record Office) 1990
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes provision for an increase in most fees chargeable by the Scottish Record Office of 50 per cent. overall. The introduction of specification of size in electrostatic prints results in a reduction in one instance (see Schedule, Part III, item 1(2)(a)(i)). New fees are included for prints from microfiche and duplicate copies of existing microfiche.

items.title: The Oversea Companies (Accounts) (Modifications and Exemptions) Order 1990
items.description: This Order modifies the requirements of the Companies Act 1985 relating to the preparation of accounts and related documents for the purpose of their application to oversea companies. It also exempts oversea companies from the requirements listed in the Schedule to the Order. Certain oversea companies with unlimited liability are exempted altogether from such requirements of the 1985 Act.

items.title: The Lyon Court and Office Fees (Variation) Order 1990
items.description: This Order increases most of the fees for the matters listed in Schedule B to the Lyon King of Arms Act 1867. The fees were last increased in 1988. These fees are exclusive of stamp duty when stamp duty is exigible.

items.title: The Electricity and Pipe-line Works (Assessment of Environmental Effects) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations re-enact the Electricity and Pipe-line Works (Assessment of Environmental Effects) Regulations 1989 with modifications. They make textual changes consequent upon the Electricity Act 1989, make minor and drafting amendments and add provisions as to consultation, provision of information and service of notices.

items.title: The Offshore Generating Stations (Exemption) Order 1990
items.description: By section 36 of the Electricity Act 1989, the consent of the Secretary of State is required for the construction, extension or operation of a generating station above a permitted capacity.

items.title: The Housing (Prescribed Forms) Regulations 1990
items.description: Regulation 2 prescribes new forms for use by local housing authorities for the purposes of an order extinguishing a public right of way under section 294 of the Housing Act 1985 and for the purposes of Part X (overcrowding) of that Act.

items.title: The Goods Vehicles (Plating and Testing) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Goods Vehicle (Plating and Testing Regulations 1988 (the “1988 Regulations”) as follows.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Tests) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Motor Vehicles (Tests) (Amendment) Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Public Service Vehicles (Conditions of Fitness, Equipment, Use and Certification) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Public Service Vehicles (Conditions of Fitness, Equipment, Use and Certification) (Amendment) Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Horticultural Development Council (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 7th March 1990, amends the Horticultural Development Council Order 1986.

items.title: The Electricity (Applications for Consent) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations apply in relation to applications to the Secretary of State for Energy in England and Wales and the Secretary of State for Scotland in Scotland for consent under section 36 of the Electricity Act 1989 ( “the 1989 Act”) to construct, extend, operate or change the manner of operation of a generating station, or under section 37 of the 1989 Act to instal or keep installed an electric line above ground.

items.title: The New Towns (Defects Grants) (Payments to District Councils) (Extensions of Time) Order 1990
items.description: This Order defers the date for the termination of payments of grants under section 51A of the New Towns Act 1981 to Easington and Sedgefield District Councils and Stevenage Borough Council. The power of the Secretary of State to make such grants would otherwise cease on 1st April 1990.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning General Development (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Town and Country Planning General Development (Amendment) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Civil Aviation (Navigation Services Charges) (Fifth Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Civil Aviation(Navigation Services Charges) Regulations 1986. The following changesare made—1The charges payable to the Civil Aviation Authority for navigation services provided in connection with the use of the aerodromes specified in the Table have been changed as follows:—aIn respect of the three London Airports, the standard charge for the first 100 metric tonnes maximum total weight authorised of an aircraft, or part thereof, is increased from 1.55 per metric tonne to 1.80 for an aircraft engaged on international flights and from 1.31 per metric tonne to 1.52 for aircraft engaged on domestic flights.For each additional metric tonne, or part thereof, in excess of 100 metric tonnes, the charge is increased from 0.67 per metric tonne to 0.77 for aircraft engaged on international flights and from 0.54 per metric tonne to 0.62 for aircraft engaged on domestic flights.bCharges at AberdeenDyceare increased by 7.1 per cent for domestic and 6.9 per cent for international flights, charges at Edinburgh are increased by 7.0 per cent for domestic and 7.1 per cent for international flights.Charges at Glasgow are increased by 7.1 per cent for domestic and 6.9 per cent for international flights and charges at Prestwick are increased by 7.1 per cent for both domestic and international flights (regulation 2(1)).2The charge payable to the Civil Aviation Authority by the operator of an aircraft which flies within the Shanwick Oceanic Control Area and in respect of which a flight plan is communicated to the appropriate air traffic control unit is increased from 74 to 86 (regulation 2(2)).3The charge payable to the Civil Aviation Authority for a flight made by a helicopter from a point in the United Kingdom to an off-shore installation within the area of the northern North Sea specified in regulation 6(2) of the principal Regulations is increased from 110 to 115 (regulation 2(3)).4The charge payable to the Civil Aviation Authority for a flight made by a helicopter from a point in the United Kingdom to an off-shore installation within the area of the southern North Sea specified in regulation 6(4) of the principal Regulations is increased from 42.50 to 46 (regulation 2(4)).

items.title: The Police (Grant) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order further amends the Police (Grant) Order 1966. The percentage of a police authority’s share of the Secretary of State’s expenditure on common services which is to be deducted from the authority’s grant is fixed at 49 per cent (article 2), except in the case of the Metropolitan Police District where the percentage is 48 (article 4). The revocation (by article 3) of article 3 of the 1966 Order brings the City of London’s grant up to the same level as those of most other police authorities (51 per cent of net expenditure).

items.title: The Wireless Telegraphy (Broadcast Licence Charges and Exemption) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations raise the amount of the basic fee for television licences from £22 to £24 in the case of monochrome and from £66 to £71 in the case of colour. In the case of instalment licences the issue fee for the Standard Instalment Licence is increased from £33 to £35.50 with instalment fees raised from £16.50 to £17.75. For the Premium Instalment Licence the issue and instalment fees of £17.75 are both increased to £19.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Type Approval and Approval Marks) (Fees) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations re-enact with modifications the provisions of the Motor Vehicles (Type Approval and Approval Marks) (Fees) Regulations 1989.

items.title: The Personal Community Charge (Exemption for the Severely Mentally Impaired) Order 1990
items.description: Under paragraph 4 of Schedule 1 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988 a person who is severely mentally impaired and who holds a certificate of a registered medical practitioner to that effect is exempt from the personal community charge if one or more of the conditions mentioned in sub-paragraph (2) of that paragraph is satisfied. Those conditions were added to under article 3 of the Personal Community Charge (Exemptions) Order 1989 so as to allow a person to qualify for exemption if he is in receipt of an invalidity pension or an unemployment supplement or allowance. Article 2(1) of this Order adds further conditions so allowing a person to qualify if he is entitled to attendance allowance or constant attendance allowance (or would be entitled to it but for the requirements as to residence or presence in Great Britain or for a claim). It also deletes the condition relating to pensionable age, so that it is no longer possible for a severely mentally impaired person to qualify for exemption merely because he has reached that age.

items.title: The M3 Motorway (Bar End-Compton Section) Scheme 1990
items.description: This version of the The M3 Motorway (Bar End-Compton Section) Scheme 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The M3 Motorway (Bar End—Compton Section) Connecting Roads Scheme 1990
items.description: This version of the The M3 Motorway (Bar End—Compton Section) Connecting Roads Scheme 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Blight Provisions) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends sections 192 and 207 of the Town and Country Planning Act 1971 (which relate to planning blight) to take account of the changes to the rating system made by the Local Government Finance Act 1988. The Order also increases from £2,250 to £18,000 the annual value limit which applies for the purposes of the blight provisions in the case of an owner-occupier of a hereditament. The blight provisions enable a person with an interest in certain categories of land (including land affected by certain planning and highway proposals) to require the appropriate authority to purchase that interest.

items.title: The Fixed Penalty Offences (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: Section 75 of the Road Traffic Offenders Act 1988, which applies only in Scotland, authorises the procurator fiscal to offer an alleged offender an opportunity to avoid prosecution and conviction for certain road traffic offences by paying a fixed penalty. The offences to which this procedure can be applied are listed in Schedules 3 and 5 of that Act.

items.title: The Fixed Penalty (Increase) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, which extends to Scotland only, increases the amounts of the fixed penalties which may be offered by the procurator fiscal as an alternative to prosecution for certain road traffic offences, under Part III of the Road Traffic Offenders Act 1988. The fixed penalty for an offence involving obligatory endorsement of a driving licence is increased from £24 to £32 and for other offences from £12 to £16.

items.title: The National Health Service (Charges for Drugs and Appliances) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st April 1990, amend the National Health Service (Charges for Drugs and Appliances) (Scotland) Regulations 1989 (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for the making and recovery of charges for drugs and appliances (other than dental or optical appliances) supplied by chemists and doctors under or by virtue of the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978.

items.title: The Police (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police (Scotland) Regulations 1976 in relation to payment of overtime, rent allowance, supplementary rent allowance and compensatory grant.

items.title: The Insider Dealing (Recognised Stock Exchange) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order declares the investment exchange known as OM London Limited to be a recognised stock exchange for the purposes of the Company Securities (Insider Dealing) Act 1985 (“the Act”). The Act provides, amongst other matters, that it shall be an offence to deal in securities on a recognised stock exchange whilst in possession of unpublished price sensitive information (as defined in section 10) in relation to those securities, or to counsel or procure such dealing.

items.title: The High Court of Justiciary Fees Amendment Order 1990
items.description: This Order increases some of the fees payable to the Principal Clerk of Session and Justiciary or any officer acting for him.

items.title: The Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees in Civil Proceedings) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees in Civil Proceedings) Regulations 1984 so as to increase the fees allowable to Counsel for legal aid in civil proceedings given under the Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 1967. The increased fees will apply to fees for work done on or after 1st April 1990.

items.title: The Sheriff Court Fees Amendment Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Sheriff Court Fees Order 1985 so as to increase most of the fees payable to the sheriff clerk in relation to proceedings in the sheriff court. It also extends the scope for charging fees at paragraphs 6 and 18 to cover parties opposing an interim order.

items.title: The Civil Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Civil Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees) Regulations 1989 so as to increase the fees allowable to Counsel for civil legal aid (and for legal aid in certain proceedings relating to children) given under the Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 1986. The increase will apply to fees for work done on or after 1st April 1990. The overall increase is around 8%.

items.title: The Criminal Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Criminal Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees) Regulations 1989 so as to increase the fees allowable to Counsel for criminal legal aid given under the Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 1986. The increased fees will apply to proceedings concluded on or after 1st April 1990. The overall increase is around 8%.

items.title: The Scottish Land Court (Fees) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, which replaces the Scottish Land Court (Fees) Order 1989, prescribes fees payable in respect of applications to the Scottish Land Court and, in certain cases, increases the fees which were payable under the 1989 Order. It also extends the scope for charging fees at paragraph 1(e), 1(f), 2(d), 2(i) and 3(d) and introduces at 1(g) a new fee to the Table of Fees.

items.title: The Court of Session etc. Fees Amendment Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Court of Session etc. Fees Amendment Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Lands Tribunal for Scotland (Amendment) (Fees) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules amend the Lands Tribunal for Scotland Rules 1971 for the purpose of increasing the fees payable to the Tribunal. They also extend the scope for charging the fees at Items 4(b) and 6, and introduce new fees at Items 10 and 11 to the Table of Fees.

items.title: The Measuring Instruments (EEC Requirements) (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the fees payable in connection with services provided by the Department of Trade and Industry so that, with effect from 2nd April 1990—1the fees payable for an application for EEC initial verification of all classes of alcoholometers and alcohol hydrometers are increased from £26.20 to £28.00;2the fees payable per hour for EEC initial verification of certain instruments and systems are increased from £44.60 to £48.00; and3the fees payable per hour for EEC pattern approval for examiner staff time, for equipment test unit staff time and for the use of an environmental testing chamber are amended from £37.20, £30.20 and £11.20 to, respectively, £33.00, £48.00 and £9.20.

items.title: The Industrial Tribunals (Interest) Order 1990
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Order)

items.title: The Milk Quota (Calculation of Standard Quota) (Amendment) Order 1990 (revoked)
The Education (Inner London Education Authority) (Schools Designation) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates St Francis Xavier Sixth Form College in relation to the London Borough of Wandsworth and Woolverstone Hall in relation to Suffolk County Council. The schools are currently maintained by the Inner London Education Authority (“ILEA”). On the abolition of ILEA on 1st April 1990 it will become the duty of each authority in its capacity as a local education authority to maintain the school designated in relation to it.

items.title: The Education (Gloucestershire College of Arts and Technology (Higher Education) Higher Education Corporation) (Dissolution) Order 1990
items.description: This Order dissolves the higher education corporation named Gloucestershire College of Arts and Technology (Higher Education) and transfers the property, rights and liabilities of that corporation to the Cheltenham and Gloucester College of Higher Education Trust incorporating the Church of England Foundation of St Paul and St Mary. Contracts of employment are included in the transfer, and the Order contains supplemental provisions which provide for the continuity of employment of those persons whose contracts are being transferred.

items.title: The Education (Harrow College of Higher Education Higher Education Corporation) (Dissolution) Order 1990
items.description: This Order dissolves the higher education corporation named Harrow College of Higher Educationand transfers the property, rights and liabilities of that corporation to the Polytechnic of Central London. Contracts of employment are included in the transfer,and the Order contains supplemental provisions which provide continuity of employment for the of the persons whose contracts are being transferred. Certain property rightsand liabilities are transferred from the London Borough of Harrow Local Education Authority to the Harrow CHE Higher Education Corporation under sections 126and 127 of the Education Reform Act 1988 on 1st April 1990 by virtue of the Education (Higher Education Corporations) (No.6) Order 1989. The transfer effected by this Order takes effect on that date immediately after that transfer.50p net

items.title: The Pensions Increase (Review) Order 1990
items.description: Under section 59 of the Social Security Pensions Act 1975 as amended by section 11 of the Social Security Act 1979 and as modified by section 59A of the 1975 Act (introduced by section 11(4) of the 1979 Act) the Treasury (in whom the functions conferred by those provisions are now vested) are required to provide by order for the increase in the rates of public service pensions.

items.title: The Civil Legal Aid (Assessment of Resources) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Civil Legal Aid (Assessment of Resources) Regulations 1989 by—1increasing the free limit and eligibility limit of disposable income for civil legal aid from £2,515 and £6,035 to £2,645 and £6,350 respectively (regulation 4(1));2increasing the eligibility limit for disposable capital from £6,000 to £6,310 (regulation 4(1));3increasing the eligibility limits for disposable income to £7,000 and disposable capital to £8,000 where, the application for legal aid is made in respect of a personal injury claim (regulation 4(1));4omitting regulation 8 of the 1989 Regulations so that a child’s resources are separately assessed and are no longer taken to include sums to another payable for his maintenance (regulation 4(2));5amending regulation 9 of the 1989 Regulations to allow account to be taken of resources of which an applicant has deprived himself for whatever reason (regulation 4(3));6making provision for allowances to be made in respect of the community charge and water and sewerage charges (regulation 4(5)(a), (b) ands (d));7preserving the dependents' allowances applicable at the beginning of the computation period (regulation 4(5)(e);8allowing an additional disregard of capital on a sliding scale in respect of persons of pensionable age where their disposable income (excluding investment income) is below the free income limit (regulation 4(6)(b));9allowing a disregard in respect of capital payments made in relation to the incident which has led to the litigation (eg disaster fund payments) (regulation 4(6)(c)).

items.title: The Land Charges (Amendment) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules amend the Land Charges Rules 1974 to permit a facsimile transmission application for an official search in the index under section 10(1)(b) of the Land Charges Act 1972 and to prescribe the form in which it shall be made. Additionally, the Rules provide that an application for an office copy of an entry may also be made by facsimile transmission if the fee is debited to the applicant’s credit account, and prescribe the form to be used.

items.title: The Legal Advice and Assistance (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Legal Advice and Assistance Regulations 1989 by—1increasing the disposable capital limit for eligibility for advice and assistance from £890 to £935 (regulation 3(1);2amending regulation 22(5) of the 1989 Regulations so that it no longer applies to ABWOR in respect of proceedings covered by regulation 9 of the Legal Advice and Assistance (Scope) Regulations 1989 (S.I. 1989/550) (Mental Health Review Tribunal and board of prison visitors proceedings) (regulation 3(2));3amending paragraph 6 of Schedule 2 of the 1989 Regulations to allow account to be taken of resources of which the applicant has deprived himself for whatever reason (regulation 3(3));4amending the scale of contributions payable for legal advice and assistance (regulation 3(4)).

items.title: The Legal Advice and Assistance at Police Stations (Remuneration) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Legal Advice and Assistance at Police Stations (Remuneration) Regulations 1989 and increase the rates of remuneration for legal aid work done on or after 1st April 1990. There is an overall increase of 7 189 %.

items.title: The Legal Aid in Criminal and Care Proceedings (Costs) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Legal Aid in Criminal and Care Proceedings (Costs) Regulations 1989 by making some minor amendments to the standard fee provisions for solicitors and counsel and by increasing the rates of remuneration for legal aid work in criminal and care proceedings done on or after 1st April 1990. There is an overall increase of 7½% for work done by solicitors and 7% for work done by counsel.

items.title: The Legal Aid in Criminal and Care Proceedings (General) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Legal Aid in Criminal and Care Proceedings (General) Regulations 1989 by—1amending paragraph 3 of Schedule 3 of the 1989 Regulations to allow account to be taken of resources of which an applicant has deprived himself for whatever reason (regulation 3(1)(a));2making provision for allowances to be made in respect of the community charge and water and sewerage charges (regulation 3(1)(b) to (d));3increasing the levels of disposable income on which contribution is payable (regulation 3(2)).

items.title: The River Tay Catchment Area Protection (Renewal) Order 1990
items.description: This Order renews the River Tay Catchment Area Protection Order 1986 which prohibited fishing for or taking freshwater fish in inland waters in the area prescribed in that Order without legal right or without written permission from someone having such a right. The effect of the renewal is that the prohibition applies during the period from 30th January 1990 to 29th January 1993.

items.title: The Matrimonial Causes (Costs) (Amendment) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules amend the Matrimonial Causes (Costs) Rules 1988 so as to revise the prescribed rates of costs.

items.title: Register of County Court Judgments (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Register of County Court Judgments Regulations 1985 following the restriction of county courts' banking function by the County Court (Amendment No. 3) Rules 1989 (S.I. 1989/1838).

items.title: The Rules of the Supreme Court (Amendment) 1990
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Rules)

items.title: The Local Government Finance (Payments) (English Authorities) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations apply where the Secretary of State is liable under the Local Government Finance Act 1988 (“the Act”) to make payments to a charging authority, and the charging authority, or a precepting authority which precepts on that charging authority (“an appropriate precepting authority”), is at the same time liable under the Act to make payments to the Secretary of State.
