The Order also exempts the persons mentioned in article 3(1) from the requirement to obtain authorisation under the Financial Services Act in respect of any investment business they carry on and the persons mentioned in article 3(2) from that requirement to the extent specified in the article.

items.title: The Levying Bodies (General) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations confer a general power on bodies to issue levies to local authorities for the purpose of meeting their expenses in respect of financial years beginning in or after 1990 where, but for section 117 of the Local Government Finance Act 1988, they would have a statutory power to require the authorities to pay those expenses.

items.title: The References to Rating (Housing) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: The References to Rating (Housing) Regulations 1990 (which come into force on the same date as these Regulations) amend certain housing legislation in consequence of the discontinuance of rating of domestic property. These Regulations correct an error in those Regulations by amending sub-paragraph (ii) of section 1(1)(a) of the Leasehold Reform Act 1967 (c. 88), as substituted by the earlier Regulations. That sub-paragraph is relevant for determining whether a tenant under a tenancy entered into on or after 1st April 1990 can enfranchise or claim an extended lease.

items.title: The Public Trustee (Fees) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Public Trustee (Fees) (Amendment) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Highways (Road Humps) Regulations 1990
items.description: These regulations revoke the Highways (Road Humps) Regulations 1986 and replace them with new provisions.

items.title: The Traffic Signs (Amendment) Regulations 1990 and the Traffic Signs (Amendment) General Directions 1990
items.description: The Regulations in Part I of this instrument amend the Traffic Signs Regulations 1981 (“the 1981 Regulations”) as follows.They insert a plate (“Humped Pelican Crossing”) to be used with the sign shown in diagram 543 (traffic signals ahead) in a case where a road hump is constructed and maintained in accordance with the Highways (Road Humps) Regulations 1990 (“the 1990 Regulations”) within the limits of a “Pelican” crossing.They substitute for diagram 1060 a new diagram which indicates a round top road hump extending across the whole width of a carriageway. Traffic signs which comply with the previous version of diagram 1060 shall be treated as complying with the 1981 Regulations as amended by these Regulations for 2 years.They insert three new diagrams to indicate road humps as follows–diagram 1060.1 to indicate a round top road hump with tapered ends;diagram 1061 to indicate a flat top road hump extending across the whole width of a carriageway; anddiagram 1061.1 to indicate a flat top road hump with tapered ends.They insert a provision to clarify references to directions in the 1981 Regulations. Such references are to paragraphs in the Traffic Signs General Directions 1981.There is a consequential amendment in respect of the use of reflecting material on the markings shown in diagrams 1060.1, 1061 and 1061.1. There is also an amendment to the indication below diagram 547.5 (“Humped Zebra Crossing”) consequential on the coming into force of the 1990 Regulations.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No.1) (Miscellaneous) 1990
items.description: This Act of Sederunt–1amends the Rules of the Court of Session to–aprovide for applications in relation to the recall or restriction of an arrestment or an inhibition on the dependence of an action before calling to be by motion (paragraph 2(2));bprovide for service on a defender at his dwelling place or a place where he carries on business on persons at such a place or depositing there (paragraph 2(3));calter the procedure for dealing with expenses in decrees in absence (paragraph 2(6) and (31));dprovide for summary decree in commercial causes (paragraph 2(7));eprovide for a person to apply for the recall or variation of an order to give evidence following a letter of request under the Evidence (Proceedings in other Jurisdictions) Act 1975(c. 34) (paragraph 2(8));fprovide for the lodging of documents founded on in pleadings in actions (paragraph 2(4), (5) and (9)) and petitions (paragraph 2(15) and (16));gprovide for recall of arrestments in admiralty causes to be by motion, and for motions to seek authority to move arrested ships (paragraph 2(10));hprovide for the cessure of the exclusion of vacation days from the adjustment period in consistorial actions (paragraph 2(11));iprovide for the procedure to be applied in applications by a person who is not a parent, tutor, curator or guardian of a child for custody of that child in a consistorial action and consequential amendments (paragraph 2(12)(a) and (d), (24)(b) and (32)(f) and (g));jprovide for applications to the court in relation to children after decree in consistorial actions (paragraph 2(12)(b) and (c));kprovide for the referral of custody or access disputes to the Family Conciliation Service (paragraph 2(12)(d) and 24(c));lprovide for the procedure for applying for diligence on the dependence in applications by a defender for financial provision in a divorce action (paragraph 2(13)(a));mprovide for certain applications for incidental orders under the Family Law (Scotland) Act 1985(c. 37) (paragraph 2(13) (b));nprovide for applications under any enactment not otherwise provided for in the rules to be by petition to the Outer House (paragraph 2(14));oprovide for applications by judicial factors to encroach on capital of wards (paragraph 2(17));pprovide for causes under the Criminal Justice (Scotland) Act 1987 (paragraph 2(18) and (32)(h));qprovide for the induciae for service of, and the non-application of the requirement to advertise, a petition for the disqualification of a company director (paragraph 2(19));rprovide for applications under the Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency Act 1988 (paragraph 2(20));sprovide for reciprocal enforcement of orders in relation to confiscation of the proceeds of crime under the Criminal Justice (Scotland) Act 1987 and the Criminal Justice Act 1988 (paragraph 2(21) and (32)(e));tapplications under the Trade Marks Act 1938 and the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 (paragraph 2(22));ucorrect the cross-heading above rule 260P (registration and enforcement of custody orders under the Family Law Act 1986) (paragraph 2(25));vprovide for reclaiming, without leave, interlocutors which dispose of a cause other than the question of expenses (paragraph 2(26));wprovide for appeals under the Inheritance Tax Act 1984 and the Stamp Duty Reserve Tax Regulations 1986 and consequential amendments (paragraph 2(27) and (29));xprovide for appeals relating to penalties under section 100C(4) of the Taxes Management Act 1970(c. 9) (substituted for section 100(6) of that Act by section 167 of the Finance Act 1989(c. 26) (paragraph 2 (28));yprovide that a certificate of additional remuneration for a skilled witness may be obtained where the proof or trial does not proceed (paragraph 2(30));zcorrect the numbering of forms relating to applications for judicial review and petitions for custody of children (paragraph 2(23), (24)(a) and (b) and (32)(a) to (d));2revokes the Act of Sederunt (Computer Evidence in the Court of Session) 1969.

items.title: The Rate Support Grant (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Rate Support Grant (Scotland) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Offshore Installations (Life-saving Appliances and Fire-fighting Equipment) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: The Offshore Installations (Life-saving Appliances) Regulations 1977 prohibit the presence of any person on an installation to which the Regulations apply unless the life-saving appliances on that installation have been examined by a person acting at the direction of the Secretary of State. The Offshore Installations (Fire-fighting Equipment) Regulations 1978 contain a similar prohibition in respect of the fire-fighting equipment provided on an offshore installation. Both the 1977 and the 1978 Regulations require the owner of an installation on which an examination is carried out to pay a fee to the Secretary of State, comprising a sum based on the time spent by the examiner in carrying out the examination and in travelling to and from the installation, calculated in accordance with a table set out in the Schedule to the Regulations, and the cost of his travelling and subsistence expenses. These Regulations substitute in the 1977 Regulations and the 1978 Regulations a new table specifying the rates payable in respect of the time spent by an examiner. These new rates replace those having effect by virtue of the Offshore Installations (Life-saving Appliances and Fire-fighting Equipment) (Amendment) Regulations 1989 which were as follows —Time spentRate per hour or part thereofOn any day except a Saturday, Sunday or public holiday —between 8 a.m. and 6 p.m.£42.00before 8 a.m. or after 6 p.m.£63.00On a Saturday£63.00On a Sunday or public holiday£84.00

items.title: The Transport Levying Bodies Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations confer a general power on certain transport bodies (regulation 3) to issue levies to local authorities for the purpose of meeting their expenses in respect of financial years beginning in or after 1990 where, but for section 117 of the Local Government Finance Act 1988, they would have a statutory power to require the authorities to pay those expenses.

items.title: The Community Charges (Administration and Enforcement) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the provisions of the Community Charges (Administration and Enforcement) Regulations 1989 (“the principal Regulations”) and the Community Charges (Co-owners) Regulations 1990 concerned with billing and collection in respect of community charges. They make amendments to the provisions of the principal regulations dealing with the issue of community charge bills net of community charge benefit (so that amongst other matters such bills are calculated by reference to the current determination of benefit for the case in question); provide that sums paid by a spouse or manager in respect of a chargeable person’s liability may be recovered (where appropriate) from the chargeable person’s personal representative if he dies; and clarify the provisions of the principal Regulations and the Community Charges (Co-owners) Regulations 1990 dealing with the awarding of costs where liability orders are made against persons who are jointly and severally liable.

items.title: The Personal Community Charge (Qualifying Courses of Education) Regulations 1990
items.description: Under paragraph 6A of Schedule 1 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988 a person is exempt from the personal community charge if he is aged under 20, he is undertaking a qualifying course of education, and the course is not undertaken in consequence of an office or employment held by him.

items.title: The Companies Act 1989 (Commencement No. 5 and Transitional and Saving Provisions) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Companies Act 1989 (Commencement No. 5 and Transitional and Saving Provisions) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Isles of Scilly (Road Traffic Regulation) Order 1990
items.description: This Order replaces the Isles of Scilly (Road Traffic Regulation) Order 1971.

items.title: The Highland Regional Council (Loch Lannsaidh) Water Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Highland Regional Council (Loch Lannsaidh) Water Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees of Solicitors in the Sheriff Court) (Amendment) 1990
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Solicitors in the Sheriff Court) 1989 by increasing the fees payable to solicitors by about 8 per cent.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No.2) (Solicitors' Fees) 1990
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends Chapters I and III of the Table of Fees in rule 347 of the Rules of the Court of Session by increasing fees payable to solicitors by about 8 per cent.

items.title: Act of Adjournal (Consolidation Amendment No. 1) (Drug Trafficking) 1990
items.description: This Act of Adjournal amends the Act of Adjournal (Consolidation) 1988 in relation to proceedings in the High Court of Justiciary under Part I of the Criminal Justice (Scotland) Act 1987 (confiscation of proceeds of drug trafficking etc.).

items.title: The Public Airport Companies (Capital Finance) Order 1990
items.description: Part IV of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 establishes a framework of capital finance controls for local authorities in relation to the financial years starting on or after 1st April 1990. Section 39 enables these controls to be extended and adapted so as to take account of the capital finance transactions of bodies under local authority control. Among these bodies are public airport companies which, under the Airports Act 1986, were established under the control of those local authorities which own their issued share capital and subsidiaries of such companies.

items.title: The Internal Drainage Boards (Finance) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to England and Wales, modify the powers of internal drainage boards to make drainage rates and require those boards to raise part of their expenses by means of special levies issued to local authorities. The Regulations also make changes in relation to the membership of the boards.

items.title: The Passenger Transport Executives (Capital Finance) Order 1990
items.description: Part IV of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 establishes a framework of capital finance controls for local authorities in relation to the financial years starting on or after 1st April 1990. Section 39 enables these controls to be extended and adapted so as to take account of the capital finance transactions of bodies under local authority control. Among these bodies are the Passenger Transport Executives each of which is responsible under the Transport Act 1968 (c. 73) to the Passenger Transport Authority (“PTA”) for its area.

items.title: The Social Security (Claims and Payments) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which replace a similar provision in regulation 2 of the Income-Related Benefits (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 1990 (S.I. 1990/671), enable a person who becomes entitled to income support only as a result of the increase in the capital limit from £6,000 to £8,000, and whose claim for income support is received by 27th May 1990, to have that claim treated as made on the day he satisfied the entitlement conditions for income support.

items.title: The Social Security (Industrial Injuries and Diseases) Miscellaneous Provisions (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 12th February 1990, amend the Social Security (Industrial Injuries and Diseases) Miscellaneous Provisions Regulations 1986 (“the 1986 Regulations”).

items.title: The Football Spectators (Designation of Enforcing Authority) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates the organisation referred to in article 2 as the enforcing authority for the purposes of Part II of the Football Spectators Act 1989. That authority has the functions set out in sections 19 and 20 of that Act.

items.title: The Football Spectators (Designation of Football Matches in England and Wales) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates association football matches falling within the description set out in article 2(2) for the purposes of Part I of the Football Spectators Act 1989 (“the 1989 Act”). Under section 1(1) of that Act, Part I applies to designated football matches played in England and Wales.

items.title: The Football Spectators (Designation of Football Matches outside England and Wales) Order 1990 (revoked)
items.description: This Order designates association football matches falling within the description set out in article 2(3) as football matches to which Part II of the Football Spectators Act 1989 applies. Under section 14(1) of that Act, Part II applies to designated football matches played in a country outside England and Wales.

items.title: The Sussex Police (Amalgamation) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order contains the Secretary of State’s approval to a Scheme, submitted to him under the Police Act 1964, amending the Sussex Police Amalgamation Scheme 1973 with effect from 1st April 1990.

items.title: The Export of Goods (Control) (Amendment No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order further amends the Export of Goods (Control) Order 1989.

items.title: The Devon and Cornwall Police (Amalgamation) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order contains the Secretary of State’s approval to a Scheme, submitted to him under the Police Act 1964, amending the Devon and Cornwall Police Amalgamation Scheme 1974 with effect from 1st April 1990.

items.title: The Dyfed-Powys Police (Amalgamation) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order contains the Secretary of State’s approval to a Scheme, submitted to him under the Police Act 1964, further amending the provisions of the Dyfed-Powys Police Amalgamation Scheme 1973 relating to the apportionment of the expenses of the combined police authority between the constituent councils, with effect from 1st April 1990.

items.title: The South Wales Police (Amalgamation) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order contains the Secretary of State’s approval to a Scheme, submitted to him under the Police Act 1964, amending the South Wales Police Amalgamation Scheme 1973 with effect from 1st April 1990.

items.title: The Thames Valley Police (Amalgamation) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order contains the Secretary of State’s approval to a Scheme, submitted to him under the Police Act 1964, further amending the provisions of the Thames Valley Police Amalgamation Scheme 1968 relating to the apportionment of the expenses of the combined police authority between the constituent councils, with effect from 1st April 1990.

items.title: The Education (The London Institute) (Designated Staff) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates, for the purposes of section 127 of the Education Reform Act 1988, five persons employed by the Inner London Education Authority (“ILEA”) to work partly at Furzedown Hostel and partly at other property of that authority.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Videotron London Limited) (Greenwich and Lewisham) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Videotron London Limited on 15th March 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the whole of the London Boroughs of Greenwich and Lewisham. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 29th March 1990. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Videotron London Limited will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its Greenwich and Lewisham public telecommunication systems when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Charities) Order 1990
items.description: This Order varies, witheffect from 1st May 1990, items 1, 2 and 8 of and Notes (1) and (4) to Group 16 of Schedule 5 to the Value Added Tax Act 1983 (c. 55).

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Tour Operators) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends, with effect from 1st May 1990, articles 5 and 10 of the Value Added Tax (Tour Operators) Order 1987.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Transport) Order 1990
items.description: This Order varies, with effect from 1st May 1990, items 3, 4, 5, 6, 9 and 11 of and Notes (1) and (3) to Group 10 of Schedule 5 to the Value Added Tax Act 1983 (c. 55). It also adds a new Note (2A) to that Group.

items.title: The A614 Trunk Road (Bawtry to Austerfield Roundabout) (De-Trunking) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A614 Trunk Road (Bawtry to Austerfield Roundabout) (De-Trunking) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Superannuation (National Museums and Galleries on Merseyside) Order 1990
items.description: The Board of Trustees of the National Museums and Galleries on Merseyside (the Board) was incorporated by The Merseyside Museums and Galleries Order 1986 (S.I. 1986/226) under section 46 of the Local Government Act 1985 (c. 51). This order adds employment by the Board to the employments and offices listed in Schedule 1 to the Superannuation Act 1972 so that the Principal Civil Service Pension Scheme and the Civil Service Additional Voluntary Contribution Scheme made under section 1 of that Act may apply to persons employed by the Board.

items.title: The Northern Ireland (Emergency Provisions) Act 1978 (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order adds the Irish People’s Liberation Organisation to the proscribed organisations contained in Schedule 2 to the Northern Ireland (Emergency Provisions) Act 1978.

items.title: The Electricity (Restrictive Trade Practices Act 1976) (Exemptions) Order 1990
items.description: This Order specifies descriptions of agreements relating to the generation, transmission or supply of electricity to which the Restrictive Trade Practices Act 1976 (“the 1976 Act”) is deemed not to appy and never to have applied. By virtue of section 100(1) of the Electricity Act 1989 electricity is treated as goods for the purposes of the 1976 Act. This Order provides that agreements meeting any of the descriptions set out in the Schedule to the Order, and satisfying the relevant condition specified in that Schedule, will nevertheless fall outside the provisions of the 1976 Act.

items.title: The Movement of Animals (Restrictions) Order 1990
items.description: This Order enables a veterinary inspector, if he has reasonable grounds to suppose that any disease of animals (other than a disease which is subject to similar controls under another Order) exists on any premises, to prohibit, by a notice in Form A, the movement of animals, carcases or other things on to or off the premises, except under licence and to impose such other restrictions or requirements as he may consider necessary for the purpose of preventing the spread of disease. A notice in Form A will remain in force until it is withdrawn by a further notice in Form B served by a veterinary inspector. A notice in Form A shall be withdrawn by a notice in Form B if a veterinary inspector is satisfied that the suspected disease does not exist on the premises or the keeping in force of the notice in Form A is no longer necessary for the purposes of the Order (article 4 and Schedule).

items.title: The Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (Commencement No. 6 and Miscellaneous Provisions) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st April 1990 the following provisions of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 insofar as not already in force— section 33 (promotion of economic development); section 34 (restrictions on promotion of economic development); section 35 (guidance and consultation about promotion of economic development); and section 194(1) insofar as it gives effect to an amendment to section 130 of the Town and Country Planning Act 1971 (c. 78).

items.title: The Local Government (Promotion of Economic Development) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations impose restrictions on the exercise by relevant authorities in England and Wales (London borough councils, county councils, district councils, the Common Council of the City of London and the Council of the Isles of Scilly) of powers to promote the economic development of their areas.

items.title: The Juvenile Courts (London) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Juvenile Courts (London) Order 1975 as amended by the Juvenile Courts (London) (Amendment) Order 1984, which assigns divisions to juvenile courts for the Inner London boroughs and the City of London and specifies the places where such courts may sit. The effect of the Order is to provide a new address for juvenile court sittings which formerly took place at The Court House, 58B Bow Road, E3 4DJ and which served the Thames division and the City of London. The new courthouse is situated at 66 Bow Road, E3 4DH.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Borrowing) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations are concerned with the new borrowing powers of local authorities conferred by Part IV of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989. The powers will be exercisable from 1st April 1990 as part of the new capital finance system for local authorities.

items.title: Register of County Court Judgments (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations correct an error in one of the amendments made to the Register of County Court Judgments Regulations 1985 by the Register of County Court Judgments (Amendment) Regulations 1990, S.I. 1990/491

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Alteration of Lists and Appeals) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: Regulation 2 of these Regulations amends the Non-Domestic Rating (Alteration of Lists and Appeals) Regulations 1990 (“the principal Regulations”). The principal change is that an omission from regulation 4 (effective date of alteration) is rectified in relation to the time from which alterations to local non-domestic rating lists are to have effect. Minor adjustments are also made to regulation 11 (proposals treated as invalid), 33 (written representations) and 44 (review of decisions).

items.title: The A12 Trunk Road (Eastern Avenue East, Havering) (Prohibition of Use of Gap in Central Reservation) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A12 Trunk Road (Eastern Avenue East, Havering) (Prohibition of Use of Gap in Central Reservation) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Combined Probation Areas (Gwent) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Combined Probation Areas Order 1986 to take account of abolition of the petty sessional divisions of Abergavenny, Chepstow, Cwmbran, Monmouth, Pontypool and Usk and their reconstitution as the new division of East Gwent, effected by the Petty Sessional Divisions (Gwent) Order 1990 (S.I. 1990/26) which comes into force on 1st April 1990.

items.title: The Education (Inner London Education Authority) (Property Transfer) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order makes further provision for the transfer of property, rights and liabilities of the Inner London Education Authority (“ILEA”) on its abolition by the Education Reform Act 1988 (“the Act”) on 1st April 1990 (“the abolition date”). It is supplementary to the provisions of the Education (Inner London Education Authority) (Property Transfer) Order 1990 (“the principal order”).

items.title: The Education (Inner London Education Authority) (Transitional and Supplementary Provisions) Order 1990
items.description: This Order applies the provisions of the Education Act 1962 which are concerned with mandatory and discretionary awards to students to the London Residuary Body (“the LRB”). From 1st April 1990, the date on which the Inner London Education Authority (“ILEA”) is abolished, the LRB is constituted the local education authority for the former ILEA area in respect of awards already made by ILEA and awards relating to courses beginning between 1st September 1989 and 31st March 1990 but not yet made.

items.title: The Education (Inner London Education Authority) (Transitional and Supplementary Provisions) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This order amends the Social Security (Unemployment, Sickness and Invalidity Benefit) Regulations 1983 and the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 by adding to the list of exclusions from the definition of compensation in those regulations compensation payable by virtue of section 173 or section 178(3) or (4) of the Education Reform Act 1988.

items.title: The Education (Inner London Education Authority) (Repayment of Loans) Order 1990
items.description: Section 177 of the Education Reform Act 1988 provides that, on the abolition of the Inner London Education Authority (ILEA) on 1st April 1990, the rights and liabilities of the ILEA in respect of money borrowed by the ILEA, including money borrowed by any other authority whose rights and liabilities in respect of that money have been transferred to the ILEA, shall vest in the London Residuary Body (LRB) and that those liabilities shall be charged on the revenues of the LRB. Section 177 goes on to provide that each inner London council shall, on the abolition date, be deemed to have borrowed from the LRB a sum specified or determined in accordance with an order made by the Secretary of State so as to enable the LRB to discharge those liabilities.

items.title: The Local Government Finance (Repeals, Savings and Consequential Amendments) Order 1990
items.description: This Order makes provision consequent upon the Local Government Finance Act 1988 (which establishes new systems of community charges and non-domestic rates to replace the present rating system) and upon Part IV of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (which establishes a new system for the control of the capital expenditure of local authorities).

items.title: The General Rate Act 1967 and Related Provisions (Savings and Consequential Provision) Regulations 1990
items.description: As a consequence of the introduction on 1st April 1990 of the new community charges and non-domestic rating systems, section 117(1) of the Local Government Finance Act 1988 provides that the General Rate Act 1967 ceases to have effect after 31st March 1990. These Regulations continue the provisions of that Act, other than those specified in Schedule 1 to the Regulations, for the limited purposes mentioned in regulation 3.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Capital Finance) (Consequential Amendments) Order 1990
items.description: This Order provides for the amendment of various enactments and instruments consequential upon Part IV of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (revenue accounts and capital finance of local authorities). References to Part VIII of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980 (c. 65) (capital expenditure of local authorities), which ceases to have effect for financial years beginning on or after 1st April 1990 by virtue of section 39(8) of the 1989 Act, are replaced by references to Part IV of that Act.

items.title: The Housing Corporation Advances (Increase of Limit) Order 1990
items.description: The Housing Corporation’s powers of borrowing are subject to a limit under section 93 of the Housing Associations Act 1985. This Order increases that limit from £2,000 million to £2,300 million.

items.title: The Dairy Produce Quotas (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Dairy Produce Quotas Regulations 1989 (“the principal Regulations”). The Regulations clarify Schedule 8B to the principal Regulations (small producer supplementary development provision) — regulation 5, and Schedule 9 to the principal Regulations (exceptional hardship provision) — regulation 6. In particular they make clear that Schedule 8B applies only to producers in respect of holdings in Scotland and Northern Ireland and that Schedule 9 applies only to producers in respect of holdings in Northern Ireland. Errors in the definitions of “appropriate date” and “wholesale development quota” are corrected — regulation 4.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (Subsidy) Order 1990
items.description: This Order makes provision for the calculation of housing benefit subsidy payable under sections 30(2), (2A) and (3) of the Social Security Act 1986 to authorities who grant rent rebates or allowances, rate rebates or, in Scotland, community charge rebates under that Act in the year ending 31st March 1990.

items.title: The Regional Flood Defence Committees (Welsh Area) (Appointed Day) Order 1990
items.description: Section 137 of the Water Act 1989 contains provisions for the establishment, as from 1st September 1989 (“the transfer date”), of regional flood defence committees in place of regional land drainage committees. Subsection (4) of section 137 of that Act provides for the membership of a regional flood defence committee during a period beginning with the transfer date and ending with a day appointed by, in the case of a regional flood defence committee for an area the greater part of which is in Wales, the Secretary of State.

items.title: The National Health Service (Charges for Drugs and Appliances) (Scotland) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Charges for Drugs and Appliances) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990 (“the amending Regulations”) which amend Regulations providing for the making and recovery of charges for drugs and appliances under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rates (Levying) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision as to the rateable value in accordance with which the non-domestic rate will be levied for the financial year 1990-91.

items.title: The Local Government (Promotion of Economic Development) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 4 of the Local Government (Promotion of Economic Development) Regulations 1990 (S.I. 1990/763). The effect of that regulation as amended is that an authority will be regarded as providing financial assistance under section 33(3)(f) of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 if it provides property, services or other financial benefit for a consideration less than the best that can reasonably be obtained.

items.title: The Income Tax (Employments) (No. 19) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Tax (Employments) Regulations 1973 (“the PAYE Regulations”) in various respects. Apart from regulations 3, 4 and 7, which come into force on 13th February 1990, they come into force on 6th April 1990.

items.title: The Meters (Approval of Pattern or Construction and Method of Installation) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations set out the procedure for obtaining approval of the pattern or construction and manner of installation of meters to be used for ascertaining the quantity of electricity supplied by an electricity supplier to a customer.

items.title: The Meters (Certification) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations set out the procedure for enabling meters used for the purpose of measuring the quantity of electricity supplied to customers to be certified. They continue, with modifications, the provisions relating to certification contained in the Electric Lighting (Clauses) Act 1899, the Electricity Supply (Meters) Act 1936 (c. 20) and the Electricity Act 1957 (c. 48) (which provisions are repealed on 31st March 1990, the date when these Regulations come into force).

items.title: The Sunderland (Castletown and Doxford Park) Enterprise Zones (Designation) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Sunderland (Castletown and Doxford Park) Enterprise Zones (Designation) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Sunderland (Hylton Riverside and Southwick) Enterprise Zone (Designation) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates as an enterprise zone the area of the Sunderland No. 1—Hylton Riverside and Southwick Scheme which was adopted by the Tyne and Wear Development Corporation on 13th November 1989.

items.title: The Education (Designated Institutions) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends as from 4th April 1990 the Education (Designated Institutions) Order 1989 by removing the College of St Paul and St Mary (Cheltenham) from the list of institutions in that Order designated as eligible to receive funds from the Polytechnics and Colleges Funding Council and adding the Cheltenham and Gloucester College of Higher Education incorporating the College of St Paul and St Mary.

items.title: The Housing and Planning Act 1986 (Commencement No. 16) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 31st March 1990 section 53(1) of the Housing and Planning Act 1986 so far as it relates to paragraphs 39 and 40 of Schedule 11 to that Act. Paragraphs 39 and 40 themselves were the subject of the Housing and Planning Act 1986 (Commencement No.14) Order 1990 (S.I. 1990/511). These provisions relate to planning and local government matters in Scotland although the Order extends throughout the United Kingdom.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No.2) (Shorthand Writers' Fees) 1990
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the fees for shorthand writers in the Court of Session by increasing certain fees by about 6 per cent and increasing attendance fees to reflect a payment equal to 3 hours (half a day).

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Shorthand Writers' Fees) 1990
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the fees for shorthand writers in the Sheriff Court by increasing certain fees by about 6 per cent and increases attendance fees to reflect a payment equal to 3 hours (half a day).

items.title: The Self-Governing Schools (Ballots and Publication of Proposals) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision in respect of the holding of a ballot of parents of pupils in attendance at a school on the question of whether self-governing status should be sought for that school, under the Self-Governing Schools etc. (Scotland) Act 1989 (“the Act”) and, for the circumstances where the result of such a ballot has been in favour of that status being applied for, prescribe the manner in which proposals to that effect should be published.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) Regulations 1974 (“the 1974 Regulations”) which regulate the terms on which doctors and chemists provide general medical services and pharmaceutical services under the National Health Service Act 1977.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Driver Licensing and Information Systems) Act 1989 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st June 1990 the provisions of the Road Traffic (Driver Licensing and Information Systems) Act 1989 relating to driving licences and driver information systems specified in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Compensation for Restrictions on Mineral Working) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Town and Country Planning (Compensation for Restrictions on Mineral Working) (Amendment) Regulations 1990 Approved by both Houses of Parliament is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Gas and Electricity Industries (Rateable Values) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This order amends three orders made under the Local Government Finance Act 1988 which provide for rateable values effective on and after 1st April 1990 to be entered in local rating lists in respect of electricity generating plant, and in central rating lists in relation to certain hereditaments occupied by British Gas plc, and by companies engaged in the generation and distribution of electricity.

items.title: The Police Pensions (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st May 1990, amend the Police Pensions Regulations 1987 (“the principal Regulations”) and the Police Pensions (Purchase of Increased Benefits) Regulations 1987 with effect from 6th April 1988 (retrospection is authorised by section 1(5) of the Police Pensions Act 1976).

items.title: The Combined Probation Areas (Avon) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Combined Probation Areas Order 1986 to take account of the abolition of the petty sessional divisions of Avon North, Bath, Thornbury and Wansdyke and their reconstitution into the new divisions of Bath and Wansdyke and North Avon, effected by the Petty Sessional Divisions (Avon) Order 1990 (S.I. 1990/291) which comes into force on 1st April 1990.

items.title: The Milk and Milk Products (Protection of Designations) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Scotland only, and come into force on 2nd May 1990, provide for the enforcement of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 1898/87 on the protection of designations used in the marketing of milk and milk products.

items.title: The Docks and Harbours (Rateable Values) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order makes provision for the valuation for the financial year 1990-91 and any subsequent financial year of certain lands and heritages (“the prescribed class of lands and heritages”) occupied by a person (“an authority”) carrying on a dock or harbour undertaking under statutory powers, other than the British Waterways Board, The Forth Ports Authority and Caledonian MacBrayne Limited.

items.title: The Forth Ports Authority (Rateable Values) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order makes provision for the valuation for the financial year 1990-91 of certain lands and heritages occupied by Forth Ports Authority (article 3) (“the prescribed class of lands and heritages”).

items.title: The Caledonian MacBrayne Limited (Rateable Values) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order makes provision for the valuation for the financial year 1990–91 of certain lands and heritages occupied by Caledonian MacBrayne Limited (article 3) (“the prescribed class of lands and heritages”).

items.title: The Industrial and Freight Transport (Rateable Values) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order makes provision for the valuation of industrial and freight transport lands and heritages (“the prescribed class”) for the financial year 1990-91.

items.title: The British Telecommunications plc. (Rateable Values) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order makes provision for the valuation for the financial year 1990-91 of certain lands and heritages occupied by British Telecommunications plc. (article 3) (“the prescribed class of lands and heritages”).

items.title: The British Alcan Primary and Recycling Ltd. (Rateable Values) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order makes provision for the valuation for the financial year 1990-91 of certain lands and heritages occupied by British Alcan Primary and Recycling Ltd. (article 3) (“the prescribed class of lands and heritages”).

items.title: The Mines and Quarries (Rateable Values) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order makes provision for the valuation of certain mines and quarries (“the prescribed class”) for the financial year 1990-91.

items.title: The Gas Safety (Installation and Use) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Gas Safety (Installationand Use) Regulations 1984 by substituting for regulation 3 thereof the regulation set out in regulation 2 of these Regulations. The regulation as substituted makes the following changes of substance. It prohibits an employer from allowing his employees to carry out work in relation to gas fittings unless he is a member of a class of personsapproved by the Health and Safety Executive. It also prohibits a self-employed person from carrying out such work unless he is a member of such a class. The approval (and any withdrawal of it) is required to be in writing,and notice of it must be given to such persons in such manner as the Executive considers appropriate.

items.title: The Pneumoconiosis etc. (Workers' Compensation) (Payment of Claims) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: The Pneumoconiosis etc. (Workers' Compensation) (Payment of Claims) Regulations 1988 (the 1988 Regulations), as amended by the Pneumoconiosis etc. (Workers' Compensation) (Payment of Claims) (Amendment) Regulations 1989, prescribe the amount of payments to be made under the Pneumoconiosis etc. (Workers' Compensation) Act 1979 (“the Act”), as amended by the Social Security Act 1985 and the Social Security Act 1986, to persons disabled by a disease to which the Act applies, or to dependants of persons who immediately before they died were disabled by such a disease. The diseases to which the Act applies are pneumoconiosis, byssinosis, diffuse mesothelioma, primary carcinoma of the lung (where accompanied by asbestosis or bilateral diffuse pleural thickening) and bilateral diffuse pleural thickening.

items.title: The Redundancy Payments (Local Government) (Modification) (Amendment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st April 1990, amends the Redundancy Payments (Local Government) (Modification) Order 1983 as amended by the Redundancy Payments (Local Government) (Modification) (Amendment) Order 1985, the Redundancy Payments (Local Government) (Modification) (Amendment) Order 1988 and the Redundancy Payments (Local Government) (Modification) (Amendment) Order 1989. Those Orders modified certain redundancy payments provisions of the Employment Protection (Consolidation) Act 1978 in their application to persons employed in relevant local government service so that their employment in the service is to be treated as if it were continuous for the purposes of those provisions. This Order adds to the list of employers to whose employees the 1983 Order as amended applies.

items.title: The Combined Probation Areas (Gloucestershire) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Combined Probation Areas Order 1986 so as to increase the number of justices appointed to the Gloucestershire Probation Committee. The petty sessional division of Gloucester will appoint 6 justices rather than 4, and Cheltenham will appoint 5 rather than 4.

items.title: The Housing (Management of Houses in Multiple Occupation) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations make provision for ensuring that the person managing a house in multiple occupation observes proper standards of management.

items.title: Authorities for London Post-Graduate Teaching Hospitals (Establishment and Constitution) Amendment Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Authorities for London Post-Graduate Teaching Hospitals (Establishment and Constitution) Order 1982 by changing the name of the former National Hospitals for Nervous Diseases to the National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery.

items.title: The Community Charge Benefits (General) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Community Charge Benefits (General) Regulations 1989 in the following respects.

items.title: The Community Charge Benefits (General) Amendment No. 2 Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Community Charge Benefits (General) Regulations 1989. They raise, from £8,000 to £16,000, the capital limit over which a person is not to be entitled to community charge benefit, the limit for treating outstanding capital as income and the limit for calculating weekly tariff income (regulation 2). They also make provision, where a person becomes entitled to benefit because of the increase in the capital limit, for backdating a claim made not later than 27th May 1990 to the date on which the person satisfied the entitlement conditions for benefit and for entitlement to begin from the benefit week in which that date falls (regulations 3 and 4).

items.title: The Lochaber Power Company (Rateable Values) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order makes provision for the valuation for the financial year 1990-91 of certain lands and heritages occupied by Lochaber Power Company (article 3) (“the prescribed class of lands and heritages”).

items.title: The British Gas plc. (Rateable Values) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order makes provision for the valuation for the financial year 1990-91 of certain lands and heritages occupied by British Gas plc. (article 3) (“the prescribed class of lands and heritages”).

items.title: The Glasgow Underground (Rateable Values) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order makes provision for the valuation for the financial year 1990-91 of certain lands and heritages occupied by Strathclyde Passenger Transport Executive (article 3) (“the prescribed class of lands and heritages”).

items.title: The Civil Legal Aid (Financial Conditions) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations increase certain of the financial limits of eligibility for civil legal aid under the Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 1986.

items.title: The Advice and Assistance (Financial Conditions) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations increase the disposable income limit for eligibility for advice and assistance under the Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 1986 from £128 a week to £135 a week, the disposable capital limit from £890 to £935 and the weekly disposable income above which a person is required to pay a contribution from £61 to £64. They also prescribe the scale of contributions to be paid where the weekly disposable income exceeds £64 but does not exceed £135.

items.title: The Combined Probation Areas (North Yorkshire) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Combined Probation Areas Order 1986 to take account ofthe changes made by the Petty Sessional Divisions (North Yorkshire) Order 1989(S.I. 1989/2429) which comes into force on 1st April 1990.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) Regulations 1987 so as to give effect, with permitted derogations and in respect of motor vehicles other than heavy goods and public service vehicles, to the system of classification for motor vehicles set out in Article 3 of Council Directive (EEC) No. 80/1263 of 4th December 1980 on the introduction of a Community driving licence.

items.title: The Charities (Borough Lands Charity, Chippenham) Order 1990
items.description: This Order gives effect to a Scheme of the Charity Commissioners for the regulation of the Borough Lands Charity, Chippenham. The Charity is established for certain purposes in connection with the former Borough of Chippenham under Letters Patent of 1554 and subsequent dates.

items.title: The Combined Probation Areas (Hertfordshire) Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Combined Probation Areas Order 1986 to take account of the abolition of the petty sessional divisions of Hatfield and Welwyn and their reconstitution as the new division of Mid Hertfordshire effected by the Petty Sessional Divisions (Hertfordshire) Order 1990 (S.I. 1990/303) which comes into force on 1st April 1990.

items.title: The Registration of Births, Deaths, Marriages, Etc. (Prescription of Forms) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Registration of Births, Deaths, Marriages, Etc. (Prescription of Forms) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Local Government Officers (Political Restrictions) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations impose restrictions on the public political activities of local government officers who are appointed to or employed in posts which are politically restricted posts for the purposes of Part I of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989.

items.title: House of Commons Member’s Fund Resolution 1990
The British Railways Board (Rateable Values) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order makes provision for the valuation for the financial year 1990-91 of certain lands and heritages occupied by British Railways Board (article 3) (“the prescribed class of lands and heritages”).

items.title: The British Waterways Board (Rateable Values) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order makes provision for the valuation for the financial year 1990-91 and subsequent financial years of certain lands and heritages occupied by the British Waterways Board (article 3) (“the prescribed class of lands and heritages”).

items.title: The Water Undertakings (Rateable Values) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order makes provision for the valuation for the financial year 1990-91 of certain lands and heritages (“the prescribed class of lands and heritages”) occupied by water authorities specified in column 1 of the Schedule to this Order (“the authorities”) and used wholly or mainly for the purposes of water undertakings carried on by the authorities.

items.title: The Mercury Communications Ltd. (Rateable Values) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order makes provision for the valuation for the financial year 1990-91 of certain lands and heritages occupied by Mercury Communications Ltd. (article 3) (“the prescribed class of lands and heritages”).

items.title: The Self-Governing Schools etc. (Scotland) Act 1989 (Commencement) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st February 1990 the following provisions of the Self-Governing Schools etc. (Scotland) Act 1989:- section 67 - Abolition of committee to consider pay and conditions of teaching staff employed in providing further education; section 69(1) and (2) - Testing in primary schools; section 73 - Educational services: extension of power of Secretary of State to make grants; section 74 - Appointment of teachers; section 75 - Dismissal of teachers; section 76 - Remuneration of certain persons employed in providing school education; section 82(2) and Schedule 11 - Repeals; and Schedule 10, paragraphs 1, 2 and 8(7), (9), (10), (11), (13), (14), (15), (16), (17), (18), (19), (20) and (21) - Minor and consequential amendments.

items.title: The Business Expansion Scheme (Substitution of Permitted Maximum Amount) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, with effect from 1st May 1990, increases from £500,000 to £750,000 the amount which may be raised through the Business Expansion Scheme by the issue of eligible shares in the period of six months ending with the date of issue of the shares or since the previous 6th April, whichever is longer. The increase in the BES limit applies to share issues made by companies carrying on most qualifying trades, research and development or oil exploration. (Different limits apply to companies operating or chartering certain ships and to companies providing private rented housing, which are not affected by this Order.)

items.title: The Court of Protection (Enduring Powers of Attorney) (Amendment) Rules 1990
items.description: These Rules increase the fees payable in respect of applications for registration of an enduring power of attorney and applications to search the register.

items.title: The Driver Information Systems (Exemption) Order 1990
items.description: This Order specifies those descriptions of driver information systems to which Part II of the Road Traffic (Driver Licensing and Information Systems) Act 1989 does not apply.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Light Dues) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Merchant Shipping (Light Dues) Regulations 1990 (which came into force on 1st April 1990). They provide that ships of 25,000 tons or over which make payments of light dues per voyage are now only required to make payments in respect of one voyage per month (instead of two).

items.title: The A12 Trunk Road (Leyton Way, Waltham Forest) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A12 Trunk Road (Leyton Way, Waltham Forest) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Children’s Homes (Control and Discipline) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations prohibit the use of certain sanctions, including corporal punishment, in relation to children in community and voluntary homes and require those responsible for such a home to ensure that control of the home is maintained in accordance with good professional practice (regulation 2).

items.title: The Thames Water Utilities Limited (Pipelaying and Other Works) (Code of Practice) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Thames Water Utilities Limited (Pipelaying and Other Works) (Code of Practice) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Copyright (Certification of Licensing Scheme for Educational Recording of Broadcasts) (Guild Sound and Vision Limited) Order 1990
items.description: Under section 35 of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 recordings of broadcasts and cable programmes may be made by or on behalf of educational establishments without thereby infringing copyright. The section does not, however, apply if and to the extent that there is a licensing scheme certified for the purposes of that section providing for the grant of licences.

items.title: The Copyright (Certification of Licensing Scheme for Educational Recording of Broadcasts and Cable Programmes) (Educational Recording Agency Limited) Order 1990
items.description: Under section 35 of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 recordings of broadcasts and cable programmes may be made by or on behalf of educational establishments without thereby infringing copyright.The section does not, however, apply if and to the extent that there is a licensing scheme certified for the purposes of that section providing for the grant of licences.

items.title: The Sludge (Use in Agriculture) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Sludge (Use in Agriculture) Regulations 1989 (S.I. 1989/ 1263) (“the principal Regulations”), which were made in order to implement Council Directive No. 86/278/EEC (OJ No. L181/6), concerning the protection of the environment and, in particular, of the soil, when sewage sludge is used in agriculture.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Control of Advertisements) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: This version of the The Town and Country Planning (Control of Advertisements) (Amendment) Regulations 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Renfrew and City of Glasgow Districts (Atholl Crescent, Southwold Road, Rosshall, Bullwood and Hurlet) Boundaries Amendment (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order transfers on 15th April 1990 certain areas, extending in total to approximately 29 acres at Atholl Crescent, Southwold Road, Rosshall, Bullwood and Hurlet, between the City of Glasgow District and Renfrew District in Strathclyde Region, and makes consequential changes to electoral arrangements.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st May 1990, amend the National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) (Scotland) Regulations 1974 (“the principal Regulations”) which regulate the terms on which doctors and chemists provide general medical services and pharmaceutical services under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978.

items.title: The Loch Moidart, North Channel Scallops Fishery Order 1990
items.description: This Order confers on the Sea Fish Industry Authority (“the Authority”) for a period of 10 years from the date the Order comes into force, the right of several fishery for scallops in a specified area of the North Channel, Loch Moidart, Highland Region (Article 2).

items.title: The Skills Training Agency (Designation) (No. 1) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates certain activities carried on by the Skills Training Agency for the purposes of section 26 of the Employment Act 1989.

items.title: The Fertilisers Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which apply throughout Great Britain, consolidate and supersede the Fertilisers Regulations 1977 and the Fertilisers (Amendment) Regulations 1984. They implement the directives listed in paragraph 2 below, and incorporate changes in the law which are described in paragraph 5 below.

items.title: The Restrictive Trade Practices (Standards and Arrangements) Order 1990
items.description: This Order approves standards of dimension, design, quality or performance of goods and manufacturing processes and of performance in the provision of services, and arrangements as to the provision of information or advice, all of which have been adopted by Electricity Association Services Limited. The effect of the Order is that, for the purpose of determining whether an agreement is one to which the Restrictive Trade Practices Act 1976 applies, no account is taken of any term in the agreement by which the parties or any of them agree to comply with, or to apply, the standards and arrangements so approved.

items.title: The Wireless Telegraphy Apparatus (Approval) (Test Fees) Order 1990
items.description: This Order replaces the provisions of the Wireless Telegraphy Apparatus (Approval) (Test Fees) Order 1988 (S.I. No. 1949) which related to Private User Fixed Links, and substitutes a new article for article 5 of that Order.

items.title: The M42 Birmingham—Nottingham Motorway (Monkspath to Coleshill Section) and Connecting Roads Scheme (No. 2) 1978 (Revocation) Scheme 1990
items.description: This version of the The M42 Birmingham—Nottingham Motorway (Monkspath to Coleshill Section) and Connecting Roads Scheme (No. 2) 1978 (Revocation) Scheme 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Pilotage Act 1987 (Hartlepool Pilots' Benefit Fund) Order 1990
items.description: This Order provides that the Tees and Hartlepool Pilotage Company Ltd shall be managers of the Hartlepool Pilots' Benefit Fund with full powers to amend or revoke the byelaws and any other provisions governing the Fund.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Passenger Ship Construction and Survey) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Merchant Shipping (Passenger Ship Construction) Regulations 1980 and the Merchant Shipping (Passenger Ship Construction and Survey) Regulations 1984 and give effect, in relation to new United Kingdom passenger ships, to the amendments to the International Convention for the Safety of Life at Sea 1974 adopted by the International Maritime Organization at its 55th Session by Resolutions MSC. 12(56) and MSC. 13(57).

items.title: The Export of Goods (Control) (Amendment No. 3) Order 1990
items.description: This Order further amends the Export of Goods (Control) Order 1989 by lifting export control in relation to cocoa beans, cocoa paste, cocoa butter, cocoa fat, cocoa oil and cocoa powder.

items.title: The Offshore Installations (Safety Zones) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order establishes, under section 22 of the Petroleum Act 1987, a safety zone, having a radius of 500 metres from a specified point, around the installation specified in the Schedule to this Order and stationed in waters to which section 21(7) of that Act applies (these include territorial waters and waters in areas designated under section 1(7) of the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29)).

items.title: The Offshore Installations (Safety Zones) (No: 3) Order 1990
items.description: This Order establishes, under section 22 of the Petroleum Act 1987, a safety zone, having a radius of 500 metres from a specified point, around the installation specified in the Schedule to this Order and stationed in waters to which section 21(7) of that Act applies (these include territorial waters and waters in areas designated under section 1(7) of the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29)).

items.title: The A23 Trunk Road (Purley Way, Croydon (Prescribed Routes) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A23 Trunk Road (Purley Way, Croydon (Prescribed Routes) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Civil Aviation Authority (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Civil Aviation Authority Regulations 1983 to provide that where the Secretary of State has certified that the United Kingdom’s share of the capacity on air transport services between the United Kingdom and another state will within 6 months be insufficient to enable British operators to make available all the capacity they plan to provide, the Civil Aviation Authority may propose to vary the relevant air transport licences in order to allocate the available capacity without giving 21 days for the service of objections and representations. The timescales for appeal to the Secretary of State in such cases is reduced (regulation 2(1), (2) and (3)).

items.title: The National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) (Modification of Time Limit) (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations modify the time limit prescribed in regulation 8(2) of the National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) (Scotland) Regulations 1988 (“the principal Regulations”) within which a person may claim the repayment of certain charges he has paid for services under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978 or of travelling expenses he or, in certain cases, a travelling companion, has incurred in attending a hospital for treatment under the National Health Service.

items.title: The National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) (Modification of Time Limit) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations modify the time limit prescribed in regulation 8(2) of the National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) Regulations 1988 (“the principal Regulations”) within which a person may claim the repayment of certain charges he has paid for services under the National Health Service Act 1977 or of travelling expenses he has incurred in attending a hospital or disablement services centre for treatment under the National Health Service.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Bolton Telecable Ltd) Order 1990
items.description: (This is not part of the Order)

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Cable Haringey Ltd) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Cable Haringey Ltd on 2nd April 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the London Borough of Haringey. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 26th April 1990. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Cable Haringey Ltd will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Peterborough Cablevision Ltd) Order 1990
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Peterborough Cablevision Ltd on 4th April 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in part of the Borough of Peterborough centred on the City of Peterborough and two small adjacent parts of the District of Huntingdonshire. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 26th April 1990. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Peterborough Cablevision Ltd will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Football Spectators Act 1989 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order corrects an omission in the Football Spectators Act 1989 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1990 (S.I. 1990/690) and brings Schedule 1 (relevant offences) into force on 24th April 1990.

items.title: The Guarantee Payments (Exemption) (No. 27) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 21st May 1990, excludes from the operation of section 12 of the Employment Protection (Consolidation) Act 1978 employees to whom the lay-off and short-time agreement made between the parties described in Schedule 1 to the Order relates.

items.title: The Industrial Training (Transfer of the Activities of Establishment) Order 1990
items.description: This Order comes into force on 21st May 1990. It transfers the activities of the establishment specified in column 1 of the Schedule to the Order from the industry of the industrial training board established by the industrial training order specified in column 2 of the Schedule to the industry of the industrial training board established by the industrial training order specified in column 3 of the Schedule.

items.title: The Skills Training Agency (Designation) (No. 2) Order 1990
items.description: This Order designates certain activities carried on by the Skills Training Agency for the purposes of section 26 of the Employment Act 1989. These activities are in addition to those designated by the Skills Training Agency (Designation) (No. 1) Order 1990 (S.I. 1990/886).

items.title: The Local Elections (Principal Areas) (Declaration of Acceptance of Office) Order 1990
items.description: This Order prescribes the form of declaration of acceptance of office to be made by the chairman, vice-chairman or a councillor of the council of a county, district or London borough under section 83(1) of the Local Government Act 1972. The declarant is required to undertake to be guided by the National Code of Local Government Conduct issued under section 31(1) of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989.

items.title: The Parking Act 1989 (Commencement) Order 1990
items.description: This Order brings into force on 16th May 1990 the Parking Act 1989. This Act makes further amendments to the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 (c. 27) in relation to parking. Amendments relating to parking were previously made by the Road Traffic Regulation (Parking) Act 1986 (c. 27)

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Type Approval) (Great Britain) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Type Approval) (Great Britain) Regulations 1984 by correcting a drafting error in previous amending Regulations.

items.title: The Community Charges (Notices) (Substitute Charges) (England) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations relate to certain adjustment notices served by an English charging authority after making a substitute calculation under section 95 of the Local Government Finance Act 1988, or being issued with a substitute precept, pursuant to the imposition of a maximum amount under Part VII of the Act (limitation of charges etc).

items.title: The Milk Quota (Calculation of Standard Quota) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1990
items.description: This Order amends the Milk Quota (Calculation of Standard Quota) (Scotland) Order 1986, which prescribes the “standard yield per hectare” and the “average yield per hectare” to be taken into account in determining the “standard yield” for the purposes of calculating the payment which a tenant in Scotland is entitled, by Schedule 2 to the Agriculture Act 1986, to obtain from his landlord in respect of milk quota on the termination of a lease of a tenancy.

items.title: The Crofters etc. Building Grants (Scotland) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Crofters etc. Building Grants (Scotland) Regulations 1982. They relate to provision by the Secretary of State of assistance by way of grant towa rds the erection, improvement or rebuilding of dwellinghouses for crofters and certain other occupiers of land in the crofting counties.

items.title: The Control of Pollution (Clyde River Purification Board Act 1972) Amendment Order 1990
items.description: This Order makes provision to bring within Part II of the Control of Pollution Act 1974 the consents granted by the Clyde River Purification Board under section 3 of the Clyde River Purification Board Act 1972 (“the 1972 Act”) for discharge of trade or sewage effluent or any other poisonous, noxious or polluting matter to underground strata.

items.title: The Injuries in War (Shore Employments) Compensation (Amendment) Scheme 1990
items.description: The Injuries in War (Shore Employments) Compensation Schemes 1914 to 1989 provide for the payment of weekly allowance to small numbers of ex-members of the Women’s Auxiliary Forces who suffered disablement from their services overseas during the1914—18 war. This amending Scheme provides that the maximum weekly allowance payable shall be increased from £71.20 to £76.60 and that other allowances shall be increased proportionately. The increase will take effect on and after9th April 1990.

items.title: The A3 Trunk Road (Robin Hood Way, Kingston upon Thames) (Prohibition of Use of Gap in Central Reservation) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The A3 Trunk Road (Robin Hood Way, Kingston upon Thames) (Prohibition of Use of Gap in Central Reservation) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Removal, Storage and Disposal of Vehicles (Prescribed Sums and Charges etc.) (Amendment) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations amend the Removal, Storage and Disposal of Vehicles (Prescribed Sums and Charges etc.) Regulations 1989 so as to increase, with effect from 1st June 1990, the sum or charge payable under the 1989 Regulations in respect of the removal by the police or a local authority of vehicles from £75 to £80.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Councillors) (Declaration of Acceptance of Office) (Scotland) Order 1990
items.description: This Order prescribes the form of declaration of acceptance of office to be made by a person elected to office as a councillor of a local authority before he can act in that office. The form of declaration requires that person to undertake to be guided by the National Code of Local Government Conduct issued under section 31 of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989.

items.title: The County of Clwyd (Electoral Arrangements) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The County of Clwyd (Electoral Arrangements) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Seed Potatoes (Fees) Regulations 1990
items.description: These Regulations, which revoke and replace the Seed Potatoes (Fees) Regulations 1989, prescribe the fees payable in respect of certain matters arising under the Seed Potatoes Regulations 1984, as amended.

items.title: The Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (Commencement No. 7) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (Commencement No. 7) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The London-Portsmouth Trunk Road A3 (Ham Barn-Petersfield Section Slip Roads) (Variation) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The London-Portsmouth Trunk Road A3 (Ham Barn-Petersfield Section Slip Roads) (Variation) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The London–Portsmouth Trunk Road A3 (Liphook–Ham Barn Section Slip Roads) (Part 2) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The London–Portsmouth Trunk Road A3 (Liphook–Ham Barn Section Slip Roads) (Part 2) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The London-Portsmouth Trunk Road A3 (Liphook-Ham Barn Section) (Parts 2: Headley Road to Queens Road) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The London-Portsmouth Trunk Road A3 (Liphook-Ham Barn Section) (Parts 2: Headley Road to Queens Road) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The London-Portsmouth Trunk Road A3 (Liphook-Petersfield Section Detrunking) Order 1990
items.description: This version of the The London-Portsmouth Trunk Road A3 (Liphook-Petersfield Section Detrunking) Order 1990 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.
